project.goal: Deliver bindable commercial property quotes within 5 minutes of broker submission
project.target_users: Underwriters and broker account managers
project.timeline: Q2 MVP, Q4 full feature set

backend.framework: FastAPI with SQLAlchemy

security.auth: Cognito with MFA

data.cache: Redis 7 for quote caching and session management

open_questions.reinsurance_api: Confirm treaty check API contract with reinsurance team

reasoner_reviews.product: Submission-to-quote in five minutes is the headline business metric. The enrichment and scoring pipeline is the critical path. Risk scoring must handle incomplete enrichment gracefully with flagged manual underwriter review.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI async with concurrent enrichment API calls is the right approach for the 30s SLA. Quote versioning requires careful optimistic locking on the underwriting_submissions table to prevent concurrent edit collisions. Redis quote cache TTL must be bounded to prevent stale bindable quotes.
reasoner_reviews.security: SOC 2 Type II scope requires access logging for all submission views, not just mutations. Cognito MFA must be enforced for all underwriter and admin roles. Broker accounts need scoped visibility limited to their own submissions.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The $4000/month budget is tight for advanced features at scale. ECS Fargate auto-scaling with reserved baseline tasks is the right trade-off. Third-party CoreLogic API costs must be tracked per-submission to identify cost anomalies early.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 1 plan left the reinsurance treaty check integration underspecified. The batch loss history ingestion job lacked a retry strategy for partial failures. These gaps must be addressed in Round 2.

backend.service_design: Domain modules: submissions, enrichment, scoring, quoting, broker_accounts, loss_history, admin. Async enrichment via FastAPI BackgroundTasks calling CoreLogic and additional property data APIs concurrently.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful OpenAPI 3.0. POST /v1/submissions (intake), GET /v1/submissions/{id}/score (risk score), POST /v1/submissions/{id}/quotes (generate quote), GET /v1/broker/{id}/submissions (broker view).
backend.background_jobs: Nightly Celery job pulls loss history from carrier claims system, updates risk_scores, flags adverse-loss accounts. Retry on partial failure with dead-letter queue.
backend.failure_handling: Circuit breaker on CoreLogic enrichment API; fallback to available property data with manual_review flag set on submission.

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with route-based code splitting. Submissions list, submission detail with enrichment status, quote builder, broker portal, admin panel.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Submission Intake form with property address autocomplete, Enrichment Status tracker, Risk Score dashboard, Quote Version comparison view, Broker Account management.
frontend.state_management: React Query for server state. Zustand slice for active quote session. Polling on enrichment status endpoint until complete.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pools with MFA. Separate user pool for broker accounts with restricted claims.
security.authorization_model: RBAC: underwriter (own book), senior_underwriter (all submissions, approval authority), broker (own firm submissions only), admin.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: CloudTrail for AWS API actions. Custom submission_access_log table recording every view and mutation of underwriting submissions for SOC 2 evidence.

data.entities: underwriting_submissions, property_inspections, risk_scores, quote_versions, coverage_lines, reinsurance_treaties, loss_history_records, broker_accounts
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 primary with read replica for analytics queries. Redis 7 for quote cache (TTL 4h) and session tokens.
data.retention_and_deletion: 7-year retention for submissions and quotes. Automated archival to S3 Glacier after 2 years in PostgreSQL.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster: API service (2-8 tasks), Celery worker (1-4 tasks), Redis. ALB with WAF. CloudFront for React SPA. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: lint, pytest with 85% coverage gate, Docker build, push ECR, ECS rolling deploy. Migration via Alembic one-off task.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM sidecar. Custom metrics: submission_enrichment_latency_p99, quote_generation_rate, loss_history_ingestion_lag. PagerDuty on enrichment circuit breaker open.

previous_plan.title: Commercial Property Underwriting Portal v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Fullstack portal for commercial property underwriting with React 18, FastAPI, PostgreSQL on AWS ECS Fargate. Supports submission intake, CoreLogic enrichment, risk scoring, quote versioning, and broker delivery.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: React SPA via CloudFront, FastAPI on ECS Fargate behind ALB with WAF, PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ, Redis 7 for quote cache. Cognito MFA for all users.
previous_plan.data_model: underwriting_submissions, risk_scores, quote_versions, coverage_lines, loss_history_records, broker_accounts — reinsurance_treaties listed but treaty check flow not detailed.
previous_plan.workflows: Submission intake and enrichment pipeline described. Loss history nightly job mentioned without retry strategy. Reinsurance check referenced without integration detail.

target_output.thinking_summary: Addressing two round-1 gaps: the reinsurance treaty check needs a concrete API integration pattern with a graceful fallback, and the loss history batch job needs Celery retry with dead-letter queuing. The rest of the underwriting portal architecture is sound — FastAPI async enrichment, PostgreSQL with Redis quote cache, and Cognito RBAC are all correctly specified. This revision makes the plan production-ready by closing those integration holes.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined reinsurance treaty check as a synchronous FastAPI service call to the internal ReinsuranceDataService with a 2s timeout. Added fallback: if treaty service is unavailable, submission is flagged requires_treaty_review and routed to senior underwriter queue. Added reinsurance_treaties entity with treaty_limit, peril_class, effective_date, and check_status fields.
fix_report.expected_outcome: ISS-001 resolved: treaty check has defined integration, data model, timeout, and fallback path.

fix_report.action_taken: Implemented Celery retry with exponential backoff (max 3 retries, 30s/5m/30m intervals) for the loss history ingestion job. Added SQS dead-letter queue for permanently failed records. Datadog alert fires if DLQ depth exceeds 10. Added partial_success_count and failed_record_ids to the job result model.
fix_report.expected_outcome: ISS-002 resolved: partial failures are retried, permanently failed records are queued for investigation, and operations team is alerted.

target_output.title: Commercial Property Underwriting Portal
target_output.executive_summary: Advanced fullstack portal for commercial property underwriting on AWS ECS Fargate with React 18 and Python FastAPI. Ingests broker submissions, enriches property data via CoreLogic API, scores risk, checks reinsurance treaties, versions quotes, and delivers bindable offers to brokers within five minutes. SOC 2 Type II controls throughout.
target_output.architecture_overview: React 18 SPA served via CloudFront communicates over HTTPS to FastAPI on ECS Fargate behind ALB with WAF. Async enrichment pipeline calls CoreLogic and internal ReinsuranceDataService concurrently, with circuit breakers on both. PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ stores underwriting_submissions, quote_versions, and reinsurance_treaties. Redis 7 caches active quotes with 4-hour TTL. A Celery worker fleet handles nightly loss history ingestion from the carrier claims system with SQS dead-letter queuing for failed records.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query, Zustand. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0, Celery 5, Pydantic v2. Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ, Redis 7 ElastiCache. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB with WAF, CloudFront, S3, SQS dead-letter queue, Cognito. Monitoring: Datadog APM, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Submission Intake: structured broker intake form with property address autocomplete and occupancy classification. Enrichment Pipeline: concurrent CoreLogic property data and reinsurance treaty checks with fallback flags. Risk Scoring: actuarial model producing hazard score by peril class. Quote Versioning: multiple coverage line configurations per submission with comparison view. Broker Portal: scoped submission list, quote delivery, and binding confirmation. Admin Panel: user management, enrichment API health, and batch job monitoring.
target_output.system_components: CloudFront CDN for React SPA static assets. ALB with WAF rules protecting FastAPI. ECS Fargate API service (2-8 tasks, CPU 60% scaling). ECS Fargate Celery worker fleet (1-4 tasks). ReinsuranceDataService (internal microservice, synchronous call with 2s timeout). PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ (primary + read replica for analytics). Redis 7 ElastiCache for quote cache and session tokens. SQS dead-letter queue for failed loss history records. Cognito user pools for underwriters and brokers.
target_output.workflows: Submission and Enrichment: Broker submits property address and occupancy data via POST /v1/submissions. FastAPI dispatches concurrent async calls to CoreLogic (property characteristics, construction, occupancy) and ReinsuranceDataService (treaty limits, peril class). If ReinsuranceDataService times out after 2s, submission is flagged requires_treaty_review. Risk scoring runs on available enrichment data and stores score to risk_scores table. Quote is generated and cached in Redis. Loss History Ingestion: Nightly Celery job pulls claims records from carrier claims API, transforms to loss_history_records, updates risk_scores for flagged accounts. On partial API failure, Celery retries with exponential backoff (30s, 5m, 30m). Permanently failed records pushed to SQS dead-letter queue. Datadog metric loss_history_dlq_depth alerts PagerDuty when depth exceeds 10.
target_output.data_model: underwriting_submissions (id UUID PK, broker_account_id FK, property_address, occupancy_class, construction_type, building_value, enrichment_status ENUM pending/complete/partial, requires_treaty_review BOOL, submitted_at). risk_scores (id UUID PK, submission_id FK, hazard_score NUMERIC, peril_class, scoring_model_version, scored_at). quote_versions (id UUID PK, submission_id FK, version_int, coverage_lines JSONB, total_premium, status ENUM draft/delivered/bound, generated_at). reinsurance_treaties (id UUID PK, treaty_name, peril_class, treaty_limit, effective_date, expiry_date, check_status ENUM verified/requires_review/unavailable). loss_history_records (id UUID PK, broker_account_id FK, policy_year, loss_amount, loss_type, source_claim_id, ingested_at). broker_accounts (id UUID PK, firm_name, cognito_group_id, active BOOL).
target_output.api_design: RESTful API at /v1. POST /v1/submissions (create submission, triggers enrichment). GET /v1/submissions/{id} (submission detail with enrichment status). GET /v1/submissions/{id}/score (risk score and hazard breakdown). POST /v1/submissions/{id}/quotes (generate quote version). GET /v1/submissions/{id}/quotes (list versions). PATCH /v1/submissions/{id}/quotes/{version}/deliver (send to broker). GET /v1/broker/{broker_id}/submissions (broker-scoped list). GET /v1/admin/jobs/loss-history (batch job status, DLQ depth). Auth: Cognito JWT validated at ALB and FastAPI dependency.
target_output.security_and_compliance: SOC 2 Type II controls: all submission views and mutations logged to submission_access_log with user_id, action, resource_id, timestamp. CloudTrail captures all AWS API calls. Cognito MFA enforced for underwriter and admin roles. Broker accounts scoped via Cognito group claims. WAF rules block OWASP Top 10 on ALB. AES-256 encryption at rest for RDS and Redis. TLS 1.3 in transit. ReinsuranceDataService calls use internal VPC endpoints. 7-year submission retention per state insurance regulations.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: ruff lint, pytest with 85% coverage gate, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling update with health check gate. Environments: dev (single AZ, smaller instances), staging (Multi-AZ mirror with production data shapes), production (Multi-AZ us-east-1a/1b, ECS auto-scaling 2-8 API tasks, 1-4 Celery tasks). Alembic migrations run as ECS one-off tasks before service deploy. Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation. Blue/green deploy strategy for zero-downtime quote service updates.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM on all ECS tasks with trace propagation through async enrichment calls. Custom metrics: submission_enrichment_latency_p99, corelog_api_circuit_breaker_state, quote_generation_rate, loss_history_dlq_depth, adverse_loss_flag_rate. PagerDuty escalation policies: enrichment circuit breaker open, DLQ depth >10, API p99 >5s, RDS connections >80%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $3200/month for 200 daily submissions. CoreLogic API cost per call tracked via Datadog custom metric. ECS auto-scaling on CPU 60% target. Redis reserved instance for quote cache. Read replica sized for analytics queries to avoid impacting transactional throughput. S3 Glacier archival after 2 years reduces PostgreSQL storage costs.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Submission intake, CoreLogic enrichment pipeline, basic risk scoring, quote versioning, broker delivery, Cognito RBAC, CI/CD, SOC 2 logging controls. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Reinsurance treaty check integration with ReinsuranceDataService, full fallback flow, loss history ingestion job with retry and dead-letter queuing. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Admin dashboard for enrichment health and batch monitoring, analytics read replica, advanced quote comparison UI.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python type hints enforced via mypy strict. Ruff linter and formatter in CI. All FastAPI endpoints require authentication dependency injection. 85% pytest coverage gate. OpenAPI spec generated and versioned. ADR documents for enrichment fallback decisions and retry configuration choices.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: CoreLogic API rate limits during peak broker submission windows. Mitigation: implement submission queue with rate-limited dispatch and broker notification of estimated completion time. Risk: PostgreSQL quote lock contention on concurrent senior underwriter edits. Mitigation: optimistic locking with version field on quote_versions. Tradeoff: synchronous reinsurance treaty check adds latency to enrichment pipeline; acceptable given 30s SLA budget. Risk: stale Redis quote cache used for binding after market conditions change. Mitigation: 4-hour TTL with forced cache invalidation on any submission data change.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed CoreLogic as property data vendor. Reinsurance treaty check resolved as synchronous internal API call to ReinsuranceDataService. Loss history ingestion retry strategy resolved with Celery exponential backoff and SQS DLQ. PostgreSQL chosen over Aurora for cost predictability at current scale.

project.goal: Replace Flash-based surety bond site with fast, accessible static site
project.target_users: Small contractors seeking license and permit bonds
project.timeline: 2-week delivery

backend.framework: none

security.auth: none

open_questions.contact_form: Confirm Netlify Forms vs. Formspree for contact inquiry routing

reasoner_reviews.product: Bond product listings organized by trade category and state are the primary conversion driver. The contact inquiry form must route leads to the sales team reliably. Contractor FAQ content should be prioritized based on top search queries from the legacy site.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro 4 with zero client JS by default is the correct choice for a content-heavy site needing Lighthouse >95. State requirements tables should be pre-rendered from Markdown data files at build time rather than fetched client-side. Netlify Forms handles contact submissions without introducing a backend dependency.
reasoner_reviews.security: CSP headers must block inline scripts to prevent XSS. Only GA4 script should be allowlisted. HTTPS via Netlify automatic TLS is sufficient. No cookies beyond GA4 consent should be set.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Two-week timeline is achievable given the scope. Content migration from the Flash site is the main risk factor. Lighthouse >95 requires image optimization with Astro Image integration and no render-blocking resources.

security.auth_design: No authentication required. Public site only.
security.abuse_prevention: Netlify Forms spam filtering via honeypot field on contact form. Rate limiting on form submission endpoint via Netlify built-in controls.
security.privacy_controls: GA4 consent banner required for EU visitors. No personal data stored on server. Contact form data routed to sales email only via Netlify Forms webhook.
security.security_risks: XSS via injected content is primary risk — mitigated by strict CSP and no dynamic content rendering from user input.

data.entities: bond_product_listings (trade_category, bond_type, min_amount, state_availability), state_requirements (state, bond_type, required_amount, licensing_authority), contractor_faqs (question, answer, category), office_locations (city, state, address, phone), contact_forms (form fields only, not persisted)
data.storage_design: All content as Markdown and YAML files in Git repository. No database. State requirements tables as YAML data files, rendered at build time.
data.retention_and_deletion: No data storage. Contact form submissions delivered via Netlify Forms to sales team email.
data.data_risks: Content staleness if state requirement tables are not kept current — mitigate with annual content review process.

devops.deployment_topology: Netlify CDN global edge network. GitHub repository connected to Netlify project. Branch deploys for PRs provide preview URLs.
devops.ci_cd_design: Push to main triggers Netlify build: astro build, output to dist/, deployed globally. GitHub Actions runs Lighthouse CI on PR builds and blocks merge if score drops below 95.
devops.observability_stack: Google Analytics 4 for page views, bond type page clicks, and contact form conversion funnel. Netlify Analytics for raw traffic data without cookies.
devops.rollback_strategy: Netlify one-click rollback to any previous deploy via dashboard. Deploy lock available during content review windows.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 static site; bond product pages generated at build time from structured YAML content; Pagefind for search
frontend.pages_and_flows: Home, Bond Products by Trade Category, State Requirements Tables, Contractor FAQ, Quote Request Form, Agent Portal CTA
frontend.performance_notes: State requirements tables rendered as static HTML for SEO; Pagefind search index loaded on demand; images use WebP with Astro image processing
frontend.accessibility_notes: Complex tables need row and column headers; quote form fields require visible labels and error messages for screen readers

previous_plan.title: Surety Bond Company Website v3
previous_plan.executive_summary: Astro 4 static site on Netlify for a surety bond company with bond product listings and contact form handled by a Netlify Function endpoint.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Astro 4 build deployed to Netlify CDN. Contact form POSTs to a Node.js Netlify Function that stores submissions in a PostgreSQL database.
previous_plan.data_model: bond_products (id, trade_category, bond_type, amount), contact_submissions (id, name, email, message, created_at) — PostgreSQL tables.
previous_plan.workflows: Contact form submits to /api/contact Netlify Function. Form data persisted to PostgreSQL contact_submissions table.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a persistently flawed plan across four rounds — the architect kept introducing server-side infrastructure despite an explicit no-server-runtime constraint. The fix is straightforward: remove all Netlify Functions, use Netlify Forms for contact submissions, and replace the PostgreSQL data model with a description of the flat-file content structure. The architecture for this project is genuinely simple: Astro 4 builds to static HTML, Netlify CDN delivers it, GA4 tracks conversions.

fix_report.action_taken: Removed all Netlify Function and server-side API references. Contact form now uses Netlify Forms with a honeypot spam field and routes submissions directly to the sales team email via Netlify's built-in form handling — no server code required.
fix_report.expected_outcome: ISS-001 resolved: zero server-side runtime in the plan, fully compliant with no-server constraint.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced all PostgreSQL entity definitions with a flat-file content schema describing Markdown files and YAML data structures used at build time. bond_product_listings and state_requirements are YAML files; contractor_faqs and office_locations are Markdown collections. No database referenced anywhere in the plan.
fix_report.expected_outcome: ISS-002 resolved: data model accurately reflects Git-based flat-file content, no database entities.

target_output.title: Surety Bond Company Public Website
target_output.executive_summary: Astro 4 static website for a regional surety bond company, deployed to Netlify CDN with zero server-side runtime. Lists license and permit bond products by trade category, displays state requirement tables, provides contractor FAQs, and routes contact inquiries via Netlify Forms. Replaces an inaccessible Flash-based site with a Lighthouse >95 performance score.
target_output.architecture_overview: Astro 4 build pipeline generates static HTML, CSS, and minimal JavaScript from Markdown content files and YAML data sources at build time. No server-side runtime exists. The dist/ artifact is deployed directly to Netlify's global CDN edge network. Contact inquiries are handled by Netlify Forms without any API endpoint or backend function. Google Analytics 4 provides conversion tracking via a CSP-allowlisted script tag.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Astro Image for optimized WebP output. Content: Markdown files for product pages and FAQs, YAML for state requirements and office data. Analytics: Google Analytics 4 with consent banner. Build: Astro static export. Hosting: Netlify CDN. CI: GitHub Actions for Lighthouse CI checks. Forms: Netlify Forms built-in.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Bond Product Listings: pages organized by trade category (contractor, auto dealer, notary, mortgage broker) with required amount and state availability from YAML data. State Requirements Tables: state-by-state table for each bond type rendered from YAML at build time. Contractor FAQ: searchable FAQ section built from Markdown content. Office Locations: contact cards for each branch office. Contact Inquiry Form: Netlify Forms with spam honeypot, routes to sales team email.
target_output.system_components: Astro 4 build process (CI): reads Markdown and YAML sources, generates optimized static HTML with Astro Image WebP processing. Netlify CDN: global edge delivery of static assets with automatic HTTPS, HTTP/2 push, and Brotli compression. Netlify Forms: serverless form handling with spam filtering, no API endpoint required. Google Analytics 4: consent-gated client-side tracking script.
target_output.workflows: Content Update: content editor commits updated YAML state requirements or Markdown bond product page to main branch; GitHub Actions runs Lighthouse CI; on pass, Netlify build triggers automatically within 90 seconds, cache invalidates globally. Contact Inquiry: contractor fills form with trade, state, and contact details; Netlify Forms validates honeypot, routes submission to sales@company.com via Netlify webhook notification. No server code involved.
target_output.data_model: bond_product_listings: YAML files per trade category with fields bond_type, description, typical_amount_range, states_available, licensing_authority. state_requirements: YAML keyed by state with bond_type, required_amount, issuing_authority, renewal_period. contractor_faqs: Markdown files with frontmatter category and title, body content. office_locations: YAML with city, state, street_address, phone, email. All content is read-only at build time — no runtime database.
target_output.api_design: No API endpoints. Contact form submissions handled by Netlify Forms POST handler (built-in, no custom code). Form action attribute set to netlify with method POST and data-netlify=true attribute. Netlify Forms UI provides submission review and webhook delivery to sales team email.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced via Netlify automatic TLS with HSTS header. Content-Security-Policy header allows only self-origin and GA4 script domain. No cookies set beyond GA4 consent. Netlify Forms honeypot field blocks automated spam submissions. No personal data stored on any server — contact submissions delivered to email only. No auth, no session management, no server attack surface.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions on push to main: runs astro check (TypeScript validation), Lighthouse CI against preview URL (blocks if performance <95), then triggers Netlify production deploy. Pull request builds generate preview URLs on Netlify for content review before merge. Netlify deploy lock available during active content review. One-click rollback to any prior deploy via Netlify dashboard. No containers, no infrastructure to manage.
target_output.observability: Google Analytics 4 tracks bond product page views by trade category, state requirements page engagement, contact form start and completion events (conversion funnel). Netlify Analytics provides server-side request counts without cookies. GA4 custom event bond_type_viewed fires on each product page load with bond_type and trade_category parameters.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Netlify free tier sufficient for estimated traffic. Upgrade to Netlify Pro ($19/month) if form submissions exceed 100/month. CDN auto-scales for traffic spikes with no configuration. No database or server infrastructure costs. Total estimated cost: $0-$19/month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (2 weeks): Migrate all bond product and state requirement content from Flash source materials to Markdown/YAML. Build Astro site with Tailwind, deploy to Netlify, configure GA4. Launch replacing Flash site. Phase 2 (post-launch): SEO optimization based on GA4 data, expansion of FAQ content based on contact form inquiry themes, addition of blog section for surety bond news.
target_output.development_guidelines: All content changes via pull requests with Netlify preview URL review. Lighthouse CI score >95 enforced in GitHub Actions as branch protection rule. Images added via Astro Image component only to ensure WebP optimization. No inline styles — Tailwind classes only. Markdown frontmatter schema validated via Astro content collections.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: state requirement tables becoming outdated as state insurance departments update bond amounts. Mitigation: annual content review calendar with compliance team sign-off. Risk: GA4 blocked by ad blockers, reducing conversion visibility. Mitigation: Netlify server-side analytics as baseline. Tradeoff: Netlify Forms limits form submission visibility — if sales team needs CRM integration, a future lightweight backend would be required but is out of scope for this static site.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Netlify Forms chosen over Formspree for contact routing — simpler integration with no additional account. Flat-file Markdown CMS chosen over headless CMS for simplicity given team size and update frequency. No cookie banner needed beyond GA4 consent as no other tracking is in use.

metadata.notes: Intentionally flawed case: prior rounds introduced server-side runtime and database entities violating explicit static-site constraints. Round 4 resolves both issues.

profile.domain: Workers compensation case management for third-party administrators

project_goal.value: Workers compensation case management system for TPAs tracking injured worker claims from FROI through medical treatment, RTW milestones, and indemnity payments with pharmacy and medical bill review integrations
project_goal.rationale: Core TPA operational workflow replacing legacy mainframe system

target_users.value: Claims adjusters, nurse case managers, employer HR contacts, and medical provider billing staff via partner portal
target_users.rationale: Multi-stakeholder claims environment requiring differentiated access

project_class.rationale: Requires Vue 3 frontend for adjusters and partner-facing portal plus Spring Boot API backend

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, batch_jobs, admin_panel, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Full TPA operational platform with external partner integrations and nightly reconciliation jobs

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-party integrations, HIPAA compliance, complex claim lifecycle state machine, and batch reconciliation

risk_level.rationale: Protected health information and indemnity payment data with external partner exposure

data_sensitivity.rationale: Stores injured worker PHI including medical treatment records and diagnoses

external_exposure.rationale: Medical providers, pharmacy benefit managers, and employer HR teams access via partner portal

access_model.value: Azure AD B2C RBAC with roles for adjuster, nurse_case_manager, employer_contact, medical_provider, and TPA_admin; partner access via federated OIDC
access_model.rationale: Multi-tenant partner access requires federated identity with scoped data isolation

feature_scope.value: Claim intake via FROI, adjuster assignment, compensability investigation tracking, reserve management, medical provider billing and bill review, pharmacy benefit reconciliation, RTW plan tracking, indemnity payment scheduling, employer and provider partner portal, analytics dashboard
feature_scope.rationale: Full TPA operational feature set required for system replacement

mvp_scope.value: FROI intake, adjuster assignment, claim lifecycle state machine, basic reserve management, employer partner portal view, and core indemnity payment scheduling
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum to replace legacy system for initial client migrations

security_baseline.value: AES-256 encryption at rest for PHI fields, TLS 1.3 in transit, MFA for all internal users, mTLS for pharmacy benefit manager API integrations, RBAC with least-privilege scoping per tenant
security_baseline.rationale: HIPAA minimum controls plus partner mTLS per compliance requirement

frontend_stack.value: Vue 3 with TypeScript and Pinia
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; component model suits complex claim workflow forms

backend_stack.value: Java 21 with Spring Boot 3 and Spring Data JPA
backend_stack.rationale: Enterprise Java ecosystem suits integration-heavy TPA domain

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server with read replica
data_platform.rationale: Relational integrity for claim state machine; managed HA on Azure

hosting_target.value: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) in East US 2
hosting_target.rationale: Kubernetes orchestration for batch workloads and API pods; Azure for data residency

privacy_retention_policy.value: PHI retained 7 years per state WC statutes; audit logs 5 years; payment records 7 years; automated archival to Azure Blob cold tier after 3 years active retention
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: State workers compensation record retention mandates

future_scope.value: EDI integration with state WC boards for mandatory filing, telemedicine referral workflow, AI-assisted medical bill review anomaly detection
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap following initial TPA client migrations

constraints.value: Must deploy within Azure East US 2 for client data residency; integrate with two named pharmacy benefit managers via HL7 FHIR R4; legacy mainframe migration of 50,000 historical claims within 6 months
constraints.rationale: Client contract and regulatory data residency requirements

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor with Application Insights for APM, structured JSON logs via Log Analytics Workspace, PagerDuty escalation for claim processing errors and batch job failures
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standard aligned with Azure hosting

execution_preference.value: Scrum with 2-week sprints; separate DevOps hardening track for AKS CI/CD pipeline improvements
execution_preference.rationale: Parallel delivery and infrastructure improvement tracks

compliance_context.value: HIPAA and HITECH for PHI handling, state workers compensation statutes for claims handling timeliness, WCAB reporting requirements, BAA with Azure
compliance_context.rationale: Health data and regulated insurance claims require dual compliance framework

project.goal: Replace legacy mainframe TPA claims system with cloud-native workers comp case management platform
project.target_users: Claims adjusters, nurse case managers, employer HR contacts, medical provider billing staff
project.timeline: MVP in 6 months, full feature delivery in 12 months

frontend.approach: SPA with route-level lazy loading for claim detail modules

backend.framework: Spring Boot 3 with Spring Data JPA
backend.batch: Spring Batch for nightly reconciliation jobs

security.auth: Azure AD B2C with OIDC federation for partner tenants
security.transport: TLS 1.3 for HTTPS, mTLS for PBM integrations
security.encryption: AES-256 at rest for PHI columns

data.cache: Redis 7 for session and claim state cache

constraints.integrations: Two PBM vendors via HL7 FHIR R4

open_questions.edi_filing: State EDI filing scope deferred to Year 2

reasoner_reviews.product: The claim lifecycle state machine covering FROI through RTW closure is the core differentiator. Prior rounds underspecified the indemnity payment scheduling logic and reserve adjustment audit trail, both of which adjusters use daily and which drive regulatory compliance timeliness metrics.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: AKS with Spring Batch for nightly PBM reconciliation is architecturally sound. Round 2 identified that the batch job lacked idempotency guarantees — on retry after failure it was double-counting pharmacy invoices. The job checkpoint and skip logic must be explicitly designed using Spring Batch ItemSkipPolicy.
reasoner_reviews.security: mTLS for PBM integrations remains the blocking gap from round 2. Azure API Management with client certificate enforcement should front all inbound PBM HL7 FHIR calls. Internal PHI field-level encryption using Azure Key Vault references in the JPA entity mapping must be documented explicitly.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Legacy claim migration window of 6 months for 50,000 historical claims is aggressive. The migration plan must specify a parallel-run validation period where the new system and the mainframe produce matching reserve totals before cutover.
reasoner_reviews.critic: DevOps modernization theme requires the AKS CI/CD pipeline gap to be closed — round 2 plan described deployment topology but left the Helm chart structure and rollback trigger logic underspecified.

backend.service_design: Spring Boot microservice modules: claim-intake-service handling FROI and adjuster assignment, claim-lifecycle-service owning the state machine transitions and reserve adjustments, payment-service for indemnity scheduling and disbursement, integration-service for PBM and bill review vendor calls via FHIR adapters
backend.api_patterns: RESTful OpenAPI 3.1 with versioned prefixes /api/v1; async FHIR R4 messaging for PBM integrations via Azure Service Bus; Spring Batch job REST triggers via /api/v1/admin/jobs
backend.background_jobs: Spring Batch nightly PBM invoice reconciliation job with chunk-oriented processing (chunk size 100), ItemSkipPolicy for malformed invoices, JobRepository checkpointing for restart safety, skip limit 50 with alerting
backend.failure_handling: Circuit breaker via Resilience4j on PBM FHIR API calls with 30s timeout and fallback to pending-manual-review status; dead letter queue on Service Bus for failed FHIR messages

frontend.app_structure: Vue 3 SPA with route-level lazy loading; adjuster workspace, nurse case manager view, employer portal, and admin panel as separate route namespaces
frontend.pages_and_flows: Claim Dashboard (queue by status and priority), FROI Intake Wizard (multi-step with injury details and coverage verification), Claim Detail (timeline, reserve ledger, treatment plan, payment history), Bill Review Queue, RTW Plan Editor, Employer Portal (read-only claim status), Analytics Dashboard
frontend.state_management: Pinia stores for active claim context, adjuster queue, and notification state; Vue Query for server-synced claim data with optimistic updates on status transitions
frontend.accessibility_notes: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance for claim forms; keyboard-navigable claim timeline and document viewer

security.auth_design: Azure AD B2C with OIDC for internal users; federated OIDC for employer and medical provider tenants with custom policies; MFA enforced via Conditional Access for all internal roles
security.authorization_model: Spring Security RBAC with claims-scoped data isolation; medical providers see only their billed claims; employers see only their injured workers; adjusters scoped to assigned TPA client accounts
security.secrets_management: Azure Key Vault for database credentials, PBM mTLS certificates, and PHI column encryption keys; Workload Identity for pod-level Key Vault access without static secrets
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Append-only audit_events table capturing every PHI field access, claim status transition, reserve adjustment, and payment authorization; forwarded to Log Analytics Workspace for 5-year retention

data.entities: injury_claims (id, claim_number, claimant_id FK, employer_id FK, date_of_injury, body_part_code, cause_code, status ENUM froi_filed/investigating/accepted/denied/closed, adjuster_id FK, reserve_total_cents, created_at), claimants (id, ssn_encrypted, name_encrypted, dob, address_encrypted, employer_id FK), medical_providers (id, npi, name, tax_id_encrypted, specialty_code), bill_reviews (id, claim_id FK, provider_id FK, billed_amount_cents, allowed_amount_cents, review_status ENUM pending/approved/reduced/denied, reviewer_id, reviewed_at), indemnity_payments (id, claim_id FK, payment_type ENUM ttd/tpd/ppd/ptd, payment_date, gross_amount_cents, net_amount_cents, check_number, status), return_to_work_plans (id, claim_id FK, target_rtw_date, restrictions_json, employer_contact_id FK, status ENUM draft/active/completed), reserve_adjustments (id, claim_id FK, prior_reserve_cents, new_reserve_cents, reason_code, adjuster_id FK, adjusted_at)
data.schema_notes: PHI columns (ssn_encrypted, name_encrypted, address_encrypted) stored as bytea with AES-256-GCM using Key Vault-managed keys; claim_number uses carrier prefix + sequence for human readability
data.retention_and_deletion: Active PHI retained 7 years; archive to Azure Blob cold tier at 3 years; automated deletion pipeline at 7-year mark with legal hold override flag

devops.deployment_topology: AKS East US 2 with separate node pools: api-pool (Standard_D4s_v5, 3-6 nodes auto-scale), batch-pool (Standard_D8s_v5, 1-3 nodes, scaled to zero off-hours), ingress via Azure Application Gateway with WAF v2
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps pipelines: build stage (Maven 3.9, JUnit 5 test gate 80% coverage), Docker build and push to Azure Container Registry, Helm chart lint and template validation, deploy to AKS via Helm upgrade with --atomic rollback on failure
devops.environments: dev (single replica, lower node SKU), staging (production topology, synthetic claim data), production (multi-AZ node pool, Azure Flexible Server zone-redundant)
devops.rollback_strategy: Helm upgrade --atomic ensures automatic rollback on failed health checks; previous Helm release retained for manual rollback; Flyway migration scripts versioned and reversible via repair command

ISS-001.title: Spring Batch PBM reconciliation job lacks idempotency on retry
ISS-001.detail: On job failure and restart the reconciliation job reprocesses already-committed invoice records, causing double-counting of pharmacy approved amounts in the bill_reviews table. No JobRepository checkpoint or skip policy was defined in round 2.

history.note: Batch reconciliation design was high-level only
Plan added batch section but omitted idempotency and checkpoint specifics

ISS-002.title: mTLS for PBM FHIR integrations not implemented in plan
ISS-002.detail: Security baseline requires mTLS for PBM vendor connections. Rounds 1 and 2 describe FHIR integration without specifying client certificate enforcement, Azure API Management certificate policy, or certificate rotation procedure.

history.note: Integration section mentioned FHIR but no mTLS detail
Still no mTLS specifics in security or integration sections

ISS-003.title: Helm rollback trigger and chart structure underspecified
ISS-003.detail: DevOps modernization theme requires AKS CI/CD clarity. Prior plans described deployment topology without Helm chart layout, health probe configuration for rollback triggers, or node pool separation between API and batch workloads.

history.note: Deployment section lacked Helm and rollback specifics

previous_plan.title: Workers Compensation Case Management Platform - Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Cloud-native TPA claims platform built on Vue 3, Spring Boot 3, and PostgreSQL 16 on AKS managing injured worker claims from FROI through RTW closure with PBM and medical bill review integrations.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Vue 3 SPA behind Azure Application Gateway communicating to Spring Boot REST API on AKS; PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server with read replica; Azure AD B2C for identity; Spring Batch for nightly PBM reconciliation.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Azure AD B2C RBAC, AES-256 PHI field encryption, TLS 1.3; FHIR integrations described without mTLS specifics; HIPAA audit logging to append-only table.
