target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 must close the two highest-priority issues: Spring Batch idempotency for PBM invoice reconciliation and mTLS for PBM FHIR integrations. The batch fix requires explicit JobRepository checkpoint configuration and ItemSkipPolicy. The mTLS fix requires Azure API Management client certificate policy with vendor certificate thumbprint pinning. Secondary focus is Helm chart structure and AKS node pool separation to address ISS-003 and fulfill the devops_modernization theme.

fix_report.action_taken: Added Spring Batch JobRepository with chunk-size 100, ItemSkipPolicy with skip limit 50 and SkippableException for MalformedFHIRInvoiceException, restart-safe step execution via unique JobParameters including batch_date and run_id, and idempotency check querying existing approved bill_reviews records before committing new chunks
fix_report.expected_outcome: PBM reconciliation job can be safely restarted after failure without double-counting pharmacy invoice approvals

fix_report.action_taken: Added Azure API Management layer fronting all inbound PBM FHIR R4 calls with client certificate validation policy pinned to each vendor certificate thumbprint stored in Azure Key Vault; added certificate rotation procedure using Key Vault certificate renewal with 30-day expiry alerting; documented in security_and_compliance and api_design sections
fix_report.expected_outcome: All PBM FHIR integrations enforce mTLS with vendor certificate pinning meeting security baseline requirement

target_output.title: Workers Compensation Case Management Platform
target_output.executive_summary: HIPAA-compliant workers compensation case management platform for third-party administrators built on Vue 3, Spring Boot 3 on AKS, and PostgreSQL 16. Manages injured worker claims through FROI intake, compensability investigation, reserve management, medical bill review, pharmacy benefit reconciliation, indemnity payment scheduling, and RTW tracking. Integrates with pharmacy benefit managers via HL7 FHIR R4 over mTLS and with medical bill review vendors via REST. Nightly Spring Batch reconciliation jobs process PBM invoices with full checkpoint and skip-policy idempotency guarantees.
target_output.architecture_overview: Azure-hosted fullstack platform with Vue 3 SPA delivered via Azure Static Web Apps, routed through Azure Application Gateway WAF v2 to Spring Boot 3 API pods on AKS East US 2. API pods connect to PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server (zone-redundant) with read replica serving analytics queries. Redis 7 on Azure Cache provides claim state and session caching. PBM integrations flow through Azure API Management enforcing client certificate mTLS validation before routing FHIR R4 messages to the integration-service via Azure Service Bus. Spring Batch jobs run on a dedicated AKS batch-pool node pool scaled to zero off-hours. Azure AD B2C federates identity for partner tenants. All PHI columns encrypted at rest using AES-256-GCM with keys from Azure Key Vault via Workload Identity.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3, TypeScript, Pinia, Tailwind CSS, Vue Query. Backend: Java 21, Spring Boot 3, Spring Data JPA, Spring Batch 5, Resilience4j, Flyway. API Gateway: Azure API Management for mTLS PBM FHIR integration. Messaging: Azure Service Bus for async FHIR ingestion. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Flexible Server zone-redundant with read replica. Cache: Redis 7 on Azure Cache. Auth: Azure AD B2C with Conditional Access MFA. Infrastructure: AKS East US 2 (api-pool and batch-pool node pools), Azure Container Registry, Azure Key Vault, Azure Application Gateway WAF v2. CI/CD: Azure DevOps. Observability: Azure Monitor, Application Insights, Log Analytics Workspace, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: FROI Intake: Structured injury report capture with ICD-10 body part and cause codes, employer coverage verification, and auto-assignment to adjuster queue. Claim Lifecycle: State machine transitions (froi_filed, investigating, accepted, denied, closed) with reserve initialization and mandatory compensability deadline tracking. Medical Bill Review: Provider invoice ingestion, fee schedule validation, allowed amount calculation, and reviewer approval queue. Pharmacy Benefit Reconciliation: Nightly FHIR R4 invoice ingestion from two PBM vendors, approved-amount matching against treatment authorizations, exception flagging. Indemnity Payment Scheduling: TTD, TPD, PPD, and PTD payment generation with check issuance tracking. RTW Plan Management: Restriction tracking, employer contact notifications, and RTW milestone recording. Employer Partner Portal: Read-only claim status, indemnity payment history, and RTW milestone visibility. Analytics Dashboard: Adjuster caseload, reserve adequacy trends, and bill review turnaround metrics.
target_output.system_components: Azure Application Gateway WAF v2 for ingress TLS termination and WAF rule enforcement. AKS api-pool (Standard_D4s_v5, 3-6 nodes auto-scale on CPU 60%) running claim-intake-service, claim-lifecycle-service, payment-service, and integration-service pods. AKS batch-pool (Standard_D8s_v5, 0-3 nodes, scaled to zero outside nightly window) for Spring Batch PBM reconciliation and migration jobs. Azure API Management fronting inbound PBM FHIR R4 calls with client certificate validation policy. Azure Service Bus topics: fhir-pbm-invoices (ingestion), fhir-dlq (dead letter). PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server zone-redundant primary and read replica. Redis 7 on Azure Cache for claim state and session. Azure Key Vault for PHI encryption keys and mTLS certificates. Azure Container Registry. Azure AD B2C for identity federation.
target_output.workflows: FROI Intake and Adjuster Assignment: Adjuster or employer HR files FROI via API POST /api/v1/claims with injury details, ICD-10 codes, and coverage reference. claim-intake-service validates employer coverage, creates injury_claims record in froi_filed status, initializes reserve at zero, triggers adjuster assignment rule engine, creates adjuster_assignment record, sends injured worker and employer acknowledgement via Azure Communication Services. PBM Invoice Reconciliation: Nightly Spring Batch job on batch-pool launches with JobParameters including batch_date and UUID run_id. ItemReader pulls unprocessed FHIR R4 MedicationDispense bundles from fhir-pbm-invoices Service Bus topic. ItemProcessor validates bundle against approved treatment authorizations and checks existing bill_reviews for run_id idempotency key. ItemWriter commits approved bill_review records in chunks of 100. ItemSkipPolicy catches MalformedFHIRInvoiceException up to 50 skips; beyond limit, job alerts via PagerDuty and halts. Failed jobs restart from last committed chunk via JobRepository. Medical Bill Review Queue: Provider submits invoice; integration-service creates bill_review record in pending status; nurse case manager reviews fee schedule validation and allowed amount; approves or denies via PATCH /api/v1/bill-reviews/:id; payment-service schedules indemnity if bill triggers TTD continuation.
target_output.data_model: injury_claims (id UUID PK, claim_number VARCHAR unique, claimant_id FK, employer_id FK, date_of_injury DATE, body_part_code VARCHAR ICD-10-CM, cause_code VARCHAR ANSI, status ENUM froi_filed/investigating/accepted/denied/closed, adjuster_id FK, reserve_total_cents BIGINT, compensability_deadline DATE, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). claimants (id UUID PK, ssn_encrypted BYTEA AES-256-GCM, name_encrypted BYTEA, dob DATE, address_encrypted BYTEA, employer_id FK). medical_providers (id UUID PK, npi CHAR(10), name VARCHAR, tax_id_encrypted BYTEA, specialty_code VARCHAR). bill_reviews (id UUID PK, claim_id FK, provider_id FK, billed_amount_cents BIGINT, allowed_amount_cents BIGINT, review_status ENUM pending/approved/reduced/denied, batch_run_id UUID for idempotency, reviewer_id FK, reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). indemnity_payments (id UUID PK, claim_id FK, payment_type ENUM ttd/tpd/ppd/ptd, payment_date DATE, gross_amount_cents BIGINT, net_amount_cents BIGINT, check_number VARCHAR, status ENUM scheduled/issued/cleared/voided). return_to_work_plans (id UUID PK, claim_id FK, target_rtw_date DATE, restrictions_json JSONB, employer_contact_id FK, status ENUM draft/active/completed). reserve_adjustments (id UUID PK, claim_id FK, prior_reserve_cents BIGINT, new_reserve_cents BIGINT, reason_code VARCHAR, adjuster_id FK, adjusted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). adjuster_assignments (id UUID PK, claim_id FK, adjuster_id FK, assigned_at TIMESTAMPTZ, closed_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: RESTful API /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.1. POST /api/v1/claims (file FROI, returns claim_id and claim_number). GET /api/v1/claims?status=investigating&adjuster_id=X (adjuster queue). GET /api/v1/claims/:id (full claim detail with timeline). PATCH /api/v1/claims/:id/status (state machine transition with reason_code). POST /api/v1/claims/:id/reserves (reserve adjustment entry). GET /api/v1/bill-reviews?status=pending (nurse case manager queue). PATCH /api/v1/bill-reviews/:id (approve/reduce/deny with allowed_amount). POST /api/v1/payments (schedule indemnity payment). GET /api/v1/claims/:id/rtw-plan (RTW plan detail). POST /api/v1/admin/jobs/pbm-reconciliation (trigger nightly job manually). Azure API Management: POST /fhir/v1/pbm/invoices (inbound PBM FHIR R4 with client certificate validation). Rate limiting: 200 req/min per adjuster, 50 req/min per partner tenant.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HIPAA Controls: BAA with Azure, PHI fields (ssn, name, address, tax_id) encrypted AES-256-GCM with Key Vault-managed keys accessed via Workload Identity, TLS 1.3 for all HTTPS, audit_events table capturing actor, action, resource_type, resource_id, ip_address, and timestamp for every PHI access and claim state transition, forwarded to Log Analytics Workspace with 5-year retention. mTLS for PBM Integrations: Azure API Management policy enforces client certificate validation; each PBM vendor certificate thumbprint pinned in APIM named values sourced from Key Vault; certificate renewal automated via Key Vault certificate renewal with 30-day expiry alert to security team; PagerDuty alert on certificate expiry within 14 days. RBAC: Spring Security method-level authorization; adjuster scope limited to assigned TPA client accounts; medical_provider role sees only their own bill_reviews; employer_contact reads only claimants employed by their organization. Session: 30-minute idle timeout for adjuster and nurse case manager roles. WAF: Azure Application Gateway WAF v2 with OWASP Core Rule Set 3.2 blocking SQL injection and XSS. Compliance: State WC statutes compensability deadline tracking enforced in claim state machine; WCAB reporting via nightly export job.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure DevOps Pipeline Stages: (1) Build: Maven 3.9 compile, JUnit 5 with 80% line coverage gate, OWASP dependency check, Docker build and push to Azure Container Registry with image digest. (2) Lint: Helm chart lint and helm template --dry-run validation. (3) Deploy Dev: Helm upgrade --install --atomic --timeout 5m with liveness probe /actuator/health/liveness and readiness probe /actuator/health/readiness; rollback triggered automatically on probe failure. (4) Integration Tests: Synthetic claim FROI intake and bill review flow against dev AKS. (5) Deploy Staging: Same Helm upgrade pattern against staging namespace with production-topology node counts. (6) Deploy Production: Manual approval gate, Helm upgrade with --atomic and --wait, Flyway migration as Helm pre-upgrade hook. Helm Chart Structure: charts/wc-cms/ with templates/api-deployment.yaml (api-pool node selector), templates/batch-cronjob.yaml (batch-pool node selector, suspend: true off-hours), templates/apim-policy-configmap.yaml. AKS Node Pools: api-pool Standard_D4s_v5 min 3 max 6 with HPA on CPU 60%; batch-pool Standard_D8s_v5 min 0 max 3 with KEDA schedule scaler active 02:00-06:00 UTC. Environments: dev, staging, production each in isolated AKS namespaces with Network Policy restricting cross-namespace traffic. Secrets: Azure Key Vault via External Secrets Operator syncing to Kubernetes Secrets with automatic rotation.
target_output.observability: Azure Monitor with Application Insights SDK injected into Spring Boot via applicationinsights-agent-3.x Java agent for distributed tracing across claim-intake, claim-lifecycle, payment, and integration services. Custom metrics tracked in Application Insights: claim_froi_filed_total (counter), claim_state_transition_duration_ms (histogram by from_status/to_status), pbm_reconciliation_invoices_processed (gauge per batch run), pbm_skip_count (alert threshold 40 skips triggers PagerDuty before job halts at 50), bill_review_queue_depth (alert >200 pending), indemnity_payment_processing_latency_ms. Log Analytics Workspace: structured JSON logs with correlation_id, claim_id, and actor_id; query alerts for unhandled exceptions and APIM 5xx rates. PagerDuty escalation for: batch job failure, PBM FHIR dead letter queue depth >10, error rate >2%, API p99 latency >3s, AKS node pool CPU >85%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: AKS api-pool: ~$1,800/month at 3 Standard_D4s_v5 baseline with auto-scale headroom to 6 nodes during open enrollment spikes. Batch-pool KEDA schedule scaler reduces cost by 70% versus always-on — estimated $300/month for 4-hour nightly window. PostgreSQL Flexible Server zone-redundant with read replica: ~$900/month. Redis Azure Cache C2: ~$200/month. Azure APIM Developer tier for PBM integrations: ~$200/month. Total estimated ~$3,400/month at steady state. Cost optimization: Spot node pool for batch workloads reduces batch-pool cost by 60% with eviction-tolerant Spring Batch restart semantics.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (Weeks 1-12): FROI intake API and Vue 3 intake wizard, adjuster assignment rule engine, claim lifecycle state machine with reserve initialization, basic indemnity payment scheduling (TTD/TPD), employer partner portal read-only view, PostgreSQL schema with PHI encryption, AKS deployment with Azure DevOps CI/CD pipeline, HIPAA audit logging. Phase 2 Medical Integration (Weeks 13-20): Medical bill review queue and provider portal, Spring Batch PBM reconciliation with full idempotency and checkpoint, Azure API Management mTLS for PBM FHIR integrations, nurse case manager workflow, fee schedule validation service. Phase 3 Analytics and RTW (Weeks 21-26): RTW plan management and employer notification, analytics dashboard with reserve adequacy and adjuster caseload metrics, historical claim migration tooling for 50,000 legacy records with parallel-run validation, WCAB nightly export job. Phase 4 Advanced Features (Weeks 27-36): EDI state board filing integration, telemedicine referral workflow, PPD and PTD payment types, AI-assisted bill review anomaly detection.
target_output.development_guidelines: Java 21 with Spring Boot 3; Checkstyle and SpotBugs in CI; JUnit 5 with 80% line coverage gate enforced in Azure DevOps. Vue 3 components use Composition API with TypeScript strict mode; ESLint + Prettier enforced pre-commit. PHI fields must never appear in logs — Log4j2 message masking pattern for ssn, name, dob field names in structured output. Flyway migration scripts named V{version}__{description}.sql; irreversible migrations require DBA review. Spring Batch jobs must define unique JobParameters per execution including batch_date and run_id for idempotent restart. ADR documents required for state machine transition rule changes and reserve calculation methodology.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: PBM vendor FHIR R4 implementation divergence — vendors may return non-conformant bundles. Mitigation: FHIR validator in integration-service pre-processing pipeline with malformed bundle routing to dead letter queue and manual review. Risk: Azure Flexible Server zone-redundant failover latency causing Spring Batch job pause during nightly window. Mitigation: Spring Batch restart semantics recover from last checkpoint; failover window <30 seconds in zone-redundant configuration. Risk: mTLS certificate expiry causing PBM integration outage. Mitigation: Key Vault automated renewal with 30-day expiry alert and 14-day PagerDuty escalation. Tradeoff: Chunk-size 100 in Spring Batch balances memory usage against commit frequency — smaller chunks increase checkpoint overhead. Tradeoff: KEDA schedule scaler for batch-pool over HPA because batch jobs have predictable nightly schedule rather than demand-driven load patterns.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Spring Batch 5 over Quartz for PBM reconciliation due to built-in JobRepository checkpoint and native chunk-oriented processing. Confirmed Azure API Management Developer tier meets PBM mTLS integration volume. Deferred state EDI filing integration to Year 2 Phase 4 to stay within MVP timeline. Parallel-run validation period of 4 weeks added to legacy migration plan before mainframe cutover.

metadata.notes: Round 3 workers comp TPA platform addressing critical Spring Batch idempotency and mTLS PBM integration gaps from prior rounds.

profile.domain: Mobile app for independent insurance agents submitting personal auto and homeowners applications

project_goal.value: Mobile application enabling independent insurance agents to submit personal auto and homeowners applications, photograph property, receive real-time premium comparisons from multiple admitted carriers, and bind coverage in the field
project_goal.rationale: Core field productivity tool for rural agents with limited desktop access

target_users.value: Independent insurance agents, primarily rural agents submitting personal lines applications on mobile devices without reliable broadband
target_users.rationale: Target demographic identified in agent distribution channel research

project_class.rationale: React Native cross-platform app targeting iOS and Android for field agents

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Mobile frontend with backend API, carrier rating integrations, and usage analytics

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-carrier rating integration adds complexity but no real-time or AI components

risk_level.rationale: Personal applicant PII including SSN and driving records at submission stage

data_sensitivity.rationale: Applicant PII including name, address, SSN, and driving history collected at application

external_exposure.rationale: Auth0-authenticated agents only; no public access to application data

access_model.value: Auth0 with agent and agency_admin roles; agents access only their own submitted applications; agency_admin views agency-wide submission pipeline
access_model.rationale: Agency hierarchy requires scoped data access per licensed producer

feature_scope.value: Agent authentication, property photo capture and S3 upload, personal auto and homeowners application wizards, parallel carrier rating API calls, premium comparison display, offline draft persistence, application submission to carrier, policy draft creation
feature_scope.rationale: Full field workflow from photo to bound policy

mvp_scope.value: Agent login, manual application form entry for auto and homeowners, parallel rating API calls to two pilot carriers, premium comparison screen, and application submission to carrier
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP validates carrier integration pattern before photo prefill and more carriers

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 for all API calls, Auth0 JWT validation on all endpoints, PII fields encrypted at rest in device SQLite using SQLCipher, certificate pinning in React Native for carrier API calls
security_baseline.rationale: PII handling on mobile requires device-level encryption and certificate pinning

frontend_stack.value: React Native with TypeScript targeting iOS 16+ and Android 13+
frontend_stack.rationale: Cross-platform reduces maintenance cost for small engineering team

backend_stack.value: Node.js 20 with Express and TypeScript
backend_stack.rationale: Team JavaScript expertise; Express suits the integration-proxy pattern for carrier APIs

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on AWS RDS for server-side application storage; SQLite with SQLCipher on device for offline draft persistence
data_platform.rationale: Relational application records server-side; encrypted local storage for offline field use

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers; existing AWS footprint

privacy_retention_policy.value: Applicant PII retained 5 years after policy expiry per state insurance record-keeping requirements; abandoned drafts deleted after 90 days; carrier quote responses retained 1 year
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: State insurance producer record retention rules

future_scope.value: Property photo prefill via computer vision, additional carrier integrations, ACORD XML application export, agent performance analytics dashboard
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP roadmap contingent on pilot carrier integration success

constraints.value: iOS App Store and Google Play distribution; offline mode for rural low-connectivity areas; AWS budget under $2,000/month
constraints.rationale: Field agent use case requires offline resilience; small team budget constraint

observability_baseline.value: New Relic APM for backend API, New Relic Mobile agent for React Native crash and performance monitoring, structured JSON logging on API
observability_baseline.rationale: Single observability vendor across backend and mobile reduces tooling overhead

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints with Bitrise CI/CD for iOS and Android builds
execution_preference.rationale: Mobile CI requires dedicated build infrastructure

compliance_context.value: State insurance producer licensing requirements for agent license validation; FCRA compliance for any third-party prefill data usage in application prefill feature
compliance_context.rationale: Licensed agents must have license validated; prefill from consumer data sources triggers FCRA obligations

project.goal: Field mobile app for insurance agents to submit auto and homeowners applications and compare carrier premiums
project.target_users: Independent insurance agents with rural focus and intermittent connectivity
project.timeline: MVP in 4 months, photo prefill Phase 2 in Month 8

frontend.offline: WatermelonDB syncing to SQLite SQLCipher for offline drafts
frontend.state: Redux Toolkit for wizard and quote state

backend.framework: Express with TypeScript
backend.carrier_proxy: Parallel carrier rating calls via Promise.allSettled with 10s timeout

security.auth: Auth0 JWT Bearer on all endpoints
security.device_storage: SQLCipher for local PII via WatermelonDB
security.pinning: Certificate pinning for carrier API hostnames in React Native

data.cache: Redis 7 ElastiCache for carrier rating response cache 15 min TTL

devops.ci_mobile: Bitrise for iOS Xcode and Android Gradle builds

open_questions.photo_prefill: Vision API vendor selection deferred to Phase 2

reasoner_reviews.product: Agent productivity in the field is the core value driver. The application wizard must minimize required taps for rural agents who may be standing at a property while entering data. Offline draft persistence is a hard requirement given intermittent rural connectivity, and sync conflict resolution needs to be specified before development.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The carrier rating proxy using Promise.allSettled is appropriate — it handles partial carrier failures gracefully and returns available quotes without blocking on slow responders. Redis caching of rating responses for 15 minutes reduces redundant carrier API calls when agents adjust coverage options on the same application draft.
reasoner_reviews.security: Auth0 PKCE flow on mobile is the correct pattern. PII exposure in error logs and carrier API request payloads must be explicitly prevented via middleware field redaction. SQLCipher for device storage is the right control for offline PII. The FCRA consent screen must appear before any third-party prefill data fetch is triggered.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Two-carrier MVP is achievable in 4 months. The offline sync conflict resolution strategy for drafts edited both offline and subsequently online needs explicit specification — last-write-wins for drafts, server-timestamp-wins for submitted applications is the recommended approach.

backend.service_design: Express API with route modules: /auth (Auth0 middleware), /applications (CRUD for insurance applications), /ratings (carrier proxy orchestration via Promise.allSettled), /photos (S3 presigned URL generation), /agents (profile and agency management)
backend.api_patterns: REST JSON API; carrier rating fan-out via Promise.allSettled returning partial results with per-carrier carrier_status field; Redis 7 caches successful rating responses 15 min keyed by application_id and coverage_options hash
backend.failure_handling: Carrier API timeout returns partial quote set with carrier_status:timeout; Redis stale cache indicator on expired entries; dead letter queue for failed carrier submission retries

frontend.app_structure: React Native app with React Navigation v6; tab navigator for application list, new application, and agent profile; stack navigator for application wizard steps and premium comparison screen
frontend.pages_and_flows: Login, Application List with sync status badges, Auto Application Wizard (VIN entry, driver history, coverage limits), Homeowners Wizard (address, construction type, dwelling value), Premium Comparison grid, Photo Capture with upload progress, Submission Confirmation
frontend.state_management: Redux Toolkit for application wizard state and carrier quote results; WatermelonDB for offline-first draft persistence synced to server on connectivity restore

security.auth_design: Auth0 Universal Login with PKCE flow for mobile; JWT stored in iOS Keychain and Android EncryptedSharedPreferences via react-native-encrypted-storage library
security.authorization_model: Express middleware validates JWT sub matches agent_id on all /applications endpoints; agency_admin custom claim in Auth0 grants agency-wide read access to submission pipeline
security.abuse_prevention: Rate limiting on /ratings endpoint at 10 req/min per agent JWT to prevent carrier API quota exhaustion

data.entities: agent_profiles (id, auth0_sub, license_number, license_state, agency_id, created_at), insurance_applications (id, agent_id FK, application_type ENUM auto/homeowners, applicant_name, status ENUM draft/submitted/quoted/bound, submitted_at), quoted_premiums (id, application_id FK, carrier_code, annual_premium_cents, coverage_details_json, quoted_at, expires_at), property_photos (id, application_id FK, s3_key, upload_status ENUM pending/complete/failed, captured_at), carrier_appetite_rules (id, carrier_code, state, application_type, active)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ for server-side records; WatermelonDB on device syncs insurance_applications drafts to local SQLite SQLCipher database with last_sync_at and sync_status tracking columns

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate us-east-1 with ALB; single Express API service with 2 tasks baseline auto-scaling on CPU 70% target
devops.ci_cd_design: Bitrise workflow: iOS Xcode 15 archive and TestFlight export; Android Gradle 8 AAB signed and uploaded to Google Play Internal Track. GitHub Actions: TypeScript compile, Jest tests, Docker build and ECR push, ECS rolling deploy
devops.environments: dev, staging, production ECS services with separate RDS instances; production RDS Multi-AZ with automated 7-day backup retention

target_output.thinking_summary: Mobile-first insurance agent productivity tool. The carrier rating proxy pattern using Promise.allSettled gracefully handles partial carrier failures for rural agents with unreliable connectivity. Offline draft sync via WatermelonDB SQLCipher is the critical mobile-specific requirement. Auth0 PKCE is standard for mobile OAuth. Weak quality target means data model and observability sections will be lighter on detail.
target_output.title: Independent Agent Mobile Insurance Application Platform
target_output.executive_summary: React Native mobile application for independent insurance agents to submit personal auto and homeowners insurance applications and compare carrier premiums in the field. Node.js Express backend on ECS Fargate proxies parallel carrier rating API calls and returns side-by-side premium comparisons. Offline draft support via WatermelonDB with SQLCipher device encryption enables application entry in rural low-connectivity environments.
target_output.architecture_overview: React Native app authenticates via Auth0 PKCE and communicates over TLS 1.3 to a Node.js Express API on ECS Fargate behind ALB. The API routes application data to PostgreSQL 16 on RDS and fans out carrier rating requests in parallel using Promise.allSettled with a 10-second per-carrier timeout. S3 presigned URLs handle property photo uploads directly from the mobile client. Redis 7 caches carrier rating responses for 15 minutes keyed by application and coverage hash. Device-side SQLite SQLCipher via WatermelonDB provides offline draft persistence, syncing to the server when connectivity restores.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native, TypeScript, React Navigation v6, Redux Toolkit, WatermelonDB, SQLCipher, react-native-encrypted-storage. Backend: Node.js 20, Express, TypeScript, Auth0 JWT middleware. Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ, Redis 7 ElastiCache. Storage: S3 for property photos. Auth: Auth0 PKCE for mobile. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate us-east-1, ALB, RDS, ElastiCache, S3. CI/CD: Bitrise for mobile, GitHub Actions for backend. Observability: New Relic APM and New Relic Mobile.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Agent authentication with Auth0 PKCE and device secure credential storage. Personal auto application wizard capturing vehicle VINs, driver history, prior claims, and coverage limit selections. Homeowners application wizard capturing property address, construction type, year built, dwelling replacement value, and liability limits. Parallel carrier rating fan-out returning side-by-side annual premium comparison. Offline draft persistence with automatic server sync on connectivity restore. Property photo capture and S3 upload for underwriting documentation. Application submission to selected carrier with policy draft confirmation.
target_output.system_components: ALB routing HTTPS to ECS Fargate Express API tasks. Express API with modules for applications, ratings proxy, photos, and agents. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ for persisted application and quote records. Redis 7 ElastiCache for carrier rating response cache keyed by application and coverage hash. S3 bucket with presigned URL generation for direct mobile photo uploads. Auth0 tenant for agent PKCE authentication. Bitrise CI for React Native iOS and Android build pipelines.
target_output.workflows: Field Application Submission: Agent opens app, selects application type (auto or homeowners), completes wizard entering vehicle or property details and applicant information. App calls POST /api/v1/ratings; backend fans out to carrier rating APIs via Promise.allSettled with 10s timeout per carrier; available quotes returned to Premium Comparison screen. Agent selects preferred carrier and submits via POST /api/v1/applications/:id/submit, receiving policy draft confirmation. Offline Draft Sync: Agent enters application data in low-connectivity area; WatermelonDB writes draft to local SQLite SQLCipher database. On connectivity restore, sync adapter issues PUT /api/v1/applications/:id/sync; server applies server-timestamp-wins for submitted records and last-write-wins for unsubmitted drafts.
target_output.data_model: agent_profiles (id, auth0_sub, license_number, license_state, agency_id, created_at). insurance_applications (id, agent_id FK, application_type ENUM auto/homeowners, applicant_name, status ENUM draft/submitted/quoted/bound, submitted_at). quoted_premiums (id, application_id FK, carrier_code, annual_premium_cents, coverage_details_json, quoted_at, expires_at). property_photos (id, application_id FK, s3_key, upload_status ENUM pending/complete/failed, captured_at). carrier_appetite_rules (id, carrier_code, state, application_type, active).
target_output.api_design: POST /api/v1/applications (create draft). GET /api/v1/applications (agent list filtered by status). GET /api/v1/applications/:id (full detail). PUT /api/v1/applications/:id/sync (offline draft sync). POST /api/v1/ratings (fan-out carrier rating calls, returns quoted_premiums array with per-carrier carrier_status). POST /api/v1/applications/:id/submit (carrier submission). POST /api/v1/photos/presigned (generate S3 presigned upload URL). All endpoints require Auth0 Bearer JWT. Rate limit on /ratings: 10 req/min per agent.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Auth0 PKCE flow; JWT stored in iOS Keychain and Android EncryptedSharedPreferences. SQLCipher encrypts local SQLite database containing offline applicant PII. TLS 1.3 for all API and carrier calls. Certificate pinning configured in React Native network config for carrier API hostnames. Express middleware strips ssn, dob, and license_number from structured log output. FCRA compliance: consent screen presented before any third-party prefill data fetch. State producer licensing: agent license_number and license_state validated against carrier_appetite_rules before application submission to carrier.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions backend CI: TypeScript compile, Jest unit tests with 75% coverage gate, Docker build and ECR push, ECS rolling update with health check validation. Bitrise mobile CI: iOS build on macOS Xcode 15 agent with archive and TestFlight export lane. Android build on Gradle 8 with signed AAB upload to Google Play Internal Track. Environments: dev, staging, production ECS services with separate RDS instances. Production RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ with 7-day automated backup retention. AWS Secrets Manager for database credentials and carrier API keys with automatic rotation.
target_output.observability: New Relic APM on Express API tracking carrier rating proxy latency per carrier_code, p99 latency on /applications endpoints, and Redis cache hit rate. New Relic Mobile agent on React Native tracking crash rate by app version, offline sync success rate by connectivity type, and photo upload p95 latency. Custom PagerDuty alerts: carrier_rating_timeout_rate >20%, application_submission_failure_rate >5%. Structured JSON logs with application_id and agent_id correlation fields.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate 2-task baseline: ~$150/month. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ db.t4g.medium: ~$180/month. ElastiCache Redis t4g.micro: ~$25/month. S3 at 50 GB photo storage: ~$15/month. New Relic standard tier: ~$200/month. Total ~$570/month well within $2,000/month budget. Scale path: ECS auto-scale on CPU 70% supports 500 concurrent agents without architecture changes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (Weeks 1-8): Agent authentication, auto and homeowners application forms, parallel rating proxy for two pilot carriers, premium comparison screen, application submission to carrier, PostgreSQL schema, ECS deployment, Bitrise CI pipeline. Phase 2 (Weeks 9-16): Property photo capture and S3 upload, offline draft persistence with WatermelonDB SQLCipher, connectivity-restore sync with conflict resolution. Phase 3 (Weeks 17-24): Additional carrier integrations beyond two pilots, property photo prefill via computer vision API, ACORD XML export, agent performance analytics dashboard.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode on backend and React Native. ESLint Airbnb config with React Native plugin enforced pre-commit. Jest for backend unit tests with 75% coverage minimum. Detox for React Native E2E on iOS simulator for application wizard and offline sync flows. PII fields must not appear in logs — Express middleware strips ssn, dob, license_number from request logging. Carrier API keys stored only in AWS Secrets Manager, never committed to source code.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Carrier API downtime during field quote session. Mitigation: Promise.allSettled returns partial results; Redis stale cache serves quotes within 15-minute window with stale indicator displayed. Risk: Offline sync conflict when draft edited on mobile and web simultaneously. Mitigation: server-timestamp-wins for submitted applications, last-write-wins for unsubmitted drafts. Risk: SQLCipher passphrase loss on device wipe loses unsynced drafts. Mitigation: server-side backup means submitted applications are safe; agents warned before wipe. Tradeoff: WatermelonDB over AsyncStorage for offline provides proper SQLite sync primitives at the cost of a heavier dependency.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Auth0 PKCE over Cognito for superior React Native SDK support. Confirmed SQLCipher via WatermelonDB for offline PII storage meeting security baseline. Carrier integration pattern settled as parallel REST proxy rather than ACORD XML for MVP delivery speed. Photo prefill computer vision vendor deferred to Phase 3 pending pilot carrier feedback.

metadata.notes: Round 1 mobile insurance agent app; data model and observability intentionally thin for weak quality target.

profile.domain: Public knowledge hub for a cyber insurance carrier publishing risk advisories and ransomware trend reports

project_goal.value: Public knowledge hub for a cyber insurance carrier publishing risk advisories, incident response guides, and ransomware trend reports for policyholders and the broader security community, designed to handle traffic spikes following major cybersecurity incidents
project_goal.rationale: Thought leadership and policyholder support content platform replacing static HTML pages

target_users.value: Cyber insurance policyholders, enterprise security practitioners, incident responders, and the broader security research community
target_users.rationale: Primary readers identified in policyholder communications research

project_class.rationale: Content-only site with no server-side runtime; all pages statically exported via Next.js

capabilities.rationale: No backend runtime; analytics via Cloudflare Analytics for traffic pattern visibility

complexity_level.rationale: Static content delivery with CDN; no authentication, payments, or real-time features

risk_level.rationale: No user data collected; read-only public content; no authentication or PII

data_sensitivity.rationale: No personal or financial data; all content is publicly published

external_exposure.rationale: Open public access; no authentication required for any content

access_model.value: Anonymous public read access to all content; CMS access restricted to authenticated security researchers and content editors via Contentful
access_model.rationale: All advisories and reports are intended for broad distribution

feature_scope.value: Risk advisory pages by threat category and industry sector, incident response guide library, ransomware trend reports, author profile pages, full-text search via Pagefind, RSS feed for new advisories, sitemap
feature_scope.rationale: Full content discovery and subscription features for security community

mvp_scope.value: Risk advisory listing and detail pages, incident response guide library, ransomware trend report pages, full-text static search via Pagefind, Cloudflare Pages deployment
mvp_scope.rationale: Core content delivery without RSS or author profiles for initial launch

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced via Cloudflare Pages, strict Content-Security-Policy header, X-Frame-Options deny, no user input forms on site, Cloudflare Bot Management for crawl protection
security_baseline.rationale: Minimal attack surface for a read-only public static site

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript in static export mode (output: 'export')
frontend_stack.rationale: Next.js static export generates HTML at build time; no Node.js server runtime on Cloudflare Pages

backend_stack.value: None; headless CMS via Contentful for content authoring; no server-side runtime
backend_stack.rationale: Static site requires no backend runtime; content pulled at build time

data_platform.value: None; Contentful as headless CMS; Pagefind client-side search index built at compile time
data_platform.rationale: No database; content managed in Contentful and baked into static output

hosting_target.value: Cloudflare Pages on Cloudflare global edge network
hosting_target.rationale: Edge CDN absorption of surge traffic during major cyber events without origin server scaling

privacy_retention_policy.value: No user data collected or stored; Cloudflare Analytics aggregated metrics retained 90 days; no cookies set on site visitors
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR-friendly analytics with no personal data collection; no retention obligations

future_scope.value: Policyholder-gated content section requiring Cloudflare Access authentication, newsletter subscription integration, multilingual content support
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap after initial public launch

constraints.value: No server-side runtime permitted on Cloudflare Pages deployment; Contentful free tier for initial launch; static export must complete in under 3 minutes for post-incident rapid deployment
constraints.rationale: Cloudflare Pages static hosting constraint; rapid deployment required after major cyber events

observability_baseline.value: Cloudflare Analytics for traffic patterns and geographic distribution; Cloudflare Pages deploy notifications via Slack webhook; no APM needed for static delivery
observability_baseline.rationale: Static site observability limited to CDN metrics and deployment alerts

execution_preference.value: GitHub Actions CI triggered on Contentful publish webhook; automated Cloudflare Pages deploy on main branch push
execution_preference.rationale: Content editors publish advisories in Contentful and site rebuilds automatically within 2 minutes

project.goal: Surge-resilient public cyber insurance knowledge hub built on edge CDN with automated CMS-driven rebuild pipeline
project.target_users: Policyholders, enterprise security practitioners, incident responders, security researchers
project.timeline: Launch in 6 weeks, Phase 2 gated content in Q3

frontend.search: Pagefind client-side search index built during Next.js build
frontend.content: Contentful headless CMS via content delivery API at build time

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with Contentful webhook trigger

constraints.no_server_runtime: Static export only; no API routes or server actions
constraints.build_time: Under 3 minutes for rapid post-incident deploy

reasoner_reviews.product: The primary use case is surge traffic absorption during major cyber events such as a large-scale ransomware campaign. The site must be available and fast globally within minutes of a new advisory publication. Contentful webhook-triggered builds meet this need. Round 1 plan lacked explicit Pagefind integration detail and the Contentful webhook trigger pipeline was underspecified.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Next.js 14 static export to Cloudflare Pages is the right architecture. The key constraint is that no API routes or server components can be used — output: 'export' disables the Node.js runtime entirely. Round 1 plan correctly used static export but omitted the Pagefind index build step within the GitHub Actions workflow, which is needed for full-text search on the published site.
reasoner_reviews.security: Minimal attack surface for a read-only static site. Content-Security-Policy must restrict script-src to self and Cloudflare analytics domain only to prevent XSS from any injected content. Cloudflare Bot Management handles scraping. No cookies and no forms means GDPR exposure is minimal.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Build time under 3 minutes is achievable with Contentful delivery API and incremental static regeneration — but ISR is incompatible with output: 'export'. The team must use on-demand revalidation via Contentful webhook triggering a full rebuild, which should complete in under 90 seconds for the current content volume.

security.auth_design: No user authentication on public site; Contentful CMS access restricted to named editor accounts with SSO; Cloudflare Access optional for future gated policyholder section
security.abuse_prevention: Cloudflare Bot Management active on all page routes; rate limiting on Cloudflare layer for repeated automated access patterns; no server-side endpoints to attack
security.privacy_controls: No cookies set on site visitors; Cloudflare Analytics uses cookieless measurement; privacy policy page references aggregated-only analytics

data.entities: risk_advisories (id, slug, title, threat_category, industry_sectors[], published_at, severity_level ENUM critical/high/medium/informational, body_rich_text, author_profile_id), incident_reports (id, slug, title, incident_type, affected_industry, published_at, executive_summary, full_report_body), threat_categories (id, slug, name, description, parent_category_id), industry_sectors (id, slug, name), author_profiles (id, slug, name, title, bio), resource_guides (id, slug, title, guide_type ENUM incident_response/ransomware/phishing, body_rich_text, updated_at)
data.storage_design: All content managed in Contentful; fetched via Contentful Delivery API at build time using next-contentful-sdk; no runtime database
data.retention_and_deletion: Content lifecycle managed in Contentful; published advisories retained indefinitely; draft content never included in static build output

devops.deployment_topology: Cloudflare Pages project connected to GitHub repo; production branch is main; preview deployments on feature branches for content review

devops.rollback_strategy: Cloudflare Pages retains last 10 deployments; instant rollback via Cloudflare dashboard or wrangler pages deployment rollback command

ISS-001.title: Pagefind index build step missing from GitHub Actions workflow
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan described Pagefind as the search solution but the GitHub Actions workflow section did not include the npx pagefind --site ./out step after the Next.js static export. Without this step the search index is not included in the Cloudflare Pages deployment and full-text search returns no results on the live site.

history.note: Deployment section mentioned Pagefind but omitted the CLI build step from the Actions workflow

ISS-002.title: Contentful webhook trigger pipeline not specified
ISS-002.detail: The round 1 plan stated that content editors publish in Contentful and the site rebuilds automatically, but did not specify the repository_dispatch webhook configuration in Contentful or the GitHub Actions workflow trigger that listens for the content.published event type.

history.note: CMS trigger described in prose but webhook configuration mechanics absent

focus_issues.title: Pagefind index build step missing from GitHub Actions workflow
focus_issues.detail: Pagefind CLI must run after Next.js static export to generate the search index in ./out/_pagefind before Cloudflare Pages deployment.

Specify Contentful webhook repository_dispatch configuration with content.published event type and GitHub Actions on: repository_dispatch trigger

previous_audits.summary: Strong overall architecture for a surge-resilient static site. Content model and Cloudflare Pages topology are correct. Deployment pipeline has two actionable gaps that prevent the site from functioning as described.

Contentful as headless CMS with build-time data fetch is the right pattern for editorial workflow
Cloudflare Bot Management and CSP headers appropriately sized for a read-only public site

Contentful publish webhook trigger mechanics not specified — editors cannot trigger site rebuilds from CMS

Document Contentful webhook configuration: repository_dispatch event type content.published with GitHub Personal Access Token scoped to repo scope

Strong quality means all sections need rich, site-specific content.
