target_output.title: Cyber Insurance Knowledge Hub
target_output.executive_summary: Surge-resilient static knowledge hub for a cyber insurance carrier built with Next.js 14 static export deployed to Cloudflare global edge network via Cloudflare Pages. Publishes risk advisories, incident response guides, and ransomware trend reports to policyholders and the security community. Content authored in Contentful triggers automated GitHub Actions rebuilds including Pagefind full-text search index generation, deploying globally within 2 minutes of publication. No server-side runtime; all content baked at build time.
target_output.architecture_overview: Jamstack architecture with Next.js 14 in static export mode (output: 'export') generating a fully pre-rendered HTML/CSS/JS bundle at build time. Cloudflare Pages serves the bundle from 300+ edge PoPs globally, absorbing traffic spikes from major cyber events without origin autoscaling concerns. Contentful headless CMS provides editorial authoring; content fetched via Contentful Delivery API during Next.js build using generateStaticParams for advisory and report pages. Pagefind generates a client-side full-text search index from the static output during CI. Cloudflare Bot Management protects against automated scraping. No cookies, no user data, no server-side runtime.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 TypeScript static export, Tailwind CSS, React 18. Content: Contentful headless CMS, next-contentful-sdk for typed content fetching at build time. Search: Pagefind client-side search index built from static output. CDN: Cloudflare Pages on global edge network. Analytics: Cloudflare Web Analytics cookieless measurement. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with bun, wrangler CLI. Security headers: next.config.js headers array with CSP, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Risk Advisory Hub: listing page filterable by threat_category (ransomware, phishing, supply_chain, cloud_breach) and industry_sector; individual advisory pages with severity badge, affected industries, recommended mitigations, and related resources. Incident Response Guide Library: guides indexed by guide_type with print-optimized layout for on-call practitioners. Ransomware Trend Report pages with year-over-year statistics and carrier claims data visualizations using static SVG charts. Author Profile pages linking advisories by researcher. Full-Text Search via Pagefind covering advisory titles, body text, and guide content. RSS feed at /advisories/rss.xml generated as a static file during Next.js build.
target_output.system_components: Contentful CMS space with content model for risk_advisories, incident_reports, resource_guides, threat_categories, industry_sectors, and author_profiles. Next.js 14 static build process: generateStaticParams per content type, Contentful Delivery API fetch, Tailwind CSS JIT compilation, static HTML output to ./out. Pagefind CLI indexer running over ./out after Next.js build. Cloudflare Pages project connected to GitHub main branch and feature branch previews. GitHub Actions workflow triggered by push and Contentful repository_dispatch webhook. Cloudflare Analytics and Bot Management on all routes.
target_output.workflows: Content Publication to Live Site: Security researcher authors new ransomware advisory in Contentful, selects threat_category tags and industry_sectors, sets severity_level, and clicks Publish. Contentful fires repository_dispatch webhook to GitHub with event_type content.published and content_type risk_advisory payload. GitHub Actions workflow triggers: bun install, bun run build (Next.js static export fetching updated Contentful content), npx pagefind --source ./out --bundle-path ./_pagefind (search index generation), wrangler pages publish ./out --project-name cyber-knowledge-hub. Total pipeline completes in under 90 seconds; new advisory live globally on Cloudflare edge. Surge Traffic Absorption: Major ransomware campaign breaks in the news; traffic to /advisories/ransomware spikes 50x normal. Cloudflare Pages edge serves pre-rendered HTML from nearest PoP with no origin involvement; Bot Management throttles automated scrapers; Cloudflare Analytics records real-time traffic spike by country and page.
target_output.data_model: All content stored in Contentful; fetched at build time and embedded in static HTML. Content types: risk_advisories (id, slug, title, threat_category ContentfulRef, industry_sectors ContentfulRef[], severity_level ENUM critical/high/medium/informational, body_rich_text, author_profile ContentfulRef, published_at, last_reviewed_at). incident_reports (id, slug, title, incident_type, affected_industry, executive_summary, full_report_body, published_at). resource_guides (id, slug, title, guide_type ENUM incident_response/ransomware/phishing/cloud, body_rich_text, updated_at). threat_categories (id, slug, name, parent_category ContentfulRef). industry_sectors (id, slug, name). author_profiles (id, slug, name, title, organization, bio_short). Pagefind index derived from static HTML at build time; no runtime database.
target_output.api_design: No runtime API. Build-time data access only via Contentful Delivery API: GET https://cdn.contentful.com/spaces/{spaceId}/entries?content_type=riskAdvisory&order=-fields.publishedAt fetching all advisories for static page generation. GET /spaces/{spaceId}/entries?content_type=resourceGuide for guide library. RSS feed at /advisories/rss.xml generated as a static XML file during Next.js build using generateStaticParams with Contentful data. Pagefind search executes entirely client-side against the pre-built /_pagefind/index.js bundle with no server requests.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Cloudflare Pages with HSTS preload. Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' static.cloudflareinsights.com; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: images.ctfassets.net; connect-src 'self' cloudflareinsights.com. X-Frame-Options: DENY. Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin. Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=(). Cloudflare Bot Management blocks automated scraping and DDoS on edge layer. No cookies set on site visitors; Cloudflare Analytics uses cookieless pixel. No user input forms; no XSS attack surface beyond CSP headers. GDPR: no personal data collected; privacy policy documents aggregated-analytics-only measurement.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions workflow (.github/workflows/deploy.yml): triggers on push to main and on repository_dispatch with event_type: content.published. Steps: (1) actions/checkout@v4; (2) oven-sh/setup-bun@v1; (3) bun install --frozen-lockfile; (4) bun run build (next build with output: 'export' generating ./out); (5) npx pagefind --source ./out --bundle-path ./_pagefind --glob '**/*.html' (builds search index in-place in ./out); (6) wrangler pages publish ./out --project-name cyber-knowledge-hub --branch main. Contentful Webhook: configured in Contentful space settings to POST to https://api.github.com/repos/org/repo/dispatches with Authorization: Bearer {GITHUB_TOKEN} and JSON body {event_type:'content.published'}. Environments: Cloudflare Pages preview deployments auto-created on feature branches for content preview before merging. Cloudflare Pages retains 10 prior deployments for instant rollback via dashboard. Build time target: under 90 seconds for rapid post-incident advisory deployment.
target_output.observability: Cloudflare Web Analytics cookieless dashboard: page views per advisory, top-10 most-read risk advisories by week, geographic traffic distribution by country, spike detection during named cyber events. Cloudflare Pages build duration metric monitored via Cloudflare Pages API; Slack webhook notified on build failure or build duration exceeding 3 minutes. Custom metric tracked in advisory page metadata: last_reviewed_at compared to published_at to flag advisories not reviewed in 12+ months for content freshness audit. No APM needed; no runtime to instrument.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloudflare Pages free tier: unlimited bandwidth and 500 builds/month — zero CDN cost for expected traffic volumes including surge events. Contentful community tier: 25,000 API calls per month at build time; free for current content volume. GitHub Actions: ~150 build minutes per month at $0.008/minute = ~$1.20/month. Total monthly cost under $5. Surge traffic handled transparently by Cloudflare edge at no incremental cost. Scale path: Contentful Growth tier at $300/month if content volume exceeds free tier API limits.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Launch (Weeks 1-3): Next.js 14 static export with Contentful content model, risk advisory and incident report pages, Tailwind CSS styling, Pagefind search index in GitHub Actions CI, Cloudflare Pages deployment, CSP and security headers, Cloudflare Analytics. Phase 2 Full Content (Weeks 4-6): Ransomware trend report pages with static SVG chart components, resource guide library with print styles, author profile pages, RSS feed generation, Contentful webhook rebuild trigger, preview branch deployments for content review. Phase 3 Policyholder Gated Content (Q3): Cloudflare Access integration for authenticated policyholder section, newsletter subscription via Buttondown API, multilingual content support via Contentful locale API.
target_output.development_guidelines: Next.js output must remain 'export' — no server components, no API routes, no dynamic routes without generateStaticParams. All Contentful fetches must use the typed next-contentful-sdk client generated from Contentful content types. Tailwind CSS classes must be co-located in component files; no arbitrary value classes without design token justification. Pagefind attributes (data-pagefind-body, data-pagefind-meta) added to advisory and guide page templates for accurate search indexing. bun run build must complete locally before any PR merge. Security header configuration in next.config.js reviewed on every PR touching CSP.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Contentful Delivery API rate limit during full site rebuild if advisory count exceeds 1,000. Mitigation: Contentful pagination in generateStaticParams with 100-entry pages; content delivery API limits 55 req/s sufficient for current volume. Risk: Pagefind index size grows with content volume, slowing client-side search. Mitigation: Pagefind supports chunked index loading; acceptable for current content size; re-evaluate at 500+ advisories. Risk: Build time exceeds 3-minute target as content volume grows. Mitigation: Contentful Preview API with incremental content fetching; evaluate Cloudflare Pages Smart Placement if build bottleneck emerges. Tradeoff: Full static rebuild on every Contentful publish versus ISR — ISR requires server runtime incompatible with Cloudflare Pages static hosting; full rebuild is simpler and under 90 seconds for current content volume.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Pagefind over Algolia for search — zero runtime cost and no API calls on search; sufficient for advisory content volume. Contentful selected over Sanity for team familiarity and typed SDK generation. RSS feed implemented as a static file in Next.js generateStaticParams rather than a dynamic API route, maintaining static-only constraint. Preview deployments on feature branches replace staging environment for content review.

metadata.notes: Round 2 cyber insurance static knowledge hub; resolves Pagefind CI build step gap from round 1; surge-resilient Cloudflare Pages architecture.

profile.domain: MLOps infrastructure for a personal lines carrier training and deploying actuarial ML pricing models

project_goal.value: MLOps infrastructure for a personal lines carrier to train, version, validate, and deploy actuarial machine learning models used in auto insurance pricing and claim propensity scoring, enabling data science teams to iterate on pricing models without manual deployment hand-offs
project_goal.rationale: Core actuarial technology platform replacing manual model promotion hand-off process

target_users.value: Actuarial data scientists authoring pricing models, MLOps engineers operating training pipelines, and pricing platform engineers consuming deployed model APIs internally
target_users.rationale: Internal-only platform with distinct data science, MLOps, and consuming engineering personas

project_class.rationale: Platform and environment architecture: GKE, MLflow, Kubeflow Pipelines, feature store — not a user-facing application

capabilities.rationale: No frontend; infrastructure platform with training batch jobs, data engineering, and deployment automation

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-model versioning, champion-challenger traffic splitting, fairness benchmark gating, and production pricing API integration

risk_level.rationale: Deployed pricing models directly affect insurance premiums charged to consumers; errors have regulatory and financial consequence

data_sensitivity.rationale: Training data contains proprietary actuarial loss experience, driver telematics, and policyholder behavioral data

external_exposure.rationale: MLOps platform and model serving APIs are internal-only; consumed by pricing microservices within VPC

access_model.value: Google IAM with data scientist, mlops_engineer, and pricing_consumer roles; Workload Identity for GKE pod-level service account access to GCS and BigQuery; no external access
access_model.rationale: Least-privilege IAM with Workload Identity prevents static credential usage in training containers

feature_scope.value: Feature store for actuarial feature computation and versioning, automated model training pipelines on Kubeflow, MLflow experiment tracking and model registry, fairness and stability validation gates, champion-challenger traffic splitting for production pricing, model performance monitoring with loss ratio impact dashboards, data scientist self-service promotion workflow
feature_scope.rationale: Full MLOps lifecycle from feature engineering through production monitoring

mvp_scope.value: GKE Autopilot cluster, MLflow tracking server with GCS artifact store, Kubeflow Pipelines for training automation, basic fairness and stability validation gates, manual promotion to staging model serving, Terraform IaC for infrastructure
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP establishes the automated training and validation pipeline before champion-challenger and loss ratio monitoring

security_baseline.value: Google IAM with Workload Identity for all GKE workloads; training data in GCS with CMEK encryption; VPC Service Controls for BigQuery and GCS perimeter; audit logging for all model promotions and data access; no internet-egress from training pods
security_baseline.rationale: Confidential actuarial data requires VPC Service Controls and audit trail for regulatory examination

frontend_stack.value: None; internal platform with no user-facing frontend; MLflow UI served internally for data scientist experiment tracking
frontend_stack.rationale: Infrastructure project with no consumer UI

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with MLflow 2.x, Kubeflow Pipelines SDK, and FastAPI model serving endpoints
backend_stack.rationale: Python ecosystem standard for actuarial ML; MLflow provides experiment and registry integration

data_platform.value: BigQuery for actuarial training datasets and feature store; GCS for model artifacts and training data snapshots; Vertex AI Feature Store for online feature serving to model inference API
data_platform.rationale: GCP-native data stack; BigQuery for large actuarial table processing; Vertex Feature Store for low-latency inference feature lookup

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot in us-central1 with node pools for training and serving workloads
hosting_target.rationale: Autopilot manages node provisioning for variable training batch demand; serving pods require stable node allocation

privacy_retention_policy.value: Training datasets retained 5 years per actuarial data retention policy; model artifacts retained indefinitely in MLflow registry with deprecation tagging; feature store snapshots retained 2 years; PII-derived features de-identified before training
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Actuarial data retention for rate filing examination; PII de-identification for proxy variable compliance

future_scope.value: Real-time telematics feature streaming via Pub/Sub for usage-based insurance models, automated rate filing report generation from model artifacts, model explainability API for regulatory examination requests
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap after MVP model pipeline is operational

constraints.value: All training data must remain within GCP us-central1 region; champion-challenger traffic split must be configurable without redeployment; fairness benchmarks must block promotion if proxy variable correlation exceeds NAIC AI white paper thresholds
constraints.rationale: Data residency, regulatory agility, and NAIC AI fairness compliance constraints

observability_baseline.value: Grafana dashboards with Prometheus metrics from GKE and model serving pods; MLflow experiment tracking for training run metrics; custom loss ratio impact dashboard comparing champion vs challenger model predictions post-production
observability_baseline.rationale: Actuarial model monitoring requires loss ratio impact visibility beyond standard API metrics

execution_preference.value: Terraform for all infrastructure provisioning; Cloud Build for CI/CD pipelines; GitOps with ArgoCD for GKE workload deployment
execution_preference.rationale: Infrastructure as code and GitOps prevents configuration drift in model serving environment

compliance_context.value: State insurance rate filing requirements for actuarial model documentation; NAIC Artificial Intelligence white paper guidelines for fairness and transparency; fair lending and proxy variable regulations prohibiting use of protected class proxies in pricing
compliance_context.rationale: Actuarial pricing models are subject to state rate review and NAIC AI governance standards

project.goal: Automated MLOps platform for actuarial pricing model lifecycle management on GCP
project.target_users: Actuarial data scientists, MLOps engineers, pricing platform engineers
project.timeline: MVP in 10 weeks, champion-challenger in Week 18

backend.frameworks: MLflow 2.x, Kubeflow Pipelines SDK, FastAPI for model serving
backend.orchestration: Kubeflow Pipelines on GKE for training DAGs

data.feature_store: Vertex AI Feature Store for online serving features
data.warehouse: BigQuery for training datasets and actuarial tables
data.artifact_store: GCS with CMEK encryption for model artifacts and training snapshots

security.iam: Google IAM with Workload Identity for GKE service accounts
security.perimeter: VPC Service Controls for BigQuery and GCS
security.audit: Cloud Audit Logs for all data access and model promotion events

devops.iac: Terraform for all GCP resource provisioning

constraints.fairness_gate: Proxy variable correlation threshold from NAIC AI white paper

open_questions.telematics_streaming: Real-time Pub/Sub feature ingestion deferred to Phase 2

reasoner_reviews.product: The core value is eliminating manual model promotion hand-offs. The data science team currently emails model artifacts to the MLOps team who manually deploy to pricing servers. Automated Kubeflow Pipelines with MLflow registry integration and validation gates will reduce the promotion cycle from 2 weeks to same-day for models that pass benchmarks.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: GKE Autopilot with Kubeflow Pipelines is the right choice for variable training batch demand. The champion-challenger framework requires a traffic-splitting layer in the model serving tier — a FastAPI serving gateway that reads split configuration from a versioned config in GCS or Cloud Storage, updated by a promotion workflow without redeployment. MLflow model registry staging and production aliases drive the promotion workflow.
reasoner_reviews.security: VPC Service Controls for BigQuery and GCS are critical — training data includes proprietary loss experience that must not egress to internet. Workload Identity eliminates static GCP credentials in training containers. All model promotion events must be logged to Cloud Audit Logs for rate filing examination. PII-derived features must be de-identified via BigQuery Data Masking before inclusion in training datasets.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Fairness benchmark gating against NAIC AI thresholds requires a reproducible benchmark dataset held out from training and versioned in MLflow. The champion-challenger traffic split configuration must be stored in a runtime-readable location (Cloud Storage JSON config) and loaded by the serving gateway on each request routing decision without pod restart.

backend.service_design: Three Python service components: (1) training-pipeline — Kubeflow Pipelines DAG components for data fetch, feature engineering, model training, and validation; (2) model-serving-gateway — FastAPI service on GKE serving-pool routing requests to champion or challenger model replicas based on traffic split config from GCS; (3) promotion-workflow — Python script triggered by MLflow registry stage transition webhook to update champion-challenger config in GCS
backend.api_patterns: Internal REST API at /v1/predict/{model_name} on model-serving-gateway; traffic split config read from GCS on each request routing decision; MLflow Registry REST API for model lifecycle management
backend.background_jobs: Kubeflow Pipelines training DAGs scheduled via KFP SDK CronSchedule: weekly retraining for auto pricing model, monthly for claim propensity model; post-training fairness validation component runs as last DAG step before MLflow registration
backend.failure_handling: Kubeflow Pipeline step retry with exponential backoff on transient BigQuery errors; fairness gate failure terminates pipeline and notifies data scientist via Cloud Pub/Sub notification; model serving gateway falls back to champion model if challenger replica unhealthy

data.entities: model_experiments (mlflow_run_id, model_name, model_version, training_dataset_snapshot_gcs_path, hyperparameters_json, metrics_json, fairness_scores_json, registered_at), training_datasets (id, dataset_name, snapshot_date, bigquery_table_ref, row_count, feature_hash, created_at), feature_stores (feature_name, feature_version, entity_type ENUM policy/driver/vehicle, computation_sql, last_updated_at), model_versions (mlflow_model_version, model_name, stage ENUM staging/production/archived, promoted_by, promoted_at, champion_challenger_split_pct), validation_benchmarks (id, benchmark_name, benchmark_dataset_gcs_path, threshold_config_json, last_run_at), pricing_overrides (id, model_name, override_factor, reason_code, approved_by, effective_date, expiry_date), champion_challenger_configs (id, model_name, champion_version, challenger_version, challenger_traffic_pct, config_updated_at)
data.storage_design: BigQuery dataset actuarial_training for training table snapshots; GCS bucket model-artifacts-{env} with CMEK for MLflow artifact store; GCS bucket champion-challenger-configs for traffic split JSON files; Vertex AI Feature Store entity types for policy, driver, and vehicle online feature serving
data.retention_and_deletion: Training dataset BigQuery snapshots retained 5 years with table expiration; model artifacts in GCS retained indefinitely with MLflow archived stage for deprecated versions; feature store snapshots 2-year retention

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot us-central1 with three node pools: training-pool (n2-highmem-8, 0-10 nodes KEDA scaled by Kubeflow queue depth), serving-pool (n2-standard-4, 3-6 nodes HPA on prediction request rate), mlflow-pool (n2-standard-2, 2 nodes for MLflow tracking server). ArgoCD manages Helm releases for each workload. Terraform manages GKE, BigQuery datasets, GCS buckets, Vertex AI Feature Store, VPC Service Controls perimeter.
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build trigger on model training code PR merge: (1) Python unit tests for pipeline components; (2) Docker build and push to Artifact Registry; (3) Kubeflow Pipeline compile and upload to KFP endpoint. ArgoCD syncs Helm chart updates to GKE on GitOps push to model-serving chart. Promotion workflow triggered by MLflow registry webhook via Cloud Run function.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus metrics from GKE workloads scraped every 30s; Grafana dashboards: training pipeline duration by model_name, validation benchmark pass/fail rate, serving gateway p50/p99 latency by model_name, champion vs challenger prediction distribution. Cloud Audit Logs forwarded to BigQuery for model promotion audit trail queryable for rate filing.
devops.rollback_strategy: Model rollback: MLflow registry stage transition to revert champion to previous version; promotion-workflow re-writes champion-challenger config in GCS within 60 seconds. Infrastructure rollback: ArgoCD sync to previous Git revision for GKE workload rollback; Terraform state backup in GCS for infrastructure rollback.

security.auth_design: Google IAM roles: roles/bigquery.dataViewer for training-pipeline service account on actuarial datasets; roles/storage.objectAdmin for model-artifacts bucket; Workload Identity binding between GKE service accounts and GCP service accounts per workload
security.secrets_management: Secret Manager for MLflow DB credentials and Vertex AI API keys; External Secrets Operator syncing secrets to GKE namespaces; no static credentials in training container images or Kubeflow component definitions
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Cloud Audit Logs data access logs enabled on BigQuery actuarial datasets and GCS model artifact buckets; custom Cloud Logging log sink for model promotion events forwarded to BigQuery audit_events table for 5-year retention; Pub/Sub notification on any model promotion to production stage

target_output.thinking_summary: This is an MLOps infrastructure platform for actuarial pricing models — no frontend, no user authentication flows, no web app patterns. The key design decisions are: Kubeflow Pipelines for training DAG orchestration with KEDA scaling on training-pool, MLflow model registry with staging/production stage aliases driving promotion workflow, FastAPI serving gateway with GCS-backed champion-challenger traffic split config for zero-downtime split changes, and VPC Service Controls perimeter protecting confidential actuarial training data. Strong quality requires detailed Terraform topology, fairness gate specifics, and NAIC compliance documentation.
target_output.title: Actuarial ML Model Platform — Auto Pricing and Claim Propensity MLOps Infrastructure
target_output.executive_summary: GKE Autopilot-based MLOps infrastructure for a personal lines carrier enabling actuarial data scientists to train, validate, and promote auto insurance pricing and claim propensity scoring models through automated Kubeflow Pipelines with MLflow experiment tracking and model registry. Fairness validation gates enforce NAIC AI white paper proxy variable thresholds before model registration. A FastAPI serving gateway on GKE implements champion-challenger traffic splitting with configuration updated via GCS without pod redeployment. All actuarial training data remains within VPC Service Controls perimeter in GCP us-central1 with full Cloud Audit Logs trail for state rate filing examination.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier GKE Autopilot platform in us-central1. Training tier: Kubeflow Pipelines DAGs running on training-pool nodes (n2-highmem-8, KEDA-scaled 0-10 on KFP queue depth) for weekly auto pricing retraining and monthly claim propensity retraining. Validation components within each DAG enforce fairness benchmarks against NAIC proxy variable thresholds before MLflow model registration. Registry tier: MLflow tracking server on mlflow-pool nodes writes experiment metrics to Cloud SQL PostgreSQL and model artifacts to GCS CMEK bucket. MLflow model registry staging-to-production stage transitions trigger a Cloud Run promotion function that updates champion-challenger config JSON in GCS. Serving tier: FastAPI model-serving-gateway on serving-pool (n2-standard-4, HPA 3-6 nodes) reads champion-challenger config from GCS on each request and routes to champion or challenger replica accordingly. Vertex AI Feature Store provides online feature lookup for inference. VPC Service Controls perimeter wraps BigQuery and GCS to prevent data exfiltration. Terraform manages all GCP resources; ArgoCD manages GKE Helm releases via GitOps.
target_output.technology_stack: Training and Pipelines: Python 3.12, Kubeflow Pipelines SDK 2.x, scikit-learn and XGBoost for actuarial model training, pandas and BigQuery Storage API for data loading. Experiment Tracking: MLflow 2.x with GCS artifact store and Cloud SQL PostgreSQL backend. Model Serving: FastAPI 0.110 on Python 3.12, model artifacts loaded from GCS via MLflow model registry. Feature Store: Vertex AI Feature Store for online feature serving; BigQuery ML TRANSFORM for batch feature computation. Infrastructure: GKE Autopilot us-central1, KEDA for training-pool scaling, Cloud SQL PostgreSQL for MLflow backend. IaC: Terraform 1.7 with google provider. GitOps: ArgoCD 2.10 with Helm charts per workload. CI/CD: Cloud Build. Observability: Prometheus with GKE metrics, Grafana 10.x, Cloud Audit Logs, Cloud Logging. Security: VPC Service Controls, Workload Identity, Secret Manager, External Secrets Operator.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Automated Training Pipeline: data scientist commits updated feature engineering code; Cloud Build compiles and uploads Kubeflow Pipeline; KFP CronSchedule triggers weekly auto pricing retraining; pipeline steps: BigQuery data fetch with holdout split, feature engineering via Vertex Feature Store batch compute, XGBoost model training with MLflow run logging, fairness validation against NAIC proxy variable thresholds, stability benchmark against 3-month holdout. Model Registry: MLflow model registry with staging and production aliases; data scientist promotes model to staging after local review; automated CI validation runs integration benchmark before production promotion. Champion-Challenger Serving: FastAPI serving gateway reads champion_challenger_configs from GCS on each predict request; promotion-workflow updates config within 60 seconds of MLflow production alias change; traffic gradually shifted from 5% to 20% to 50% challenger with loss ratio impact monitoring at each step. Fairness Gate: fairness validation Kubeflow component computes Pearson correlation between model output and protected class proxy variables (ZIP code, marital status); blocks MLflow registration if correlation exceeds NAIC threshold config. Loss Ratio Monitoring: Grafana dashboard joining champion vs challenger predictions against eventual claim outcomes from BigQuery actuarial tables with configurable lag window.
target_output.system_components: GKE Autopilot cluster us-central1 with three node pools. KEDA ScaledObject on training-pool monitoring KFP workflow queue depth. MLflow tracking server Deployment on mlflow-pool with Cloud SQL PostgreSQL backend for experiment metadata and GCS for artifacts. Kubeflow Pipelines control plane (ml-pipeline, ml-pipeline-ui, cache-server) on mlflow-pool. FastAPI model-serving-gateway Deployment on serving-pool with HPA scaling on prediction request rate. Cloud Run promotion-workflow function triggered by MLflow registry webhook. Vertex AI Feature Store entity types: policy, driver, vehicle. BigQuery dataset actuarial_training for training snapshots. GCS buckets: model-artifacts-prod (CMEK), champion-challenger-configs, training-data-snapshots. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instance for MLflow backend. VPC Service Controls perimeter enclosing BigQuery and GCS. Artifact Registry for training and serving container images. ArgoCD server on mlflow-pool syncing Helm chart releases. Prometheus and Grafana Deployments on mlflow-pool.
target_output.workflows: Model Training and Validation: Actuarial data scientist merges feature engineering changes to model training repository. Cloud Build trigger compiles updated Kubeflow Pipeline component and uploads compiled pipeline YAML to KFP endpoint. KFP CronSchedule or manual trigger launches pipeline run on training-pool: (1) BigQuery data fetcher component pulls actuarial training snapshot and 90-day holdout dataset to GCS; (2) Feature engineering component computes actuarial features via Vertex AI Feature Store batch compute, logs feature hash to MLflow run; (3) XGBoost training component trains model, logs loss ratio RMSE, Gini coefficient, and calibration score as MLflow metrics; (4) Fairness validation component loads NAIC threshold config from GCS, computes proxy variable Pearson correlations, logs fairness_scores_json to MLflow run, fails pipeline if any correlation exceeds threshold; (5) On pass, registers model to MLflow registry in staging stage with run_id and dataset_snapshot_gcs_path logged. Champion-Challenger Promotion: MLops engineer reviews staging model metrics in MLflow UI, triggers production promotion via Python SDK mlflow.MlflowClient().transition_model_version_stage(). MLflow registry webhook fires to Cloud Run promotion-workflow function. Function reads current champion-challenger-configs/{model_name}.json from GCS, writes new config with challenger_version set to promoted version and challenger_traffic_pct set to 5. FastAPI serving gateway reads updated config from GCS on next request batch; 5% of auto pricing requests now routed to new model. Grafana loss ratio dashboard monitored; traffic gradually increased to 20%, 50%, 100% as challenger performance validates.
target_output.data_model: model_experiments stored in MLflow tracking backend (Cloud SQL): run_id UUID, experiment_id, model_name, start_time, end_time, status, metrics (loss_ratio_rmse FLOAT, gini_coefficient FLOAT, calibration_score FLOAT, proxy_correlation_zip FLOAT, proxy_correlation_marital FLOAT), params (hyperparameters JSON), tags (dataset_snapshot_path, feature_hash, pipeline_run_id). training_datasets tracked in MLflow runs and BigQuery metadata table: dataset_name, snapshot_date, bigquery_table_ref, row_count, feature_hash, created_at. feature_stores defined in Vertex AI Feature Store: policy entity (policy_id, vehicle_age_years, prior_claims_3yr, telematics_score, zip_risk_decile), driver entity (driver_id, age_bucket, years_licensed, violation_points_3yr). model_versions in MLflow registry: model_name, version, stage ENUM staging/production/archived, creation_timestamp, last_updated_timestamp, run_id. champion_challenger_configs in GCS JSON: model_name, champion_version, challenger_version, challenger_traffic_pct, config_updated_at, updated_by. pricing_overrides in Cloud SQL: id, model_name, override_factor FLOAT, reason_code, approved_by, effective_date, expiry_date, created_at.
target_output.api_design: Internal APIs only; no public endpoints. FastAPI model-serving-gateway: POST /v1/predict/auto-pricing (input: policy_id, driver_id, vehicle_id, coverage_options_json; output: base_rate_factor FLOAT, model_version, routed_to ENUM champion/challenger, prediction_id UUID). POST /v1/predict/claim-propensity (input: policy_id, claim_window_days; output: propensity_score FLOAT, model_version, prediction_id). GET /v1/health (liveness probe). GET /v1/champion-challenger/{model_name} (current traffic split config, admin use). MLflow Registry REST API: GET /api/2.0/mlflow/registered-models/get-latest-versions, POST /api/2.0/mlflow/model-versions/transition-stage. KFP API: POST /apis/v1beta1/runs for manual pipeline trigger. All internal APIs accessible only within GKE cluster VPC; no internet exposure.
target_output.security_and_compliance: VPC Service Controls perimeter enclosing BigQuery actuarial_training dataset and GCS model artifact and training data buckets; prevents data exfiltration from us-central1 perimeter. Workload Identity bindings for each GKE service account: training-pipeline-sa has roles/bigquery.dataViewer on actuarial dataset and roles/storage.objectAdmin on model-artifacts; serving-gateway-sa has roles/storage.objectViewer on model-artifacts and champion-challenger-configs. Secret Manager stores Cloud SQL MLflow backend credentials and Vertex AI API keys; External Secrets Operator syncs to GKE Secrets with automatic rotation. Cloud Audit Logs data access logging on BigQuery and GCS; audit events forwarded to BigQuery audit_events table for 5-year retention; model promotion to production stage generates Cloud Logging entry with promoted_by, model_name, model_version, and training_dataset_snapshot_path for state rate filing examination. PII de-identification: BigQuery Column-level encryption masks raw policyholder name and address in training tables; only de-identified actuarial features (age_bucket, zip_risk_decile, violation_points_3yr) used in training. NAIC AI fairness gate: proxy variable Pearson correlation thresholds defined in fairness_threshold_config.json in GCS; reviewed and updated by compliance team; threshold breach blocks MLflow registration and notifies compliance team via Pub/Sub.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Terraform 1.7 modules: gke-autopilot (cluster, node pools, KEDA), mlflow-backend (Cloud SQL PostgreSQL, GCS artifact bucket with CMEK, VPC Service Controls perimeter update), kubeflow-pipelines (KFP control plane Helm release), serving-gateway (GCS champion-challenger bucket, Cloud Run promotion function, Pub/Sub topic), observability (Prometheus, Grafana, Cloud Logging sinks). Terraform state in GCS backend with state locking. Cloud Build triggers: PR to model-training-repo builds and validates Kubeflow Pipeline component images; PR to serving-gateway-repo builds FastAPI container and runs pytest unit tests; merge to main updates Artifact Registry and triggers ArgoCD sync. ArgoCD manages Helm charts: charts/mlflow-server, charts/serving-gateway, charts/kubeflow-pipelines, charts/observability. ArgoCD App-of-Apps pattern for environment promotion (dev namespace, staging namespace, production namespace). Serving gateway rolling update strategy: maxUnavailable 0, maxSurge 1 to maintain champion traffic during updates. Environments: dev (single-replica serving gateway, reduced node pool sizes), staging (production topology with synthetic actuarial data), production (HPA-scaled serving gateway, KEDA-scaled training pool).
target_output.observability: Prometheus metrics from GKE pods scraped at 30s. Grafana dashboards: (1) Training Pipeline: pipeline run duration histogram by model_name, fairness gate pass/fail rate by week, training data row count trend. (2) Serving Gateway: prediction request rate by model_name, p50/p95/p99 latency by model_name, routed_to distribution (champion vs challenger %). (3) Loss Ratio Impact: Grafana BigQuery datasource querying eventual claims joined to prediction_id over 90-day lag window; champion vs challenger loss ratio RMSE trend. MLflow experiment UI accessible internally for data scientist metric review. Prometheus alerts: serving gateway p99 >500ms triggers PagerDuty; fairness gate failure triggers Pub/Sub notification to compliance team; KFP training pod OOM triggers Slack alert. Cloud Audit Logs query: all model promotions in past 30 days queryable for rate filing.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: GKE Autopilot training-pool KEDA-scaled 0-10 n2-highmem-8: zero cost off-hours; ~$2,000/month during weekly training window (8 hours, 5 nodes average). Serving-pool 3-6 n2-standard-4 HPA: ~$1,200/month baseline. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL db-n1-standard-2: ~$150/month. GCS model artifacts and training data: ~$200/month for 2TB. Vertex AI Feature Store: ~$400/month for policy/driver/vehicle entities. Cloud Run promotion function: negligible. Grafana and Prometheus on shared mlflow-pool nodes. Total ~$3,950/month. Cost optimization: Spot Instances for training-pool reduces training cost by 60% with KFP retry on eviction.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (Weeks 1-10): Terraform GKE Autopilot cluster and supporting GCP resources, MLflow tracking server with Cloud SQL and GCS artifact store, Kubeflow Pipelines control plane, first auto pricing training pipeline DAG with fairness validation gate, basic serving gateway (champion model only, no challenger), ArgoCD GitOps setup, Cloud Audit Logs configuration. Phase 2 Champion-Challenger (Weeks 11-18): Claim propensity model pipeline, champion-challenger serving gateway with GCS config, Cloud Run promotion-workflow function, Grafana loss ratio impact dashboard, Vertex AI Feature Store entity types and batch compute. Phase 3 Monitoring and Scale (Weeks 19-26): Full Prometheus alerting suite, MLflow registry webhook integration, pricing override management API, real-time Pub/Sub feature streaming for telematics UBI models, model explainability API for regulatory examination.
target_output.development_guidelines: All GCP resources provisioned via Terraform; no manual Console changes. Kubeflow Pipeline components defined as containerized Python functions using @kfp.dsl.component decorator with explicit input/output artifacts typed as kfp.dsl.Dataset and kfp.dsl.Model. MLflow run logging: every training run must log dataset_snapshot_path tag for reproducibility; fairness_scores_json metric must be logged before model registration. Serving gateway reads champion-challenger config from GCS on each predict request using google-cloud-storage client with 500ms timeout; cache config in memory with 60-second TTL. Terraform modules must pass terraform validate and tflint in Cloud Build before merge. ArgoCD ApplicationSets enforce namespace isolation between dev, staging, and production model serving workloads.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Fairness threshold config in GCS becomes stale as NAIC guidelines update. Mitigation: compliance team owns fairness_threshold_config.json in a separate Git repository with change review; Cloud Build deploys config updates to GCS. Risk: XGBoost model artifact size grows with feature count, slowing serving gateway cold start. Mitigation: MLflow model loading cached in serving gateway memory; readiness probe gates traffic until model loaded. Risk: BigQuery training data scan cost spikes with table size growth. Mitigation: partitioned actuarial tables by policy_effective_date; training pipelines scan only rolling 3-year window. Tradeoff: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL for MLflow backend over BigQuery — lower cost and lower latency for MLflow UI queries but requires Cloud SQL HA configuration. Tradeoff: Kubeflow Pipelines over Vertex AI Pipelines — self-managed on GKE provides more control over compute and networking inside VPC Service Controls perimeter than managed Vertex AI Pipelines.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected MLflow over Vertex AI Experiments for model registry — MLflow staging/production aliases map directly to promotion workflow; Vertex AI Experiments lacks stage transition webhooks needed for champion-challenger automation. Confirmed Kubeflow Pipelines on GKE over Vertex AI Pipelines to remain within VPC Service Controls perimeter. KEDA selected over GKE Autopilot node provisioner for training-pool scaling on KFP queue depth metric. Spot Instances for training-pool accepted given KFP retry semantics on component eviction.

constraints.no_server_runtime: Static only; no Vercel serverless functions

reasoner_reviews.product: The primary conversion goal is sandbox sign-up from PropTech developer visitors. Rounds 1 and 2 established the core page structure but the Vercel Edge Config A/B test implementation was left as a design note without the actual Astro component pattern for reading Edge Config values at build time versus edge render time. Round 3 must close this.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro 4 static generation to Vercel is architecturally sound. The A/B testing requirement via Vercel Edge Config introduces a nuance: Astro runs at build time, not edge runtime, so CTA copy variants cannot be read from Edge Config during static generation. The correct pattern is to use Vercel Edge Middleware to inject a cookie-free variant flag as a response header, then use a minimal Astro island (client:load) that reads the header from a /api/ab-flag Vercel Edge Function and swaps the CTA text client-side. This must be specified precisely.
reasoner_reviews.security: CSP header must explicitly allow Formspree script domain (formspree.io) in addition to Plausible analytics domain. Round 2 CSP was missing Formspree in script-src, which would break the contact form in browsers with strict CSP enforcement.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Vercel free tier supports Edge Functions and Edge Config. Formspree free tier supports 50 submissions/month for MVP. The Edge Middleware A/B pattern adds a small latency overhead (under 10ms) at Vercel edge — acceptable given the 2-second LCP target.

security.auth_design: No user authentication; anonymous public access; Formspree handles sandbox sign-up form submissions with its own CSRF protection
security.abuse_prevention: Formspree honeypot field enabled on sandbox sign-up form to block automated bot submissions; Vercel rate limiting at edge for repeated form submission attempts
security.privacy_controls: Plausible Analytics cookieless pixel; no consent banner required; privacy policy page references Plausible aggregated-only data and HubSpot CRM as form data processor

data.retention_and_deletion: No on-site data retention; HubSpot CRM data managed per HubSpot GDPR data retention settings; Plausible aggregated metrics retained per Plausible account settings

ISS-001.title: Vercel Edge Config A/B test pattern incompatible with Astro static generation
ISS-001.detail: Astro generates static HTML at build time; Vercel Edge Config values cannot be read during Astro build. Rounds 1 and 2 described A/B testing via Edge Config but did not specify the correct runtime pattern: Edge Middleware assigning variant, Edge Function serving variant flag, and Astro island reading flag client-side to swap CTA text.

history.note: A/B testing described as 'via Edge Config' without implementation specifics
A/B section added but described Edge Config read at build time which is incorrect for Astro static output

ISS-002.title: CSP missing Formspree script domain blocking form submission in strict CSP browsers
ISS-002.detail: Round 2 CSP header defined script-src 'self' plausible.io but did not include js.formspree.io, which is required for Formspree's embedded JavaScript form handler. Browsers enforcing strict CSP will block the Formspree script and sandbox sign-up form submissions will silently fail.

history.note: Security section added CSP but Formspree script domain was omitted from script-src

focus_issues.title: CSP missing Formspree script domain
focus_issues.detail: js.formspree.io must be added to script-src in CSP header or sandbox sign-up form will be blocked in strict CSP browsers.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified correct A/B test architecture: Vercel Edge Middleware (middleware.ts) reads cta_variant value from Vercel Edge Config and sets x-ab-variant response header on all requests to index.astro. Vercel Edge Function at /api/ab-flag.ts returns the variant from the same Edge Config. Astro island component CTAVariant.tsx (client:load) fetches /api/ab-flag and updates CTA button innerHTML with variant copy from a client-side mapping object. Edge Config updated via Vercel dashboard or vercel edge-config CLI without any redeployment.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Marketing team can update CTA copy variants in Vercel Edge Config dashboard; island component displays correct variant to each visitor without page rebuild

fix_report.action_taken: Added js.formspree.io to Content-Security-Policy script-src directive in vercel.json headers configuration alongside plausible.io; also added formspree.io to connect-src for XHR form submission calls
