target_output.title: Embedded Renters Insurance API Partner Landing Page
target_output.executive_summary: Astro 4 static landing page deployed to Vercel Edge Network targeting PropTech platform developers and property management companies evaluating embedded renters insurance API partnerships. Drives sandbox sign-up conversions via Formspree-to-HubSpot form routing. Plausible Analytics provides cookieless conversion tracking. A/B testing on hero CTA copy implemented via Vercel Edge Middleware and Edge Config without page redeployment. CSP headers restrict scripts to Plausible and Formspree domains only.
target_output.architecture_overview: Astro 4 static site (output: 'static') generates zero-runtime HTML at build time and deploys to Vercel Edge Network global CDN. Astro content collections in src/content/ manage product_feature_sections, case_studies, and pricing_tiers as Markdown files. Formspree form action handles sandbox sign-up submissions routed to HubSpot CRM without any server-side code on origin. Plausible Analytics cookieless pixel tracks pageviews and CTA clicks. A/B test architecture: Vercel Edge Middleware reads cta_variant from Vercel Edge Config on every request to / and sets x-ab-variant response header; Vercel Edge Function at /api/ab-flag.ts returns variant value; Astro island CTAVariant.tsx (client:load) fetches /api/ab-flag and swaps CTA button text from a bundled variant copy map. Vercel preview deployments on PRs; Lighthouse CI LCP gate enforced in GitHub Actions.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, minimal Astro islands for form and A/B CTA. Form handling: Formspree (free tier) routing to HubSpot CRM. Analytics: Plausible Analytics cloud (cookieless). A/B testing: Vercel Edge Config + Edge Middleware + Edge Function. CDN: Vercel Edge Network. CI/CD: GitHub Actions (lint, build, Lighthouse CI), Vercel GitHub integration. Security headers: vercel.json headers array with CSP, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero Section: API-first embedded renters insurance headline with two CTA variants (A: 'Get sandbox API keys', B: 'Start integrating today') controlled via Edge Config. Product Feature Sections: three API capability cards — instant policy issuance, real-time premium rating, and claims notification webhook — populated from Astro content collection. API Documentation CTA: deep link to developer portal sandbox registration. Case Study Section: PropTech partner testimonials with integration metric highlights from Astro content collection. Pricing Tier Section: three tiers (Starter, Growth, Enterprise) with API call volumes and per-unit pricing from Astro content collection. Sandbox Sign-Up Form: company name, contact email, platform type, monthly units under management, routed to HubSpot via Formspree. Plausible custom event tracking on CTA click and form submission.
target_output.system_components: Vercel project connected to GitHub main branch with Astro build command and ./dist output directory. Vercel Edge Config store with cta_variant key holding current A/B variant value. Vercel Edge Middleware (middleware.ts at project root) executing on all requests to set x-ab-variant header. Vercel Edge Function at api/ab-flag.ts returning variant JSON for client-side island. Astro island component CTAVariant.tsx loaded client:load on hero section. Formspree form endpoint routing sandbox sign-up submissions to HubSpot deal pipeline. Plausible Analytics script loaded via Astro component in site head. GitHub Actions workflow for lint, build, and Lighthouse CI mobile LCP gate.
target_output.workflows: CTA A/B Test Flow: Visitor requests embedded-renters.com/. Vercel Edge Middleware executes: reads cta_variant from Edge Config ('variant_a' or 'variant_b'), sets x-ab-variant response header, serves static HTML from CDN. CTAVariant Astro island loads client:load, fetches /api/ab-flag (Vercel Edge Function returns variant from Edge Config), updates CTA button innerHTML with variant copy from the bundled { variant_a: 'Get sandbox API keys', variant_b: 'Start integrating today' } map. Plausible custom event hero_cta_click fires on button click. Marketing team updates cta_variant in Vercel Edge Config dashboard; change takes effect on next visitor request without any redeployment. Sandbox Sign-Up Conversion: PropTech developer scrolls to sandbox sign-up form, enters company details and platform type, submits. Formspree endpoint validates honeypot field (blocks bots), routes submission to HubSpot CRM as new deal with platform_type and units_under_management custom properties. Sales team notified via HubSpot workflow. Plausible custom event sandbox_signup_submit fires on successful form response.
target_output.data_model: No on-site database. Astro content collections: product_feature_sections (src/content/features/*.md: title, description, icon_name, order_index), case_studies (src/content/case-studies/*.md: partner_name, summary, metric_highlight, logo_path, published_at), pricing_tiers (src/content/pricing/*.md: tier_name, api_calls_per_month, price_per_unit_cents, features_list[]). Vercel Edge Config: {cta_variant: 'variant_a' | 'variant_b'}. HubSpot CRM stores partnership_inquiries with fields: company_name, contact_email, platform_type, units_under_management, source_page, submitted_at. Plausible aggregated events: pageview, hero_cta_click (variant label), sandbox_signup_submit.
target_output.api_design: No backend API on the landing page. One Vercel Edge Function: GET /api/ab-flag (returns JSON {variant: 'variant_a' | 'variant_b'} read from Edge Config; called by CTAVariant Astro island on client load). Formspree endpoint: POST https://formspree.io/f/{formId} (handled entirely by Formspree; no landing page origin processing). Plausible Analytics: script tag at https://plausible.io/js/script.js with data-domain attribute; custom events via window.plausible() API calls in CTAVariant island and form submission handler.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Vercel with HSTS. Content-Security-Policy in vercel.json headers: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' plausible.io js.formspree.io; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: *.ctfassets.net; connect-src 'self' plausible.io formspree.io; frame-ancestors 'none'. X-Frame-Options: DENY. Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin. Formspree honeypot field _gotcha prevents bot sign-up submissions. No cookies set on site visitors — Plausible cookieless analytics. No first-party data stored; HubSpot is the data processor for form submissions under HubSpot DPA. Privacy policy page references Plausible aggregated-only data and HubSpot CRM as processors.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions workflow: (1) bun install --frozen-lockfile; (2) astro check (TypeScript and Astro type validation); (3) bun run build (Astro static build to ./dist); (4) Lighthouse CI mobile audit on built output — blocks merge if LCP >2s or Performance score <90. Vercel GitHub integration: preview deployment created on every PR with unique preview URL for marketing team content review. Production deployment on merge to main via Vercel build system running same Astro build. Vercel project settings: build command 'bun run build', output directory './dist', install command 'bun install'. Rollback: Vercel dashboard or vercel rollback CLI to any of last 10 production deployments within 30 seconds. A/B variant rollback: update cta_variant in Vercel Edge Config dashboard — effective immediately without redeployment.
target_output.observability: Plausible Analytics cloud dashboard: daily pageviews, top referrers (PropTech blogs, developer forums), CTA variant click rate by variant_a vs variant_b, sandbox_signup_submit conversion rate, geographic traffic by country. Vercel deployment Slack webhook notifying on production deploy success or build failure. Lighthouse CI scores tracked in GitHub Actions run history for LCP regression detection. HubSpot CRM sandbox sign-up pipeline monitored by sales team for weekly lead volume. No APM needed for static CDN delivery.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Hobby tier: free for static deployments with Edge Functions and Edge Config. Formspree free tier: 50 form submissions/month sufficient for MVP partnership volume. Plausible Analytics: $9/month cloud plan for up to 10,000 monthly pageviews. GitHub Actions: ~20 build minutes/month at effectively zero cost on free tier. Total monthly cost: $9. Scale path: Vercel Pro at $20/month when team preview deployments exceed free tier limits; Formspree Basic at $10/month when lead volume exceeds 50/month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (Weeks 1-3): Hero section with CTA, product feature cards from content collection, sandbox sign-up form via Formspree to HubSpot, Plausible Analytics, Vercel deployment, CSP headers with Formspree and Plausible domains, Lighthouse CI gate. Phase 2 Full Content (Weeks 4-6): Case study section from content collection, pricing tier section, API documentation CTA link to developer portal, A/B test implementation with Edge Middleware and CTAVariant island, Vercel Edge Config setup. Phase 3 International (Q4): Localized Astro page variants for UK and EU markets via Astro i18n routing, multilingual content collections, HubSpot form routing by locale.
target_output.development_guidelines: Astro output must remain 'static' — no server endpoints, no Astro SSR, no use-client directives outside named islands. Tailwind classes must use design token variables defined in tailwind.config.ts for brand colors and typography. CTAVariant island must have a static fallback rendering variant_a copy for visitors with JavaScript disabled. All content updates (feature sections, case studies, pricing) via Markdown files in src/content/ — no hardcoded copy in Astro components. Lighthouse CI score gate enforced on every PR; LCP regression must be fixed before merge. vercel.json headers CSP reviewed on every PR that adds a new third-party script.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Formspree free tier 50 submissions/month exceeded as partnership campaign scales. Mitigation: Formspree Basic at $10/month activated when approaching limit; HubSpot native form embed considered for Phase 2. Risk: Plausible script blocked by aggressive ad blockers reducing analytics coverage. Mitigation: Plausible proxy mode via Vercel rewrites to first-party domain; acceptable data gap for B2B developer audience. Risk: Vercel Edge Config latency on A/B variant assignment adds overhead to every request. Mitigation: Edge Config reads are sub-millisecond; total Edge Middleware overhead under 5ms on Vercel's network; LCP target preserved. Tradeoff: Astro island for A/B CTA over server-side rendering — Astro static export prevents SSR; island pattern adds minimal CLS since CTA button dimensions are fixed in CSS regardless of copy length.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Vercel Edge Config over third-party A/B testing tools (Optimizely, VWO) — Edge Config is free on Vercel and avoids loading third-party A/B JavaScript bundles that would fail CSP and degrade LCP. Formspree retained over HubSpot native form embed for Phase 1 simplicity; HubSpot embed reconsidered in Phase 2 when Formspree free tier is outgrown. Plausible confirmed over Google Analytics 4 — cookieless analytics avoids GDPR consent banner requirement that would hurt conversion rate for technical B2B visitors.

metadata.notes: Round 3 insurtech landing page; closes Edge Config A/B test pattern fix and Formspree CSP domain gap from prior rounds.

project_goal.value: Ingest policyholder location data from multiple cedants, geocode addresses, map exposures to catastrophe model zones, and produce treaty-level accumulation reports for portfolio underwriters at a reinsurance firm
project_goal.rationale: Core business need enabling reinsurance treaty pricing and capacity decisions

target_users.value: Portfolio underwriters, catastrophe analysts, and actuarial teams within the reinsurance firm
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers of accumulation reports and zone-level exposure summaries

project_class.rationale: No user-facing UI; all value delivered through batch ingestion, transformation, and reporting outputs

capabilities.rationale: Pipeline requires data ingestion from cedants, transformation jobs, third-party geocoding and cat model integrations, and analytical reporting

complexity_level.rationale: Hundreds of cedant files per renewal cycle, millions of policy locations, parallel geocoding against external APIs, and large-volume ClickHouse analytical queries

risk_level.rationale: Cedant exposure data is commercially sensitive; errors in accumulation reports directly impact treaty pricing decisions worth hundreds of millions

data_sensitivity.rationale: Policyholder location data shared under NDA by cedants; treated as confidential commercial information

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline outputs consumed by internal underwriting and actuarial teams only; no public or partner-facing endpoints

access_model.value: Custom JWT with role-based access; underwriters read reports, engineers administer pipeline, no self-service signup
access_model.rationale: Internal tooling accessed by named staff; LDAP-integrated JWT issuance

feature_scope.value: SFTP ingestion of cedant CSV and EDM files, address geocoding via Google Maps Platform, cat model zone mapping, treaty accumulation computation, PML reporting, reconciliation and exception reporting
feature_scope.rationale: Full pipeline scope required for treaty underwriting decisions

mvp_scope.value: Single-cedant ingestion pipeline with geocoding, zone mapping for wind and earthquake perils, and treaty accumulation report; multi-cedant parallelism and full peril suite in subsequent phases
mvp_scope.rationale: Validates core pipeline before scaling to full cedant portfolio

security_baseline.value: Data encrypted at rest with AES-256 via AWS KMS, TLS 1.3 for all data transfers, SFTP with key-based authentication, VPC-isolated processing, no cedant data stored beyond defined retention windows
security_baseline.rationale: Cedant data sharing agreements mandate strong encryption and access controls

frontend_stack.value: None; pipeline output delivered via ClickHouse query interface and exported CSV/Parquet reports
frontend_stack.rationale: Data pipeline project with no browser-based UI

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Apache Airflow for orchestration; pandas and GeoPandas for spatial transformation; custom ingestion workers on ECS Fargate
backend_stack.rationale: Airflow provides scheduling, retry logic, and DAG visibility; GeoPandas handles spatial zone intersection efficiently

data_platform.value: ClickHouse for analytical accumulation queries; PostgreSQL 16 for pipeline metadata and job state; S3 for raw cedant file storage and Parquet exports
data_platform.rationale: ClickHouse delivers sub-second query performance on billion-row zone exposure tables; PostgreSQL manages job tracking

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate for pipeline workers, MWAA for Airflow, us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: Fargate eliminates cluster management; MWAA provides managed Airflow with HA

privacy_retention_policy.value: Cedant raw files purged after 90 days; geocoded location records retained for 3 years per data sharing agreements; pipeline logs retained 1 year
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Cedant NDA stipulates data destruction schedules; 3-year retention supports retrospective analysis

future_scope.value: Multi-model vendor integration (AIR, RMS), real-time cedant data API ingestion, self-service accumulation query UI for underwriters
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap to expand cat model coverage and reduce report turnaround from hours to minutes

constraints.value: Must remain in AWS us-east-1 for data residency; geocoding API budget capped at $8K/month; treaty renewal cycle requires reports within 4-hour SLA of ingestion completion
constraints.rationale: Data residency required by cedant agreements; geocoding cost scales with policy volume

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for pipeline metrics; Airflow task logs in CloudWatch; PagerDuty alerting for DAG failures and SLA breaches
observability_baseline.rationale: Engineering on-call needs visibility into ingestion failures and report SLA status

execution_preference.value: Phased delivery with MVP single-cedant pipeline first, then incremental expansion; infrastructure-as-code via Terraform from day one
execution_preference.rationale: Validates pipeline design before committing to full multi-cedant architecture

compliance_context.value: SOC 2 Type II controls required; GDPR data subject rights apply to EU policyholder addresses processed in the pipeline; cedant data sharing agreements govern permitted use and deletion schedules
compliance_context.rationale: Reinsurer operates SOC 2 program; EU address processing triggers GDPR Article 6 lawful basis documentation

project.goal: Build a catastrophe exposure aggregation pipeline for reinsurance treaty underwriting
project.target_users: Portfolio underwriters and catastrophe analysts
project.timeline: MVP in 12 weeks, full multi-cedant in 6 months

backend.processing: GeoPandas for spatial zone intersection, pandas for tabular transforms

security.auth: Custom JWT with LDAP integration
security.encryption: KMS at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit
security.file_transfer: SFTP with RSA key pairs per cedant

data.storage: S3 for raw files and Parquet exports

open_questions.cat_model_vendor: AIR vs RMS zone grid licensing to be decided in Phase 2
open_questions.geocoding_cache: Evaluate caching geocoded coordinates to reduce API calls and cost

reasoner_reviews.product: The pipeline scope is well-defined for reinsurance treaty underwriting. SFTP ingestion with EDM format support is essential given cedant heterogeneity. The 4-hour SLA is tight for large cedants with millions of locations and must be validated against geocoding throughput limits.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: ClickHouse is the right choice for treaty accumulation queries over zone-level exposure tables that will reach hundreds of millions of rows. Airflow DAG design must account for per-cedant parallelism and geocoding rate limits. A geocoding result cache in S3 Parquet avoids re-geocoding unchanged addresses on re-submissions and is mandatory at scale.
reasoner_reviews.security: Cedant data arrives under NDA with specific destruction obligations. Each cedant should have an isolated S3 prefix with its own KMS key alias to enforce data boundary controls. GDPR requires a lawful basis and data subject rights procedure for EU policyholder addresses even in a B2B reinsurance context.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The $8K/month geocoding budget at standard Google Maps Platform pricing supports roughly 4 million geocoding calls per month. Large cedants may exceed this in a single renewal cycle; a caching layer for previously geocoded addresses is mandatory at full scale.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The reconciliation report design needs specificity. Field-level null rates and checksum comparisons are necessary but insufficient; the pipeline should validate that total insured value by zone matches cedant-reported control totals within a 0.5% tolerance before publishing accumulation reports.

backend.service_design: Airflow DAG per cedant with tasks: S3 file sensor, format detection (CSV vs EDM), schema validation, geocoding worker pool (ECS Fargate tasks with S3 cache lookup), zone intersection (GeoPandas spatial join against cat model zone GeoParquet), ClickHouse bulk insert, reconciliation check
backend.background_jobs: Nightly loss history reconciliation DAG; weekly portfolio snapshot DAG for treaty renewal reporting; geocoding cache refresh job for coordinates older than 6 months
backend.failure_handling: Per-task retry with exponential backoff; dead-letter S3 prefix for unparseable records; Airflow SLA miss callbacks to PagerDuty; reconciliation failure halts downstream accumulation report publication to prevent corrupt data
backend.scaling_notes: Geocoding workers scale horizontally as ECS Fargate tasks driven by Airflow task queue depth; ClickHouse sharded by peril and reporting_period for query parallelism; Airflow worker pool sized for 10 concurrent cedant DAGs

security.auth_design: Custom JWT issued via internal LDAP-backed auth service; Airflow UI and internal CLI protected by same JWT middleware; read-only ClickHouse user for underwriter queries
security.secrets_management: AWS Secrets Manager for geocoding API keys and ClickHouse credentials; rotated every 90 days; no secrets in DAG code or environment variables
security.privacy_controls: EU policyholder address records tagged with data_region=EU in metadata DB; GDPR deletion jobs triggered by cedant data expiry events; geocoding cache entries carry same retention schedule as source records
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every cedant file ingestion event logged to PostgreSQL audit table with file hash, record count, geocoding success rate, zone match rate, and operator identity; CloudTrail for S3 and KMS operations

data.entities: cedant_submissions (id, cedant_id, file_name, file_format ENUM csv/edm, s3_key, file_hash, ingested_at, record_count, status ENUM received/validated/geocoding/mapped/accumulated/reconciled/failed), policy_locations (id, submission_id, raw_address, latitude, longitude, geocode_confidence, cat_zone_wind, cat_zone_eq, cat_zone_flood, tiv_usd, occupancy_code, construction_code, data_region), peril_exposures (id, submission_id, peril, zone_id, total_tiv, location_count, reporting_period), treaty_accumulations (id, treaty_id, peril, zone_id, gross_tiv, ceded_tiv, net_tiv, as_of_date), reconciliation_reports (id, submission_id, source_count, geocoded_count, zone_matched_count, tiv_variance_pct, status ENUM pass/fail/review)
data.storage_design: S3 per-cedant raw prefix with dedicated KMS key alias; processed Parquet exports to S3 analytical prefix; ClickHouse for live accumulation queries; PostgreSQL for pipeline metadata and audit trail
data.retention_and_deletion: Cedant raw files purged at 90 days via S3 lifecycle rule; geocoded location records flagged for deletion at 3-year mark; EU records subject to GDPR deletion workflow triggered by cedant data expiry events
data.analytics_events: Treaty accumulation refreshed on each successful cedant run; portfolio PML snapshots computed weekly; zone-level exposure delta reports generated for treaty renewal periods

devops.deployment_topology: MWAA Airflow cluster in us-east-1 private subnet; ECS Fargate geocoding and transformation task definitions with auto-scaling; ClickHouse self-managed on EC2 r6i.4xlarge with EBS gp3; PostgreSQL RDS Multi-AZ
devops.environments: dev (single-AZ, reduced ClickHouse), staging (mirrors production for SLA validation), production (multi-AZ Fargate, ClickHouse primary + replica, RDS Multi-AZ)
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest with 80% coverage gate, Airflow DAG import validation, Docker build and ECR push, Terraform plan in CI, Terraform apply on merge to main
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scraping Airflow StatsD metrics and ECS task metrics; Grafana dashboards for DAG run duration, geocoding throughput and cache hit ratio, zone match rate by peril, ClickHouse query latency; PagerDuty for SLA breach and reconciliation failure alerts

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a high-scale catastrophe exposure aggregation pipeline for a reinsurance firm. Key decisions: ClickHouse for analytical accumulation queries over hundreds-of-millions-row zone exposure tables, Airflow DAGs per cedant with per-task retry and SLA callbacks, GeoPandas for spatial zone intersection against cat model GeoParquet grids, per-cedant S3 prefix isolation with dedicated KMS keys for NDA data boundary enforcement. Geocoding coordinate cache is architecturally mandatory given the $8K/month budget constraint at full cedant volume.
target_output.title: Catastrophe Exposure Aggregation Pipeline for Reinsurance Treaty Underwriting
target_output.executive_summary: A high-scale batch data pipeline for a reinsurance firm that ingests policyholder location exposure files from multiple cedants via SFTP, geocodes property addresses against Google Maps Platform with a Parquet coordinate cache, intersects locations with catastrophe model zone grids using GeoPandas spatial joins, and loads treaty-level accumulation summaries into ClickHouse for underwriter reporting. Orchestrated by Apache Airflow on AWS MWAA with per-cedant DAG isolation, ECS Fargate workers for compute scale, and PostgreSQL tracking pipeline state and reconciliation outcomes. SOC 2 Type II controls and GDPR deletion workflows for EU policyholder addresses.
target_output.architecture_overview: Cedant exposure files land on S3 via AWS Transfer Family SFTP gateway in per-cedant isolated prefixes encrypted with dedicated KMS key aliases. Airflow on MWAA detects new files via S3 sensors and triggers per-cedant DAGs comprising: schema validation (CSV and EDM format support), geocoding via ECS Fargate worker pools calling Google Maps Platform with S3 Parquet coordinate cache lookup to avoid re-geocoding unchanged addresses, spatial zone intersection using GeoPandas sjoin against cat model wind/earthquake/flood/wildfire zone GeoParquet files, ClickHouse bulk insert of policy_locations and peril_exposures, treaty accumulation aggregation SQL, and reconciliation report generation validating TIV variance against cedant control totals within 0.5% before publishing. Final treaty accumulation reports are queryable in ClickHouse by underwriters and exportable as Parquet for actuarial tooling integration.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.8 on AWS MWAA with S3 task sensors and ECS task operators. Processing: Python 3.12, GeoPandas 0.14 and Shapely 2 for spatial zone intersection (vectorized sjoin), pandas 2.2 for tabular transforms. Analytical DB: ClickHouse 24 on EC2 r6i.4xlarge with EBS gp3, sharded by peril and reporting_period. Metadata DB: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ. File Ingestion: AWS Transfer Family SFTP with per-cedant RSA public key auth. Geocoding: Google Maps Platform Geocoding API with S3 Parquet coordinate cache. Compute: ECS Fargate workers for geocoding and transformation. Object Storage: S3 with per-cedant KMS encryption. IaC: Terraform. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Monitoring: Grafana, Prometheus, CloudWatch Logs, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Cedant File Ingestion: AWS Transfer Family SFTP endpoint accepts CSV and EDM format exposure files landing in per-cedant S3 prefix with KMS encryption, triggering Airflow DAG via S3 sensor within 5 minutes. Format Validation: Airflow task validates required EDM columns (locnum, address1, city, state, zip, tiv, occupancy, construction); schema failures route to dead-letter S3 prefix with automated cedant notification. Geocoding Engine: ECS Fargate worker pool checks S3 Parquet coordinate cache before calling Google Maps Platform API; cache hits for unchanged addresses reduce API spend by target 60%. Peril Zone Intersection: GeoPandas spatial join assigns each geocoded location to wind, earthquake, flood, and wildfire cat model zones from vendor GeoParquet grid files. Treaty Accumulation: ClickHouse aggregation computes gross TIV, ceded TIV, net TIV, and location counts per zone/peril/treaty. Reconciliation Gating: Post-accumulation task validates processed count, geocoding failure rate, zone miss rate, and TIV variance against cedant control totals; variance above 0.5% blocks report publication and pages the accumulation team.
target_output.system_components: AWS Transfer Family SFTP gateway with per-cedant RSA key authentication. S3 per-cedant raw prefix with dedicated KMS key alias for NDA data boundary enforcement. Airflow MWAA cluster running per-cedant DAGs with task-level retry and SLA miss callbacks. ECS Fargate geocoding worker pool auto-scaled by Airflow task queue depth metric. S3 Parquet geocoding coordinate cache with 6-month expiry and same retention schedule as source records. Cat model zone GeoParquet files (AIR wind and earthquake grids) on S3 loaded into GeoPandas workers per run. ClickHouse cluster on EC2 r6i.4xlarge for peril_exposures and treaty_accumulations analytical queries. PostgreSQL RDS Multi-AZ for pipeline metadata, job state, and SOC 2 audit log. Grafana + Prometheus observability stack. PagerDuty for DAG SLA breach and reconciliation failure escalation.
target_output.workflows: Cedant Ingestion and Zone Mapping: A cedant deposits a CSV exposure file via SFTP; AWS Transfer Family writes it to s3://cat-exposures/{cedant_id}/raw/ with per-cedant KMS key. Airflow S3 sensor fires the cedant DAG. Schema validation task checks EDM columns; failures route to dead-letter prefix with Slack notification. Geocoding task fans out to ECS Fargate workers that check the S3 Parquet cache (keyed on normalized address string); cache misses call Google Maps Platform API and write new coordinates back to cache. Geocoded policy_locations rows written to PostgreSQL staging table. GeoPandas zone intersection task loads AIR wind and earthquake GeoParquet grids and performs vectorized sjoin assigning zone codes to each location. ClickHouse bulk insert loads peril_exposures. Treaty Accumulation and Reconciliation Reporting: ClickHouse aggregation SQL computes treaty_accumulations rows grouping by treaty_id, peril, and zone_id. Reconciliation task retrieves cedant-provided control totals from submission metadata and compares gross TIV by zone; if any zone variance exceeds 0.5% the DAG transitions to review_required state, pages the accumulation team, and withholds the report from the underwriter query interface until manually resolved.
target_output.data_model: cedant_submissions (id UUID PK, cedant_id FK, file_name, file_format ENUM csv/edm, s3_key, file_hash_sha256, ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ, record_count INT, geocode_success_count INT, zone_match_count INT, status ENUM received/validated/geocoding/mapped/accumulated/reconciled/failed/review_required, completed_at). policy_locations (id UUID PK, submission_id FK, raw_address TEXT, latitude DECIMAL(9,6), longitude DECIMAL(9,6), geocode_confidence ENUM high/medium/low/failed, geocode_cache_hit BOOL, cat_zone_wind VARCHAR, cat_zone_eq VARCHAR, cat_zone_flood VARCHAR, cat_zone_wildfire VARCHAR, tiv_usd DECIMAL(18,2), occupancy_code VARCHAR, construction_code VARCHAR, data_region ENUM us/eu/other). peril_exposures (submission_id FK, peril ENUM wind/earthquake/flood/wildfire, zone_id VARCHAR, total_tiv_usd DECIMAL(18,2), location_count INT, reporting_period DATE). treaty_accumulations (treaty_id VARCHAR, peril ENUM, zone_id VARCHAR, gross_tiv DECIMAL(18,2), ceded_tiv DECIMAL(18,2), net_tiv DECIMAL(18,2), cedant_count INT, as_of_date DATE). reconciliation_reports (id UUID PK, submission_id FK, source_count INT, geocoded_count INT, zone_matched_count INT, tiv_variance_pct DECIMAL(6,3), status ENUM pass/fail/review, reviewer_id, resolved_at).
target_output.api_design: No public-facing API. Internal operational interfaces: Airflow REST API (JWT-authenticated) for ad-hoc DAG triggers: POST /api/v1/dags/cedant_{id}_ingestion/dagRuns with body {conf: {s3_key, submission_id}}. ClickHouse HTTP interface for underwriter accumulation queries (read-only role): SELECT zone_id, SUM(gross_tiv) FROM treaty_accumulations WHERE treaty_id='TRE-2026-001' AND peril='wind' GROUP BY zone_id. Internal pipeline CLI: python -m pipeline trigger --cedant {id} --s3-key {key} | python -m pipeline reconcile --submission-id {id} | python -m pipeline export --treaty {id} --format parquet --as-of 2026-03-01.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Per-cedant S3 prefix isolation enforced via bucket policy conditions on s3:prefix key; dedicated KMS key alias per cedant (alias/cat-{cedant_id}) for encryption boundary. AWS Transfer Family SFTP with per-cedant RSA public key; no password authentication permitted. ECS Fargate tasks in private VPC subnets with no public IP; NAT gateway for outbound Google Maps API calls. Custom JWT with LDAP group mapping to underwriter (read-only ClickHouse) vs engineer (Airflow admin) roles. GDPR controls: EU policyholder address records tagged data_region=EU; automated deletion workflow fires on cedant data expiry date; GDPR Article 30 processing register entry maintained in compliance system. SOC 2 controls: all pipeline ingestion events written to PostgreSQL audit_log (operator, action, s3_key, file_hash, record_count, data_volume_mb, timestamp); CloudTrail captures all S3 and KMS operations; Airflow task logs archived to CloudWatch with 1-year retention. Cedant data sharing agreement obligations enforced via S3 lifecycle rules automating 90-day raw file deletion.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Terraform manages all AWS resources: MWAA environment, ECS cluster and Fargate task definitions, RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ, Transfer Family SFTP server, S3 buckets with per-cedant KMS keys, VPC private and public subnets, NAT gateway, IAM roles with least-privilege policies. GitHub Actions CI: ruff lint, mypy --strict type check, pytest with 80% branch coverage gate, airflow dags list import validation, Docker build and ECR push, Terraform plan posted as PR comment, Terraform apply on merge to main. Three environments: dev (single-AZ, t4g.medium RDS, ClickHouse single node), staging (mirrors production topology for 4-hour SLA validation with synthetic cedant files), production (RDS Multi-AZ, ClickHouse r6i.4xlarge with read replica, multi-AZ ECS Fargate). ClickHouse automated EBS snapshots daily; point-in-time recovery tested quarterly. S3 versioning enabled; raw file 90-day lifecycle deletion, analytical Parquet 3-year retention.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes Airflow StatsD: dag_run_duration_seconds by cedant_id, task_duration_seconds by task_id, geocoding_api_calls_total, geocoding_cache_hit_ratio (target >60%), zone_match_rate_by_peril (alert if <95%). Grafana dashboards: Pipeline SLA board (time-from-file-arrival to reconciled status vs 4-hour target per cedant), geocoding daily cost estimate vs $8K monthly budget, ClickHouse p95 query latency for treaty_accumulations (target <5s). PagerDuty escalation triggers: Airflow SLA miss on any cedant DAG, reconciliation status=fail unresolved >1 hour, ClickHouse disk utilization >80%, geocoding API error rate >5%, ECS task OOM kill.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $4,200/month for MVP single-cedant: MWAA small environment $1,100, ECS Fargate geocoding workers $800 variable, RDS Multi-AZ db.t4g.large $280, ClickHouse r6i.xlarge $600, S3 storage $300, Google Maps Platform geocoding at ~1M calls/month $1,200 net of free tier. Geocoding cache targeting 60% hit rate keeps API spend below $8K/month at 10-cedant volume with ~10M calls/month potential. ClickHouse sharded by peril and reporting_period maintains sub-5-second query performance as accumulated rows exceed 500M. ECS Fargate geocoding workers auto-scale via Airflow task queue depth CloudWatch metric.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP Single-Cedant (12 weeks): AWS Transfer Family SFTP setup with first cedant RSA key, Terraform S3 bucket structure and KMS keys, Airflow DAG for CSV ingestion with EDM schema validation, Google Maps Platform geocoding worker with S3 Parquet coordinate cache, AIR wind and earthquake zone GeoParquet intersection, ClickHouse schema and bulk insert, treaty accumulation SQL, reconciliation report with TIV variance check, Grafana SLA dashboard, GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline. Phase 2 Multi-Cedant and Full Peril Suite (8 weeks): Per-cedant DAG factory pattern using Airflow dag_id parameterization, EDM flat-file format parser for non-CSV cedants, flood and wildfire zone GeoParquet intersection, parallel geocoding worker scaling load test, GDPR deletion workflow for EU-tagged records, SOC 2 audit log completeness review. Phase 3 Underwriter Self-Service and RMS Integration (6 weeks): Read-only ClickHouse query access for underwriters, Parquet export CLI, treaty renewal portfolio PML snapshot automation, RMS zone grid support alongside AIR.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy --strict. ruff for linting enforced in CI. All Airflow DAG code unit-tested with pytest including dag import validation (airflow dags list). GeoPandas spatial operations must use vectorized sjoin not iterrows; benchmark >50K locations/second on geocoding worker instance type. Geocoding worker must implement exponential backoff on Google Maps API OVER_QUERY_LIMIT responses with jitter. No cedant PII in test fixtures; synthetic location data only using Faker with US address profiles. Terraform modules for reusable cedant onboarding (S3 prefix, KMS key alias, Transfer Family SFTP user, least-privilege IAM policy). All secrets via AWS Secrets Manager; never in DAG code, environment variables, or config files. PR review required for any change touching reconciliation TIV variance threshold or geocoding cache invalidation logic.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Google Maps Platform geocoding rate limits (10 QPS default) create ingestion bottleneck for large cedants with 500K+ locations; mitigation: coordinate cache targets 60% hit rate, Fargate worker pool parallelism tested against quota, API quota increase request filed for renewal peak periods. Risk: ClickHouse schema evolution on running cluster requires careful migration approach; mitigation: append-only schema policy with new columns nullable by default and reporting_period-partitioned mutations. Risk: Cedant EDM format variations (non-standard column names, character encoding differences) require per-cedant format adapters; mitigation: configurable schema mapping YAML per cedant with validation before any geocoding API spend. Tradeoff: Self-managed ClickHouse on EC2 over managed AWS Redshift or Athena chosen for sub-5-second query performance on denormalized zone exposure aggregations; operational overhead accepted given team ClickHouse experience. Tradeoff: Per-cedant Airflow DAG factory increases Airflow scheduler load above 20 cedants; monitor scheduler heartbeat metric and evaluate Airflow 2.9 dag bundle feature for consolidation.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected ClickHouse over Redshift Serverless for sub-5-second accumulation query performance on denormalized zone tables at 500M+ rows. Confirmed Google Maps Platform over HERE Maps based on geocode match rate comparison test on sample cedant extract. Decided per-cedant DAG factory over single parameterized DAG for fault isolation and independent retry behavior. Geocoding coordinate cache confirmed as mandatory architecture component given $8K/month geocoding budget constraint at full multi-cedant volume.

metadata.notes: High-scale reinsurance catastrophe exposure aggregation pipeline with ClickHouse, Airflow on MWAA, GeoPandas spatial joins, per-cedant KMS isolation, geocoding cost controls, and SOC 2 + GDPR compliance.

profile.domain: Insurance Core System Data Migration CLI

project_goal.value: Command-line tool for insurance IT teams to migrate policy and claims data from legacy COBOL-era flat-file formats to modern relational schemas during core system replacements, with field mapping validation, data type coercion, and reconciliation reporting
project_goal.rationale: Core migration tooling needed for core system replacement programs at carrier IT departments

target_users.value: Insurance IT engineers and data migration specialists executing core system replacement projects
target_users.rationale: Technical users running batch migration jobs with custom mapping configurations

project_class.rationale: No browser UI; all interaction via command-line flags and config files; outputs written to database and report files

capabilities.rationale: CLI tool with data processing, PostgreSQL target database, and legacy flat-file format integration; no frontend or auth service needed

complexity_level.rationale: Non-trivial format parsing (COBOL PIC clauses, fixed-width, packed decimal), configurable field mapping, and reconciliation logic; no distributed systems or real-time concerns

risk_level.rationale: Handles confidential policy and claims data; migration errors could corrupt the target core system; risk contained by staging validation before production migration

data_sensitivity.rationale: Policy and claims data includes policyholder PII and claim financials; treated as confidential under insurance data governance policies

external_exposure.rationale: CLI runs on-premise on migration workstations or jump hosts; no network-exposed services

access_model.value: Custom JWT for database authentication; CLI invoked by named IT staff with OS-level access controls on migration workstation; no multi-user session management
access_model.rationale: Local tool run by authorized migration engineers; OS access control is the primary boundary

feature_scope.value: COBOL flat-file and CSV parsing, configurable YAML field mapping, data type coercion (packed decimal, COMP-3, date reformatting), schema validation against target PostgreSQL schema, dry-run mode, full migration run, reconciliation report generation
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set required for carrier core system replacement programs

mvp_scope.value: Fixed-width and CSV flat-file parsing, YAML field mapping, schema validation, PostgreSQL bulk insert, and reconciliation report with record counts and null rate summary
mvp_scope.rationale: Core migration path validates tool before COBOL packed-decimal and advanced coercion features are added

security_baseline.value: Database credentials via environment variables or encrypted config file; source flat files accessed read-only; migration logs scrubbed of PII before writing to disk; no sensitive data in CLI stdout
security_baseline.rationale: Migration workstations are on-premise with OS-level controls; primary risk is accidental PII logging

frontend_stack.value: None; CLI interface only with structured stdout, stderr, and file-based reconciliation reports
frontend_stack.rationale: CLI tool with no browser or desktop GUI component

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Cobra CLI framework for command and flag parsing
backend_stack.rationale: Go produces a single self-contained binary with no runtime dependency; Cobra is the standard Go CLI framework with subcommands and flag validation

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 as migration target database; source data is legacy flat files (fixed-width, CSV, EBCDIC-encoded COBOL extracts)
data_platform.rationale: Target core system uses PostgreSQL; source formats are legacy COBOL-era files from mainframe or mid-range systems

hosting_target.value: Self-hosted bare-metal migration workstations or jump hosts; distributed as a compiled Go binary via internal artifact repository
hosting_target.rationale: On-premise execution required for data residency; single binary distribution simplifies installation on IT engineer workstations

privacy_retention_policy.value: Migration run logs retained 90 days for audit purposes; reconciliation reports retained per state insurance department data retention schedules (typically 5-7 years); source flat files remain under source system retention policy
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Migration activity logs support state insurance department exam requirements

future_scope.value: Support for XML-based legacy formats, parallel chunk processing for large policy tables, integration with data quality dashboards
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 features to handle additional legacy formats and improve throughput for large carriers

constraints.value: Must run on Linux x86_64 and Windows Server 2019 migration workstations; no internet connectivity during execution; binary must install without external package managers
constraints.rationale: IT workstations are air-gapped or restricted; single Go binary meets zero-dependency installation requirement

observability_baseline.value: Structured JSON logs written to migration_run.log; progress printed to stdout as records processed; final reconciliation report written to reconciliation_{timestamp}.json
observability_baseline.rationale: IT engineers need run-time progress visibility and post-run audit trail without external monitoring infrastructure

execution_preference.value: Single compiled Go binary; subcommand design (validate, migrate, reconcile, report); YAML config-driven field mapping; dry-run flag for validation without DB writes
execution_preference.rationale: Config-driven design allows reuse across multiple carrier migration engagements without code changes

compliance_context.value: State insurance department data retention requirements for migration audit logs; internal data governance policy requires field-level PII handling controls during migration
compliance_context.rationale: Insurance data migrations subject to state exam scrutiny; PII field handling must be documented

project.goal: CLI tool for legacy flat-file to PostgreSQL migration with field mapping and reconciliation
project.target_users: Insurance IT migration engineers
project.timeline: MVP 8 weeks, full COBOL support 14 weeks

backend.framework: Cobra CLI with subcommands validate, migrate, reconcile, report
backend.processing: Custom fixed-width and CSV parsers, packed decimal COMP-3 decoder

security.auth: Database credentials via env vars or encrypted config
security.pii_handling: PII fields masked in logs; scrubbing list configurable in mapping YAML

data.source: Fixed-width flat files, CSV, EBCDIC-encoded COBOL extracts
data.target: PostgreSQL 16 staging schema, then promotion to core system schema
data.mapping: YAML field mapping config per source file type

devops.distribution: Internal artifact repository as versioned binary
devops.environments: No deployment environments; single binary run on-premise

open_questions.chunk_size: Optimal PostgreSQL bulk insert chunk size for policy tables with 10M+ rows to be benchmarked

reasoner_reviews.product: The subcommand design with dry-run mode is well-suited for insurance IT migration teams who need to validate mappings before committing to target database writes. The YAML mapping config approach enables tool reuse across multiple carrier engagements.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go with Cobra is the correct choice for a zero-dependency binary targeting air-gapped Windows and Linux workstations. The packed decimal COMP-3 decoder is the most technically complex component and should be isolated in its own package with extensive unit tests against known COBOL field values.
reasoner_reviews.security: PII field scrubbing from migration logs is the critical security control given these tools run on workstations with variable log retention practices. The scrubbing field list in the YAML mapping config is a good pattern but must default to common PII fields (SSN, DOB, account number) if not explicitly configured.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The zero-dependency single binary constraint is non-negotiable for air-gapped IT environments. PostgreSQL bulk insert chunk size needs to be configurable to handle both small claims tables and large policy tables with 10M+ rows without OOM on workstation memory.

backend.service_design: Cobra root command with four subcommands: 'validate' (parse source file + mapping YAML, check field types against target schema, report errors without DB writes), 'migrate' (full run with PostgreSQL bulk insert), 'reconcile' (post-migration comparison of source record counts and checksum totals against target), 'report' (generate reconciliation_report.json from last run metadata)
backend.api_patterns: No HTTP API. CLI flags: --config (mapping YAML path), --source (flat file path), --target-dsn (PostgreSQL DSN), --dry-run (validate only), --chunk-size (bulk insert batch size, default 5000), --log-level (info/debug/warn), --output (report output path)
backend.failure_handling: Per-record error accumulation in error_manifest.json; configurable --fail-fast flag halts on first error vs --continue-on-error collects all errors; transformation errors do not write partial rows to target; reconciliation detects partial writes via record count mismatch
backend.scaling_notes: Sequential single-threaded processing for MVP; Phase 2 adds --parallel-chunks flag using Go goroutines for concurrent chunk inserts; PostgreSQL COPY protocol for bulk insert instead of individual INSERTs for 10x throughput improvement

security.auth_design: PostgreSQL DSN passed via MIGRATION_TARGET_DSN environment variable or --target-dsn flag; DSN masked in log output replacing password segment with asterisks
security.secrets_management: Optional encrypted config file support using AES-256-GCM with passphrase from MIGRATION_CONFIG_KEY env var for environments where env var DSN is not permitted
security.privacy_controls: PII scrubbing list in mapping YAML under pii_fields key; scrubbed fields replaced with [REDACTED] in migration_run.log; error_manifest.json never includes field values for PII-designated fields

data.entities: migration_jobs (id, source_file_path, source_format ENUM fixed_width/csv/ebcdic, mapping_config_path, target_table, started_at, completed_at, status ENUM running/completed/failed/partial, records_processed, records_failed), field_mapping_configs (id, source_field_name, source_type, source_start_pos, source_end_pos, target_column, target_type, coercion_rule, pii_flag), transformation_rules (id, rule_type ENUM date_reformat/packed_decimal/text_trim/null_coerce/lookup_map, parameters_json), reconciliation_reports (id, job_id, source_record_count, target_record_count, source_checksum, target_checksum, field_null_rate_json, status ENUM pass/fail, generated_at), error_manifests (id, job_id, source_line_number, source_field, error_type, error_message, skipped BOOL)
data.schema_notes: migration_jobs and reconciliation_reports written to a lightweight SQLite file (migration_state.db) on the workstation for audit trail; target tables are the carrier core system PostgreSQL schema defined in the mapping config
data.migration_notes: PostgreSQL COPY FROM STDIN with binary format for bulk insert; fallback to INSERT with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING for tables with unique constraints on source legacy keys

devops.deployment_topology: No server deployment; single compiled Go binary distributed via internal Nexus artifact repository as versioned release (polmigrate_v1.2.0_linux_amd64, polmigrate_v1.2.0_windows_amd64.exe)
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions matrix build: go test ./... on Linux and Windows runners, go vet, staticcheck, cross-compile binaries, goreleaser for versioned release artifacts uploaded to internal Nexus
devops.observability_stack: Structured JSON logs via zerolog written to migration_run_{timestamp}.log; progress output to stdout as records processed counter; reconciliation_report_{timestamp}.json written on run completion
devops.rollback_strategy: Migration target table uses a staging schema (migration_staging.{table}) with promotion SQL script; rollback is DROP TABLE migration_staging.{table} with no impact on live core system data

ISS-001.title: COMP-3 packed decimal decoder not specified in architecture
ISS-001.detail: The previous plan described COBOL flat-file parsing in generic terms but did not specify how COMP-3 packed decimal fields (common in COBOL policy premium and reserve amounts) are decoded. The runtime parser must handle both positive and negative packed decimals with scale factors specified in the mapping config.

history.note: Raised during security review; unparsed COMP-3 fields silently produce incorrect premium values

ISS-002.title: Reconciliation does not validate TIV or premium totals against source control file
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan described reconciliation as record count and null rate comparison only. Insurance migration reconciliation requires financial control total validation (total written premium, total reserve amounts) against cedant-provided or system-generated control files to detect transformation errors in monetary fields.

history.note: Raised by product review; record count alone is insufficient for insurance financial data migration validation

focus_issues.title: COMP-3 packed decimal decoder not specified
focus_issues.detail: Must specify COMP-3 packed decimal decoding with sign handling and scale factor support in mapping config

focus_issues.title: Reconciliation lacks financial control total validation
focus_issues.detail: Reconciliation must validate total written premium and reserve amounts against source control totals, not just record counts

latest_recommendations: Add COMP-3 packed decimal decoding specification with sign byte and scale factor handling to the backend and data model sections
Extend reconciliation report to include financial control total comparison for monetary fields (premium, reserve) against source control files

previous_plan.title: Insurance Legacy Data Migration CLI v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: A Go CLI tool using Cobra for migrating COBOL-era flat files to PostgreSQL with YAML field mapping, schema validation, and reconciliation reporting.

target_output.title: Insurance Legacy Data Migration CLI
target_output.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 CLI tool using Cobra for migrating COBOL-era fixed-width and CSV flat files to PostgreSQL 16 during carrier core system replacements. Supports YAML-configured field mapping, COMP-3 packed decimal decoding for monetary fields, data type coercion, dry-run validation, PostgreSQL COPY-based bulk insert to staging schema, and reconciliation reporting with financial control total validation. Distributed as a zero-dependency binary for air-gapped insurance IT workstations.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single Go binary built with Cobra exposing four subcommands. The 'validate' subcommand parses the source flat file using the YAML mapping config and checks all field types and coercion rules against the PostgreSQL target schema without writing any rows. The 'migrate' subcommand executes the full pipeline: source file parsing (fixed-width field extraction or CSV), COMP-3 packed decimal decoding for monetary fields, date reformatting per coercion rules, PostgreSQL COPY FROM STDIN bulk insert to migration_staging schema in configurable chunk sizes, and error accumulation to error_manifest.json. The 'reconcile' subcommand compares source record count, target record count, and optionally monetary field sums against a source control file. The 'report' subcommand reads migration state SQLite and writes reconciliation_report_{timestamp}.json. No network services, no external runtime dependencies.
target_output.technology_stack: Language: Go 1.22. CLI Framework: Cobra 1.8 with persistent flags and subcommand structure. YAML Parsing: gopkg.in/yaml.v3 for field mapping config. PostgreSQL Driver: pgx v5 with COPY protocol support. Decimal Arithmetic: github.com/shopspring/decimal for monetary field sum comparisons without floating-point error. SQLite State: modernc.org/sqlite (pure Go, no CGo) for migration_state.db. Logging: zerolog for structured JSON log output. Build: goreleaser for cross-platform binary distribution to Linux x86_64 and Windows amd64.
target_output.functional_feature_map: validate subcommand: parse source file with mapping config, validate all field type coercions against target schema, output validation_errors.json, exit non-zero on any error. migrate subcommand: full pipeline run with optional --dry-run flag for test inserts rolled back, --chunk-size for bulk insert batch tuning, --continue-on-error for non-halting runs, --control-file for reconciliation control totals. reconcile subcommand: compare source vs target record counts, optional monetary field sum validation against control file within 0.01% tolerance. report subcommand: read SQLite state and write formatted reconciliation_report.json. COMP-3 decoder package: decode packed decimal bytes with sign nibble, configurable scale_factor, returns decimal.Decimal.
target_output.system_components: Cobra root command with validate/migrate/reconcile/report subcommands and shared persistent flags (--config, --target-dsn, --log-level). Fixed-width field extractor using start/end positions from mapping YAML. CSV reader using encoding/csv. COMP-3 packed decimal decoder package (comp3.Decode accepting []byte, scale_factor int, returns decimal.Decimal, error). Date reformatter supporting YYYYMMDD, MMDDYY, Julian date inputs to RFC3339. PostgreSQL COPY bulk inserter using pgx CopyFromRows. SQLite migration_state.db writer. Reconciliation engine comparing counts and monetary sums. zerolog structured logger with PII field scrubbing middleware.
target_output.workflows: Validation Workflow: Engineer runs 'polmigrate validate --config policies_mapping.yaml --source POLFILE.DAT --target-dsn $MIGRATION_TARGET_DSN'. Tool reads fixed-width record layout from mapping YAML, extracts all fields from first 1000 records, attempts all coercion rules including COMP-3 decode for monetary fields, and checks extracted types against target PostgreSQL column types. Outputs validation_errors.json listing source line, field name, extracted raw value, attempted coercion, and error. Engineer fixes mapping config and retries before running migrate. Migration and Reconciliation Workflow: Engineer runs 'polmigrate migrate --config policies_mapping.yaml --source POLFILE.DAT --target-dsn $DSN --chunk-size 5000 --control-file POLCTL.txt'. Tool streams records in chunks of 5000, decodes COMP-3 fields using decoder package, reformats dates, and bulk-inserts to migration_staging.policies via PostgreSQL COPY. Failed rows written to error_manifest.json with source line number and error details (PII fields redacted). On completion, reconcile subcommand reads POLCTL.txt control totals (record count and sum of written_premium) and compares against target counts and SUM(written_premium_decimal) from staging table; variance >0.01% fails reconciliation with detail in report.
target_output.data_model: Field mapping YAML config structure: {source_file: {format: fixed_width, record_length: 500}, fields: [{source_name: POLPREM, source_start: 120, source_end: 126, source_type: comp3, scale_factor: 2, target_column: written_premium_decimal, target_type: numeric(18,2), pii: false}, {source_name: INSNAME, source_start: 10, source_end: 40, source_type: text, target_column: insured_name, target_type: varchar(100), pii: true}]}. SQLite migration_state.db tables: migration_jobs (id, source_file, mapping_config, target_dsn_masked, started_at, completed_at, status, records_processed, records_failed). reconciliation_reports (id, job_id, source_record_count, target_record_count, count_match BOOL, monetary_control_totals_json TEXT storing [{field, expected_sum, actual_sum, variance_pct, pass}], status ENUM pass/fail, generated_at). error_manifests (job_id, source_line, field_name, raw_bytes_hex, error_type, skipped BOOL).
target_output.api_design: CLI command surface: polmigrate validate --config <yaml> --source <file> --target-dsn <dsn> [--log-level debug|info|warn]. polmigrate migrate --config <yaml> --source <file> --target-dsn <dsn> [--chunk-size 5000] [--dry-run] [--continue-on-error] [--control-file <path>] [--output <dir>]. polmigrate reconcile --job-id <id> --control-file <path> --target-dsn <dsn>. polmigrate report --job-id <id> --output <path>. polmigrate version. Exit codes: 0 success, 1 validation errors, 2 migration errors, 3 reconciliation failure, 4 configuration error.
target_output.security_and_compliance: PostgreSQL DSN passed via MIGRATION_TARGET_DSN environment variable; --target-dsn flag supported but DSN password segment masked as [REDACTED] in all log output. PII field scrubbing: zerolog middleware replaces values of fields marked pii:true in mapping YAML with [REDACTED] in migration_run.log and error_manifest.json field values. No PII field values in stdout progress output. Optional encrypted mapping config via AES-256-GCM with passphrase from MIGRATION_CONFIG_KEY env var. State database migration_state.db uses os.OpenFile with 0600 permissions. State insurance department compliance: migration_run.log and reconciliation reports retained 90 days on workstation; IT team responsible for archival per carrier data governance policy.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Distribution: goreleaser builds polmigrate_v{version}_linux_amd64 and polmigrate_v{version}_windows_amd64.exe and uploads to internal Nexus artifact repository. IT engineers download binary, chmod +x on Linux. No installer, no package manager dependency. GitHub Actions CI matrix: go test ./... on ubuntu-latest and windows-latest runners, go vet ./..., staticcheck, go build -ldflags '-s -w' for release binaries. Release triggered by git tag vX.Y.Z. No deployment environments; tool runs on-premise on migration workstations. SQLite state database created in --output directory or current working directory on first run.
target_output.observability: zerolog structured JSON logs to migration_run_{timestamp}.log with fields: timestamp, level, job_id, records_processed, records_failed, current_chunk, phase (parsing/coercion/insert/reconcile). stdout progress line updated every 10K records: 'Processed 50000/1200000 records (4.2%) | Errors: 3 | Elapsed: 00:02:14'. Final summary printed to stdout on completion. reconciliation_report_{timestamp}.json written to output directory with all metrics. IT engineer uses log file for post-migration audit; no external monitoring infrastructure required.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost; runs on existing IT migration workstations. Binary size target <20MB with no CGo dependencies. Memory usage scales with chunk-size setting; default 5000-row chunk requires <50MB heap for typical policy record widths. For 10M-row policy tables, sequential processing at 50K records/second throughput target completes in ~3.5 minutes. Phase 2 parallel chunk processing via goroutines targets 3x throughput improvement for large policy and claims tables.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Fixed-width and CSV parser, YAML mapping loader with COMP-3 packed decimal decoder, date reformatter, PostgreSQL COPY bulk insert to staging schema, validate and migrate subcommands, reconcile with financial control total comparison, zerolog structured logging with PII scrubbing, goreleaser CI for Linux and Windows binaries. Phase 2 (6 weeks): EBCDIC encoding support for true mainframe file extracts, parallel chunk processing with goroutines, XML legacy format support, integration test suite against sample COBOL extract files from carrier test environments.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go 1.22 modules, go vet and staticcheck in CI. COMP-3 decoder package must have 100% unit test coverage including edge cases: zero value, maximum positive, maximum negative, odd and even nibble counts, scale_factor 0 through 6. No floating-point arithmetic for monetary fields; use shopspring/decimal throughout. PII field scrubbing tested with real mapping configs in unit tests using synthetic PII values. Cross-platform binary tested on both Linux and Windows in CI matrix. Mapping YAML schema validated with JSON Schema before processing begins.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: COBOL source file encoding variations (EBCDIC vs ASCII, line terminator differences) may cause silent misparse on mainframe extracts; mitigation: mapping YAML includes encoding field (ascii/ebcdic) and Phase 2 adds proper EBCDIC codec. Risk: Large policy tables (10M+ rows) may exhaust workstation memory if chunk-size set too high; mitigation: streaming parser never loads full file into memory; chunk-size defaults to 5000 rows. Tradeoff: Pure Go SQLite (modernc.org/sqlite) over mattn/go-sqlite3 to avoid CGo and enable cross-platform builds without C toolchain on Windows migration workstations. Tradeoff: Sequential processing in Phase 1 simplifies error isolation at the cost of throughput; parallel processing deferred to Phase 2 after reconciliation correctness is validated.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed COMP-3 packed decimal decoder design: sign nibble 0x0C positive, 0x0D negative, 0x0F unsigned; scale_factor per field in mapping YAML. Confirmed reconciliation financial control total validation within 0.01% tolerance. Selected shopspring/decimal over standard float64 for monetary field arithmetic. Confirmed modernc.org/sqlite (pure Go) over mattn/go-sqlite3 to avoid CGo dependency for cross-platform builds.

metadata.notes: Go CLI tool for insurance COBOL flat-file to PostgreSQL migration; revision round 2 resolves COMP-3 decoder and financial reconciliation issues from round 1.

project_goal.value: AI-powered fraud detection system for a specialty insurance carrier analyzing claim submissions in real time using LLM-assisted narrative analysis alongside structured anomaly detection to identify suspicious patterns across property, liability, and medical claims
project_goal.rationale: Core fraud prevention capability reducing claim leakage and SIU investigation backlog

target_users.value: Special Investigations Unit investigators, claims adjusters, and fraud analytics team within the carrier
target_users.rationale: SIU investigators consume fraud alerts and evidence summaries; adjusters receive automated risk flags on their assigned claims

project_class.rationale: Core value derived from LLM narrative analysis and ML anomaly scoring; system behavior driven by AI model decisions

capabilities.value: backend, data, ai_llm, auth, analytics, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Requires LLM integration for narrative analysis, anomaly ML models, REST API backend, PostgreSQL data storage, Azure AD auth, and claims system integration

complexity_level.rationale: Real-time LLM inference combined with structured anomaly scoring, claimant network graph analysis, SIU queue management, and adverse action compliance requirements

risk_level.rationale: AI model decisions trigger adverse actions against claimants; errors cause wrongful claim denials or missed fraud; EU AI Act and FCRA impose strict governance requirements

data_sensitivity.rationale: Claim data includes claimant PII, medical narratives, and financial loss details; fraud alert reasoning is commercially sensitive

external_exposure.rationale: Fraud detection system accessible only to internal SIU and claims staff; no external API surface

access_model.value: Azure AD with RBAC; investigator role (full SIU queue access), adjuster role (own claims only), analyst role (aggregate reports), admin role (model configuration)
access_model.rationale: Claims staff have role-differentiated views; investigators need cross-claim network visibility that adjusters do not

feature_scope.value: Real-time fraud scoring on claim submission, LLM narrative consistency analysis, claimant network graph anomaly detection, SIU investigation queue with evidence summaries, model decision explainability, adverse action documentation, analyst reporting dashboard
feature_scope.rationale: Full scope required for operational SIU integration and regulatory compliance

mvp_scope.value: Real-time fraud scoring pipeline with LLM narrative analysis and structured anomaly score, SIU alert queue with basic evidence summaries, Azure AD auth, claims system webhook integration
mvp_scope.rationale: Validates core detection pipeline before adding network graph and explainability features

security_baseline.value: Azure AD SSO with MFA, claim data encrypted at rest via Azure Disk Encryption, TLS 1.3 for all internal calls, LLM prompts must never include claimant PII in system prompt logs, model decision audit trail
security_baseline.rationale: Adverse action capability requires strict audit trail; PII in LLM prompts creates data residency and logging exposure risks

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript for SIU investigator queue and analyst dashboard
frontend_stack.rationale: Internal investigator-facing UI for reviewing fraud alerts and evidence; React 18 team expertise

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI for scoring API and SIU queue service; Celery for async LLM inference tasks
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI for real-time scoring endpoint; Celery enables async LLM inference without blocking claim submission webhooks

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for claims and fraud alert persistence; Redis 7 for scoring result cache; Azure Blob Storage for model artifacts
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for relational claim and alert data; Redis caches recent fraud scores to avoid re-scoring identical claim submissions on retry

hosting_target.value: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) in Azure West US 2
hosting_target.rationale: Azure-native to leverage Azure AD integration and Azure OpenAI Service for LLM inference

privacy_retention_policy.value: Fraud alert evidence retained 7 years for regulatory compliance; model decision logs retained 3 years; LLM inference prompt and response logs retained 90 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: State insurance fraud reporting statutes require multi-year retention of SIU investigation records; LLM log retention balanced against data minimization

future_scope.value: Claimant network graph fraud ring detection, real-time streaming scoring via Kafka, model performance monitoring with drift detection, FCRA adverse action letter automation
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap to expand detection scope and automate compliance workflows

constraints.value: LLM inference must use Azure OpenAI Service (no public OpenAI API endpoints); all data must remain in Azure West US 2; model scoring latency target p95 <3 seconds for real-time claim submission path
constraints.rationale: Azure OpenAI ensures data residency and enterprise logging agreements; 3-second latency compatible with claim submission UI expectations

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor with Application Insights for APM; custom Grafana dashboards for fraud alert volume, model score distributions, LLM inference latency, SIU queue depth
observability_baseline.rationale: SIU operations team needs real-time visibility into alert volume and model health

execution_preference.value: Agile 2-week sprints; API-first design enabling claims system integration before UI completion; model versioning from day one
execution_preference.rationale: Claims system integration drives business value; model versioning required for FCRA adverse action auditability

compliance_context.value: FCRA adverse action requirements when fraud score influences claim decision; state insurance fraud reporting statutes (mandatory SIU referral thresholds); EU AI Act high-risk AI system classification for claimant impact; SOC 2 Type II
compliance_context.rationale: FCRA applies to adverse action on insurance claims; EU AI Act Article 10-15 data governance and human oversight requirements apply given claimant impact

llm_integration.value: Azure OpenAI Service GPT-4o for claim narrative consistency analysis; prompts extract inconsistency flags, timeline anomalies, and suspicious pattern indicators from claim description and medical narrative fields; structured JSON output schema enforced
llm_integration.rationale: Azure OpenAI ensures data residency; GPT-4o structured output mode enables reliable JSON scoring response parsing

project.goal: Real-time AI fraud detection system for property, liability, and medical claims
project.target_users: SIU investigators, claims adjusters, fraud analysts

backend.framework: FastAPI for scoring API and SIU service
backend.async: Celery with Redis broker for LLM inference tasks

security.auth: Azure AD SSO with MFA and RBAC
security.encryption: Azure Disk Encryption at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit
security.pii_in_llm: PII fields pseudonymized before LLM prompt construction

open_questions.network_graph: Neo4j vs PostgreSQL recursive CTE for claimant network graph in Phase 2

reasoner_reviews.product: The SIU alert queue with LLM-generated evidence summaries addresses the core investigator workflow. The risk scoring combination of narrative analysis and structured anomaly detection is well-designed. Explainability output must be human-readable enough for investigators who are not data scientists.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The two-stage scoring design (fast structured anomaly score, then async LLM narrative analysis via Celery) correctly handles the 3-second latency constraint. Model versioning with champion-challenger promotion is required for FCRA auditability. Pseudonymizing PII before LLM prompt construction is the right architecture to prevent PII from appearing in Azure OpenAI Service request logs.
reasoner_reviews.security: FCRA adverse action documentation requires that every model decision influencing a claim denial has a complete audit trail including model version, input features, score, and decision timestamp. The model_decisions table must be append-only and immutable post-write. EU AI Act human oversight requirement must be reflected in architecture as mandatory investigator review before any SIU referral.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o structured output mode is the right choice for reliable JSON schema enforcement on LLM scoring responses. Token budget per claim narrative should be capped to control inference cost and latency. p95 <3 seconds is achievable with async LLM via Celery and returning the structured anomaly score synchronously while LLM result is pending.

backend.service_design: Two FastAPI services: ScoringService (POST /score/claim receives claim webhook, runs structured anomaly model synchronously, enqueues LLM narrative task to Celery, returns preliminary score in <500ms), SIUService (GET /alerts, PATCH /alerts/{id}/decision, GET /alerts/{id}/evidence providing investigator queue and decision management)
backend.llm_integration_design: Celery worker invokes Azure OpenAI Service GPT-4o with structured output schema: {narrative_flags: [{flag_type, description, confidence}], timeline_anomalies: [str], overall_narrative_risk: low/medium/high/critical}. PII pseudonymization: claimant_name replaced with CLAIMANT_A, SSN/DOB omitted, provider names replaced with PROVIDER_1 before prompt construction. Token budget: 2000 tokens max per claim narrative. Response merged with structured anomaly score to produce final fraud_probability_score.
backend.failure_handling: LLM inference failure falls back to structured-anomaly-only score with llm_status=unavailable flag; SIU queue displays fallback indicator; Celery task retry max 3 with exponential backoff; dead-letter queue for permanently failed LLM tasks surfaced in admin alert

security.auth_design: Azure AD App Registration with MSAL; FastAPI dependency injection validates Azure AD JWT on every request; role claims mapped to investigator/adjuster/analyst/admin
security.authorization_model: Adjuster role: can only retrieve fraud alerts for claims in their own adjuster_id assignment; investigator role: full SIU queue read and decision write; analyst role: aggregate reporting only; admin role: model version configuration and threshold management
security.audit_and_logging_controls: model_decisions table append-only with PostgreSQL row-level security blocking UPDATE and DELETE; every LLM inference request/response logged to azure_openai_audit_log with prompt_hash (not raw prompt), token counts, and latency; CloudTrail equivalent: Azure Monitor activity logs for all AKS and Key Vault operations
security.incident_response_notes: Fraud model compromise or scoring outage: fallback to manual SIU triage; runbook in Confluence; PagerDuty escalation to ML engineering and SIU ops within 5 minutes

data.entities: claim_submissions (id, claim_number, claim_type ENUM property/liability/medical, claimant_id, adjuster_id, submission_date, policy_id, loss_description_text, medical_narrative_text, total_claimed_amount, status), fraud_alerts (id, claim_id FK, fraud_probability_score DECIMAL, anomaly_score DECIMAL, llm_narrative_risk ENUM low/medium/high/critical, llm_status ENUM pending/complete/failed, alert_date, status ENUM new/under_investigation/referred_siu/dismissed, assigned_investigator_id), model_decisions (id, claim_id FK, model_version_id FK, input_features_hash, anomaly_score, llm_narrative_risk, combined_score, decision_timestamp, llm_task_id), investigation_cases (id, alert_id FK, investigator_id, opened_at, closed_at, disposition ENUM fraud_confirmed/no_fraud/inconclusive, siu_referral_number, fcra_adverse_action_doc_id), claimant_networks (claimant_id, related_claimant_id, relationship_type, shared_attribute, detected_at)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server for all relational data; Redis 7 for fraud score cache keyed on claim_id+model_version with 24hr TTL; Azure Blob Storage for anomaly model pickle artifacts and LLM prompt templates; model_decisions append-only via PostgreSQL row security policy
data.analytics_events: fraud_alert_created, fraud_alert_decision (investigator action), siu_referral_submitted, model_score_computed (with latency and model_version), llm_inference_completed (with token usage and latency)

devops.deployment_topology: AKS cluster West US 2 with namespaces: fraud-scoring (ScoringService pods, Celery workers), fraud-siu (SIUService pods), fraud-data (PostgreSQL Flexible Server, Redis Cache for Azure). Azure Container Registry for images. Azure Key Vault for secrets.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps pipeline: pytest with 80% coverage gate, mypy type check, ruff lint, Docker build and push to ACR, Helm chart deployment to AKS staging namespace, integration test suite against staging, manual approval gate for production deployment
devops.observability_stack: Application Insights SDK in FastAPI services for request traces, dependency tracking, and exception capture. Custom Grafana dashboards over Application Insights: fraud alert daily volume, p50/p95 scoring latency, LLM inference latency and token usage, SIU queue depth by alert status, model score distribution by claim type.
devops.rollback_strategy: Helm chart versioned releases; AKS kubectl rollout undo to previous deployment; model version rollback via admin API setting active_model_version; LLM prompt template rollback via Azure Blob version restore
