However, ISS-002 - the adverse action notification reliability gap - remains unresolved and is a regulatory compliance risk. The plan describes Pub/Sub publishing but includes no dead-letter queue, retry policy, or compliance team visibility into failed notifications.

LLM restricted to review-queue accounts keeps token spend within $800/month budget
Cloud Run traffic splitting canary deployment is appropriate for model version releases
concerns: Pub/Sub adverse action notifications have no DLQ or retry - a transient GCP failure could silently miss a state 30-day deadline
No manual retry mechanism for compliance team if a notification batch fails

recommendations: Configure Pub/Sub subscription with dead-letter topic (max_delivery_attempts=5); add Cloud Monitoring alert on dead-letter message count > 0; expose POST /v1/adverse-action-notifications/{id}/retry endpoint on review-api for compliance team manual recovery

issue_updates.title: Adverse action notification has no retry or dead-letter mechanism
issue_updates.detail: Pub/Sub publish step has no delivery guarantee; plan does not address GCP transient failure scenario

previous_audits.summary: Core triage engine design is sound but two issues prevent production readiness: raw SHAP values in underwriter review queue make the tool unusable without actuarial translation (ISS-001), and LLM summarization called for all 5000 nightly accounts will exceed budget cap (ISS-003).
strengths: Three-model ensemble with routing thresholds is actuarially defensible

Restrict LLM summarization to review-queue accounts only (estimated 30% of nightly volume)

previous_plan.title: Automated Commercial Renewal Underwriting Tool Architecture v3
previous_plan.executive_summary: Nightly batch scoring tool using a three-model ensemble to route 5000 renewing commercial accounts to auto-renew, review, or decline. LLM summarization via private Vertex AI for review-queue accounts only. SHAP explanation narratives added in this round. Adverse action notifications via Pub/Sub.
previous_plan.workflows: Nightly Batch: Cloud Scheduler triggers at 11 PM; batch-orchestrator scores all expiry accounts, routes to queues, enqueues LLM summarization for review accounts, publishes auto-decline events to adverse_action Pub/Sub topic. Notification service subscribes and sends state-specific letters. No DLQ on subscription.
previous_plan.system_components: renewal-scorer Cloud Run, batch-orchestrator Cloud Run Job, llm-summarizer Celery worker, review-api FastAPI Cloud Run. PostgreSQL 16, Redis Memorystore.
previous_plan.api_design: GET /v1/review-queue, GET /v1/accounts/{id}/triage-decision with explanation_narrative field, POST /v1/accounts/{id}/override.

best_plan.title: Automated Commercial Renewal Underwriting Tool Architecture v3
best_plan.executive_summary: Nightly batch scoring tool using a three-model ensemble to route 5000 renewing commercial accounts to auto-renew, review, or decline. LLM summarization via private Vertex AI for review-queue accounts only. SHAP explanation narratives added in this round. Adverse action notifications via Pub/Sub.
best_plan.workflows: Nightly Batch: Cloud Scheduler triggers at 11 PM; batch-orchestrator scores all expiry accounts, routes to queues, enqueues LLM summarization for review accounts, publishes auto-decline events to adverse_action Pub/Sub topic.
best_plan.system_components: renewal-scorer Cloud Run, batch-orchestrator Cloud Run Job, llm-summarizer Celery worker, review-api FastAPI Cloud Run. PostgreSQL 16, Redis Memorystore.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 4 must close ISS-002: adverse action notification reliability. The fix is adding a Pub/Sub dead-letter topic with max_delivery_attempts=5, a Cloud Monitoring alert on dead-letter message depth > 0, and a manual retry endpoint on the review-api for the compliance team. This is a critical regulatory compliance issue - missed state adverse action deadlines carry fines and market conduct examination exposure. All other architectural decisions from round 3 are sound and carry forward.

fix_report.action_taken: Added Pub/Sub dead-letter topic (adverse-action-dlq) to the adverse_action subscription with max_delivery_attempts=5. Added Cloud Monitoring alerting policy on dead-letter message count > 0 with PagerDuty escalation to compliance team. Added POST /v1/adverse-action-notifications/{id}/retry endpoint on review-api allowing compliance team to manually trigger reprocessing of failed notifications. Added retry_count and last_failure_reason columns to adverse_action_notifications table. Added Grafana panel showing adverse action notification success rate and DLQ depth.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Transient GCP Pub/Sub failures result in automatic retry up to 5 attempts; undeliverable notifications land in DLQ and alert compliance team within minutes; compliance team can manually retry via API before 30-day state deadline; full audit trail of notification delivery attempts maintained in adverse_action_notifications table

target_output.title: Automated Commercial Renewal Underwriting Tool Architecture v4
target_output.executive_summary: Nightly batch automation scoring up to 5000 renewing commercial lines accounts through a three-model ML ensemble (loss trend, exposure change, market condition) with routing to auto-renew, human-review, and auto-decline queues. LLM-generated loss narrative summaries via private Vertex AI PaLM 2 support the review queue for approximately 1500 accounts per night within the $800/month budget. Plain-language SHAP factor narratives are displayed in the underwriter review interface. Adverse action notifications use a Pub/Sub dead-letter queue with 5-retry delivery guarantee and compliance team manual retry endpoint.
target_output.architecture_overview: Four-service architecture on GCP Cloud Run within VPC Service Controls: renewal-scorer (ML inference), batch-orchestrator (nightly Celery job), llm-summarizer (async Vertex AI calls for review-queue accounts), and review-api (FastAPI underwriter interface). Cloud Scheduler triggers the 11 PM nightly batch via Pub/Sub. Auto-decline events publish to an adverse-action Pub/Sub topic with a dead-letter topic (max_delivery_attempts=5); a dedicated notification-service subscriber handles state-specific adverse action letter generation. PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL stores all triage decisions, SHAP values, LLM summaries, underwriter overrides, and notification delivery state. Redis Memorystore is the Celery broker.
target_output.technology_stack: Python 3.12 throughout. FastAPI 0.111 for review-api. Celery 5.4 with Redis broker for batch orchestration. scikit-learn 1.5 for three-model ensemble (GradientBoostingClassifier) with SHAP 0.45 for explanation values. Vertex AI PaLM 2 Text (text-bison@002) via google-cloud-aiplatform SDK on private endpoint. PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL. Redis 7 on Memorystore. GCP Cloud Run, Cloud Scheduler, Pub/Sub with dead-letter topics, Cloud Build CI, Artifact Registry, Secret Manager, Cloud Monitoring, Grafana with Prometheus.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Nightly Triage Batch: queries policy admin system for accounts expiring in 60-90 days, scores via three-model ensemble, routes to auto_renew/review/decline, writes triage_decisions, enqueues LLM summarization for review accounts, publishes decline events. Underwriter Review Queue: review-api exposes pending review accounts with triage score, plain-language factor narrative, LLM loss summary, and premium change recommendation. Override Workflow: underwriter submits POST /v1/accounts/{id}/override with decision and reason_code; original and override decisions stored. Adverse Action Path: auto-decline events on Pub/Sub trigger notification-service generating state-specific letters; DLQ with retry and compliance team endpoint. Model Performance Dashboard: Grafana shows daily routing distribution, score quartile drift vs. 30-day baseline, LLM token spend, underwriter override rate, adverse action notification success rate.
target_output.system_components: Cloud Scheduler (nightly 11 PM cron) -> Pub/Sub trigger topic -> batch-orchestrator Cloud Run Job (Celery worker: score, route, write triage_decisions, enqueue LLM tasks, publish decline events). renewal-scorer Cloud Run service: serves ML model loaded from GCS artifact; POST /internal/score endpoint. llm-summarizer Cloud Run service: Celery worker consuming summarization queue, calls Vertex AI text-bison@002, writes llm_summaries. review-api Cloud Run service: FastAPI serving GET /v1/review-queue, GET /v1/accounts/{id}/triage-decision, POST /v1/accounts/{id}/override, POST /v1/adverse-action-notifications/{id}/retry. adverse-action Pub/Sub topic with subscription (max_delivery_attempts=5, dead_letter_topic=adverse-action-dlq) -> notification-service: generates and sends state-specific adverse action letters. Cloud Monitoring alert on adverse-action-dlq message depth > 0 -> PagerDuty compliance team escalation. PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL, Redis 7 Memorystore.
target_output.workflows: Nightly Batch (11 PM - target 6 AM completion): Cloud Scheduler publishes to trigger topic; batch-orchestrator Cloud Run Job starts, queries policy admin API for 5000 expiry accounts, chunks into 100-account batches, calls renewal-scorer in parallel, applies triage thresholds (auto_renew: score < 0.25, review: 0.25-0.65, decline: score > 0.65), writes triage_decisions with SHAP values, enqueues llm-summarizer tasks for review-queue accounts only (~1500), publishes auto-decline events to adverse-action Pub/Sub. Batch job emits Prometheus metric on completion; PagerDuty fires if metric not received by 6 AM. LLM Summarization: llm-summarizer Celery worker dequeues tasks, formats prompt from loss history notes and claim narratives (no PII fields), calls text-bison@002 via private endpoint, writes llm_summaries record, updates triage_decision with llm_summary_id. Token budget monitored daily; if cumulative daily spend projects to exceed monthly cap, summarization tasks are paused and compliance team notified. Underwriter Review: underwriter opens review queue in policy admin UI (calls GET /v1/review-queue), selects account, views factor narrative from explanation_renderer, LLM summary, premium change recommendation, submits override decision via POST /v1/accounts/{id}/override. Adverse Action Notification Reliability: notification-service receives from Pub/Sub subscription with max_delivery_attempts=5; on success marks adverse_action_notifications.notification_state=sent; on failure increments retry_count and records last_failure_reason; after 5 attempts message moves to adverse-action-dlq; Cloud Monitoring alert fires; compliance officer uses POST /v1/adverse-action-notifications/{id}/retry to resubmit after root cause resolution.
target_output.data_model: renewal_accounts (id UUID PK, policy_number VARCHAR, account_name VARCHAR, line_of_business VARCHAR, naics_code CHAR(6), expiry_date DATE, current_premium DECIMAL(12,2), three_year_loss_ratio DECIMAL(5,4), account_manager_id VARCHAR, imported_at TIMESTAMP). triage_decisions (id UUID PK, account_id FK, batch_run_id FK, decision ENUM auto_renew/review/decline, composite_score DECIMAL(5,4), loss_trend_score DECIMAL(5,4), exposure_change_score DECIMAL(5,4), market_condition_score DECIMAL(5,4), shap_values JSONB, explanation_narrative TEXT, premium_change_recommendation DECIMAL(6,2), llm_summary_id FK nullable, decided_at TIMESTAMP, algorithm_version VARCHAR). llm_summaries (id UUID PK, account_id FK, decision_id FK, summary_text TEXT, prompt_tokens INT, completion_tokens INT, model_version VARCHAR, generated_at TIMESTAMP). underwriter_overrides (id UUID PK, decision_id FK, original_decision ENUM, override_decision ENUM, reason_code VARCHAR(20), notes TEXT, underwriter_id VARCHAR, overridden_at TIMESTAMP). adverse_action_notifications (id UUID PK, decision_id FK, account_id FK, state_code CHAR(2), notification_state ENUM pending/sent/failed/dlq, sent_at TIMESTAMP nullable, retry_count INT DEFAULT 0, last_failure_reason TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMP). batch_runs (id UUID PK, started_at TIMESTAMP, completed_at TIMESTAMP, accounts_processed INT, auto_renew_count INT, review_count INT, decline_count INT, status ENUM running/completed/failed).
target_output.api_design: Internal REST API on review-api Cloud Run. GET /v1/review-queue?status=pending&underwriter_id={id}&line={lob}&page={n} -> paginated list of accounts with composite_score, explanation_narrative, llm_summary. GET /v1/accounts/{id}/triage-decision -> full triage_decision including shap_values, explanation_narrative, premium_change_recommendation, llm_summary_text. POST /v1/accounts/{id}/override -> body: {override_decision: ENUM, reason_code: VARCHAR, notes: TEXT} -> returns updated decision record. GET /v1/batch-runs?limit=30 -> recent batch run summaries for ops monitoring. GET /v1/adverse-action-notifications?state=failed -> compliance team view of failed notifications. POST /v1/adverse-action-notifications/{id}/retry -> triggers reprocessing of DLQ'd notification. All endpoints authenticated via Google IAM OIDC token validated by Cloud Run IAM policy.
target_output.security_and_compliance: IAM: four dedicated service accounts with minimum roles - batch-orchestrator (Cloud SQL Client, Pub/Sub Publisher, Cloud Run Invoker), renewal-scorer (none - invoked by orchestrator), llm-summarizer (Vertex AI User scoped to text-bison@002 model resource), review-api (Cloud SQL Client, Secret Manager Accessor). VPC Service Controls perimeter prevents egress from GCP project to public internet, enforcing Vertex AI private endpoint requirement. Secret Manager: PostgreSQL connection strings, LLM endpoint URL, and notification service SMTP credentials stored as versioned secrets with automatic rotation reminder. Adverse action compliance: every triage_decision with decision=decline triggers a Pub/Sub message; notification-service generates state-specific letter within 24 hours; adverse_action_notifications table tracks delivery; 30-day regulatory deadline monitored via a daily Cloud Monitoring alert. 7-year retention: triage_decisions and shap_values archived to GCS Coldline storage after 2 years in PostgreSQL; retrieval SOP documented for regulatory examination. ECOA/fair lending: SHAP values reviewed by actuary before each model version release for proxy variable presence; review documented in model governance log.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Build CI: on PR, runs pytest (85% coverage gate), mypy, model unit tests validating known analytical outputs against frozen fixture. On merge to main: builds Docker images, pushes to Artifact Registry, deploys to staging Cloud Run services. Staging validation: compliance team runs adverse action notification end-to-end test against staging environment including DLQ trigger. On version tag: production deployment via Cloud Run traffic splitting (10% canary for 30 minutes monitoring routing distribution for anomalies, then 100%). Model artifact versioning: each scikit-learn model version stored in GCS with immutable retention; batch-orchestrator loads model from specific GCS path in environment variable, enabling instant rollback by changing environment variable. Cloud Run service minimum instances set to 1 for review-api to eliminate cold-start latency for underwriters; batch services scale from 0.
target_output.observability: Grafana dashboards sourced from Prometheus metrics exported by each Cloud Run service. Batch run dashboard: job start/end time, accounts processed per minute, routing distribution (auto_renew/review/decline counts and percentages), LLM tasks queued vs. completed. Model health dashboard: composite score p25/p50/p75 per day with 30-day rolling baseline - PagerDuty alert if p50 drifts > 0.05. LLM budget dashboard: daily token spend per account, cumulative monthly spend vs. $800 cap - alert at 80% of cap. Notification reliability dashboard: adverse_action_notifications success rate, DLQ message depth, oldest unresolved DLQ message age. Cloud Logging structured logs from all services with correlation to batch_run_id and account_id for end-to-end tracing.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Run scales to 0 between batch windows; estimated monthly compute cost $180 for batch execution and review-api. PostgreSQL db-custom-2-8192 instance ~$130/month. Redis Memorystore Basic ~$40/month. LLM: text-bison@002 at approximately $0.00025/1K input tokens and $0.0005/1K output tokens; 1500 accounts/night with 800-token prompt and 300-token completion = $0.52/night = ~$16/month, well within $800 cap. Total infrastructure estimate: ~$380/month. Scaling to 10K accounts/night requires upsizing PostgreSQL write IOPS and adding Cloud Run concurrency; scoring parallelism already horizontally scalable.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (deployed): nightly triage batch, three-model ensemble, Pub/Sub adverse action trigger, PostgreSQL persistence, review-api with override endpoint. Phase 2 (deployed): LLM summarization restricted to review-queue accounts, SHAP explanation_renderer with plain-language narratives. Phase 3 (deployed round 3): model performance Grafana dashboard, Cloud Build canary deployment pipeline, LLM token budget monitoring. Phase 4 current release: Pub/Sub dead-letter topic with 5-retry policy, Cloud Monitoring DLQ alert to compliance team PagerDuty, POST /v1/adverse-action-notifications/{id}/retry endpoint, adverse action Grafana panel. Phase 5 roadmap: real-time single-account scoring API for mid-term endorsements, model A/B testing framework with traffic split, catastrophe concentration scoring integration.
target_output.development_guidelines: All services use Python 3.12 with strict mypy type checking. Pydantic v2 models for all API request/response schemas and internal data transfer. pytest for unit and integration tests with 85% coverage gate enforced in Cloud Build. Parameterized tests for triage_engine covering all three routing decision boundaries. Model unit tests validate known analytical outputs against frozen test fixtures (no live model inference in CI). LLM prompts managed as versioned template files in prompts/; no prompt strings hardcoded in Python. Pub/Sub message schemas versioned in a shared schemas/ package with backward-compatible evolution policy. All database migrations managed with Alembic; no manual schema changes.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Three-model ensemble may exhibit correlated failure modes in unusual market conditions, routing too many accounts to decline simultaneously. Mitigation: daily routing distribution alert fires if decline rate exceeds 2x 30-day baseline before adverse action notifications are sent. Risk: Vertex AI PaLM 2 model deprecation (text-bison@002 has a sunset timeline). Mitigation: LLM model version parameterized in Secret Manager; migration path to Gemini documented. Tradeoff: Celery + Redis over a managed queue service (Cloud Tasks) - Celery provides richer job state visibility for the compliance team but adds Redis operational overhead. Accepted as Redis is already used and team has operational experience. Risk: ECOA proxy variable in ML features - geographic rating territory variables may correlate with protected class. Mitigation: actuary SHAP proxy review gate before every model version release.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed private Vertex AI endpoint over Azure OpenAI - GCP-native keeps data within VPC Service Controls perimeter. Confirmed Pub/Sub with DLQ over SQS - consistent with GCP-only infrastructure. Confirmed underwriter_overrides stored with original decision (not replacing it) to maintain complete audit trail for regulatory examination. Confirmed LLM restricted to review-queue accounts only after ISS-003 budget analysis.

metadata.notes: Commercial renewal triage tool; round 4 closes adverse action notification DLQ reliability gap; ISS-001 (SHAP narratives) and ISS-003 (LLM budget) already resolved in prior rounds.

profile.domain: Insurance Policy Form Compliance Scanner CLI

project_goal.value: CLI tool for compliance officers at insurance carriers to scan policy form libraries for outdated mandatory state endorsements, comparing in-force form editions against state DOI-published approved form catalogs and flagging mismatches before a policy is issued
project_goal.rationale: Prevents non-compliant policy issuance that could expose the carrier to regulatory fines and market conduct examination findings

target_users.value: Compliance officers and policy administration system release engineers
target_users.rationale: Two personas: manual compliance scans by officers and automated CI gate by release engineers

project_class.rationale: Command-line binary invoked both interactively by compliance staff and as a CI gate

capabilities.rationale: HTTP integration with DOI catalog API, local SQLite state for catalog caching, data transformation for mismatch reporting

complexity_level.rationale: DOI API integration with multiple state schemas, local catalog caching, and severity-rated mismatch reporting require careful design

risk_level.rationale: Tool reads internal form library data; no write access to production systems; incorrect mismatch reporting could allow non-compliant issuance

data_sensitivity.rationale: Form library data is internal proprietary data but not customer PII or financial records

external_exposure.rationale: Binary runs on compliance officer workstations and CI runners; no inbound network services

access_model.value: No authentication required; tool runs on trusted internal workstations; DOI API calls use a shared read-only API key stored in local config
access_model.rationale: DOI catalog APIs are read-only public data; no auth required for the CLI itself

feature_scope.value: State-specific form library scan, DOI catalog fetch and local cache, edition mismatch detection, severity-rated mismatch report (critical/warning/info), CI non-zero exit on critical mismatches, multi-state batch scan, catalog update command, YAML-based scan configuration per state
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set approved by compliance and engineering teams

mvp_scope.value: Single-state scan with DOI catalog fetch, mismatch report to terminal, non-zero exit code for critical mismatches
mvp_scope.rationale: Core compliance gate use case for a single state before multi-state expansion

security_baseline.value: DOI API key stored in ~/.config/form-scanner/config.yaml with 0600 file permissions; no form library data logged; exit codes follow POSIX conventions for CI integration
security_baseline.rationale: Prevents API key exposure in shared CI environments

frontend_stack.value: N/A - CLI tool with terminal output and JSON/CSV report files
frontend_stack.rationale: No browser UI; output is terminal tables and machine-readable report files

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Typer CLI framework
backend_stack.rationale: Typer provides type-annotated CLI with auto-generated help; Python ecosystem has strong HTTP and SQLite support

data_platform.value: SQLite for local DOI catalog cache; no external database
data_platform.rationale: Catalog caching avoids DOI API calls on every scan; SQLite is zero-configuration and portable

hosting_target.value: Distributed as a Python package via internal PyPI mirror; installed with pip or pipx on compliance workstations and CI runners
hosting_target.rationale: PyPI package enables pip install and version pinning in CI requirements files

privacy_retention_policy.value: Local SQLite catalog cache and scan reports retained per user; no central data collection; cache TTL 24 hours to avoid stale DOI data
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Tool is stateless at the platform level; data ownership is with the operator

future_scope.value: Multi-carrier form library support, DOI catalog webhook for real-time updates, integration with policy administration release gate, historical mismatch trend reporting
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP roadmap items approved by compliance leadership

constraints.value: Must work offline using cached DOI catalogs; DOI API rate limit of 100 requests per hour per key; Python 3.10+ required on target workstations
constraints.rationale: Compliance officers often work offline; DOI API has strict rate limits

observability_baseline.value: Structured JSON logs to stderr; --verbose flag for debug output; scan summary printed to stdout; exit code 0 clean, 1 critical mismatch, 2 warning only, 3 error (API failure, config missing)
observability_baseline.rationale: CI pipelines need deterministic exit codes; humans need readable terminal output

execution_preference.value: Kanban; releases coordinated with compliance team review of test scan results against known mismatch fixtures
execution_preference.rationale: Compliance sign-off required before each release

project.goal: CLI tool to scan carrier form libraries against DOI approved catalogs and flag edition mismatches
project.target_users: Compliance officers and CI release pipelines
project.timeline: Before next state rate filing window

backend.framework: Typer
backend.output: Rich for terminal tables, JSON/CSV for machine-readable reports

reasoner_reviews.product: The round 1 tool covers the single-state scan workflow but the mismatch report lacks the severity detail compliance officers need to prioritize remediation. A critical endorsement mismatch (mandatory endorsement entirely absent) is very different from a warning (wrong edition year but same form content), and the report must distinguish them clearly.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The SQLite catalog cache is the right approach for offline support but the round 1 cache has no invalidation beyond manual delete. The 24-hour TTL requirement from the contract must be enforced in the schema with a fetched_at timestamp per state catalog, and the scan command must check TTL before using cached data.
reasoner_reviews.security: The DOI API key in config.yaml with 0600 permissions is adequate for workstation use, but CI environments share runners where another job could read the file. The CI use case should support the FORM_SCANNER_DOI_API_KEY environment variable as an alternative to the config file, with the env var taking precedence.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 100 req/hour DOI rate limit is only relevant for multi-state batch scans; a single-state scan is 1-3 requests. The batch scan command needs a rate limiter that tracks requests per hour and inserts appropriate sleeps rather than failing mid-batch.

backend.service_design: Single Python package with Typer CLI. Command groups: scan (state, batch), catalog (update, status, clear), config (init, show). Each command is a function in a dedicated module under form_scanner/commands/.
backend.api_patterns: httpx AsyncClient with retry (max 3, exponential backoff) for DOI API calls. DOI API response schemas vary by state; a state-specific adapter pattern normalizes responses to the internal FormEdition dataclass.
backend.business_modules: catalog_client: fetches and caches DOI approved form catalogs per state in SQLite with TTL check. form_library_reader: parses the carrier's form library from CSV or directory of form metadata files. mismatch_detector: compares in-force form editions against cached catalog, classifying mismatches as critical (form absent from catalog or edition superseded by mandatory update), warning (edition outdated but not yet mandatory), or info (form present in newer edition available). report_renderer: formats mismatch results as terminal table (Rich), JSON file, or CSV file.
backend.failure_handling: DOI API unavailable with empty cache: exit code 3 with message instructing user to run 'form-scanner catalog update' while online. Partial batch scan failure: completed states written to output, failed states listed with error. Exit code 1 on any critical mismatch regardless of other states.

security.secrets_management: FORM_SCANNER_DOI_API_KEY env var takes precedence over config.yaml API key. Config init command creates ~/.config/form-scanner/config.yaml with 0600 permissions. Warning printed if config file has permissions wider than 0600.
security.abuse_prevention: DOI API rate limiter tracks request timestamps in SQLite rate_log table per API key; before each request, counts requests in the past 60 minutes and sleeps if approaching the 100/hour limit.

data.entities: state_catalogs (state_code CHAR(2) PK, catalog_json TEXT, fetched_at TIMESTAMP, ttl_seconds INT), catalog_forms (state_code, form_number VARCHAR, edition_date DATE, form_title VARCHAR, mandatory_as_of DATE nullable, supersedes_edition_date DATE nullable), rate_log (id INT PK, api_key_hash VARCHAR, requested_at TIMESTAMP), scan_results (id INT PK, scanned_at TIMESTAMP, state_code, form_number, in_force_edition DATE, catalog_edition DATE, severity ENUM critical/warning/info, mismatch_reason TEXT)
data.storage_design: SQLite file at ~/.local/share/form-scanner/cache.db. catalog_client checks fetched_at < NOW() - ttl_seconds before using cached data; if stale, fetches fresh from DOI API.
data.data_risks: Form library files may be checked into version control; documentation instructs users to add form_library/ to .gitignore when using local directory input.

devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: on PR runs ruff lint, mypy --strict, pytest with coverage gate (80%). On tag: builds wheel with build, publishes to internal PyPI via twine. CI environments use FORM_SCANNER_DOI_API_KEY env var from GitHub Actions secret.
devops.rollback_strategy: Prior package versions retained on internal PyPI. CI requirements files pin version; rollback is a version pin change in the policy admin CI config.

ISS-001.title: Mismatch severity not differentiated - all mismatches shown as errors
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan classified all form edition mismatches as the same severity. Compliance officers cannot distinguish between a mandatory endorsement absent from the form library (critical - blocks issuance) and an optional newer edition available (info - advisory only). Report is not actionable.

history.note: Compliance officer tested round 1 output and flagged it as unusable for prioritization

focus_issues.title: Mismatch severity not differentiated - all mismatches shown as errors
focus_issues.detail: All form edition differences classified identically; compliance officers cannot prioritize critical mandatory endorsement gaps over advisory edition updates.

latest_recommendations: Implement three-tier mismatch severity: critical (mandatory endorsement absent or superseded by mandatory update), warning (edition outdated but not yet required), info (optional newer edition available). Exit code 1 for critical, exit code 2 for warning-only. CI gate blocks on exit code <= 1.
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Insurance Policy Form Compliance Scanner CLI Architecture v1

convergence_state.reason: High-severity mismatch classification gap makes tool non-actionable for compliance officers

previous_audits.summary: The CLI architecture and DOI integration approach are sound, but the flat mismatch severity model renders the tool non-actionable for compliance officers. A critical mandatory endorsement gap and an advisory edition update are treated identically, making the report impossible to use for compliance prioritization.
strengths: SQLite catalog cache with offline support is correctly designed for the constraint
httpx with retry is appropriate for DOI API integration
Exit code convention for CI gate is well-specified
concerns: All mismatches classified as the same severity regardless of regulatory impact
No distinction between mandatory and optional form updates in the DOI catalog model
blocking_issues: ISS-001: flat severity model makes compliance officer output non-actionable; tool cannot be used in production until this is resolved
recommendations: Add mandatory_as_of and supersedes_edition_date fields to catalog_forms table from DOI API responses; implement severity logic: critical if mandatory_as_of is not null and <= today and in-force edition < superseded edition; warning if mandatory_as_of is in future; info if optional update available

issue_updates.title: Mismatch severity not differentiated
issue_updates.detail: catalog_forms table and mismatch_detector have no severity classification logic; all outputs are identical

previous_plan.title: Insurance Policy Form Compliance Scanner CLI Architecture v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Python/Typer CLI tool scanning carrier form libraries against DOI state catalogs. SQLite cache for offline support. Single mismatch category reported.
previous_plan.workflows: Scan: CLI reads form library CSV, fetches DOI catalog (or uses cache), compares form editions, outputs mismatch list. All mismatches reported at the same level with no severity differentiation.
previous_plan.data_model: state_catalogs (state_code, catalog_json, fetched_at), catalog_forms (state_code, form_number, edition_date, form_title). No mandatory_as_of or supersedes fields.
previous_plan.api_design: scan state --state TX --library forms.csv --output report.json. Exits 0 if no mismatches, 1 if any mismatch regardless of type.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must resolve ISS-001 by implementing three-tier mismatch severity classification. The fix requires adding mandatory_as_of and supersedes_edition_date to the catalog_forms schema (populated from DOI API responses), and implementing severity logic in mismatch_detector: critical when a mandatory update is past its effective date and the in-force form is behind, warning when mandatory update is future-dated, info when optional. Exit code 1 for critical mismatches enables CI blocking. This changes the data model, mismatch_detector business logic, report rendering, and exit code taxonomy.

fix_report.action_taken: Added mandatory_as_of DATE nullable and supersedes_edition_date DATE nullable columns to catalog_forms SQLite table, populated from DOI API response fields 'mandatoryEffectiveDate' and 'supersedesEdition'. Implemented three-tier severity logic in mismatch_detector: critical = mandatory_as_of IS NOT NULL AND mandatory_as_of <= today AND in_force_edition < supersedes_edition_date; warning = mandatory_as_of IS NOT NULL AND mandatory_as_of > today; info = optional newer edition available with no mandatory date. Updated report_renderer to group and color-code mismatches by severity (Rich: red critical, yellow warning, blue info). Updated exit code logic: exit 1 on any critical, exit 2 on warnings only, exit 0 if only info or clean.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Compliance officers see prioritized mismatch report with critical mandatory gaps highlighted in red; CI pipeline exits 1 and blocks deployment on mandatory endorsement violations; advisory updates shown as informational and do not block issuance

target_output.title: Insurance Policy Form Compliance Scanner CLI Architecture v2
target_output.executive_summary: Python 3.12/Typer CLI tool for compliance officers to scan carrier form libraries against DOI state-approved endorsement catalogs and detect mandatory edition mismatches before policy issuance. Three-tier severity classification (critical/warning/info) distinguishes mandatory regulatory violations from advisory updates. SQLite-cached DOI catalogs support offline operation. CI integration blocks policy administration deployments on critical mismatches. DOI API key configurable via environment variable or config file for CI runner compatibility.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single Python package installable via pip/pipx from internal PyPI mirror. Typer command tree with three groups: scan (state, batch), catalog (update, status, clear), and config (init, show). The catalog_client module fetches state DOI form catalogs via httpx and persists them to a local SQLite cache with 24-hour TTL per state. The form_library_reader parses the carrier's form library from CSV or a directory of form metadata YAML files. The mismatch_detector compares in-force editions against cached catalog entries using mandatory_as_of and supersedes_edition_date fields to classify mismatches as critical, warning, or info. The report_renderer outputs Rich terminal tables with color-coded severity rows or machine-readable JSON/CSV for CI integration.
target_output.technology_stack: Python 3.12. Typer 0.12 for CLI command tree with type annotations. httpx 0.27 with tenacity retry for DOI API calls. SQLite via Python stdlib sqlite3. Rich 13 for terminal table rendering with color-coded severity. Pydantic v2 for config file validation and DOI API response models. State-specific DOI API adapters normalizing to internal FormEdition dataclass. GitHub Actions CI with ruff, mypy --strict, pytest. Build: Python build + twine for internal PyPI distribution. Install: pip or pipx.
target_output.functional_feature_map: form-scanner scan state --state TX --library forms.csv --output report.json: scans a single state form library against the cached DOI TX catalog, outputs severity-classified mismatch report. form-scanner scan batch --states TX,CA,FL --library forms.csv: multi-state scan with rate limiter; exits 1 if any state has critical mismatches. form-scanner catalog update --state TX: fetches fresh DOI catalog for TX, stores in SQLite cache. form-scanner catalog status: shows last fetch time and TTL remaining per cached state. form-scanner catalog clear --state TX: removes cached catalog to force refresh. form-scanner config init: creates ~/.config/form-scanner/config.yaml with 0600 permissions. --verbose flag: debug-level httpx request/response logging to stderr.
target_output.system_components: Typer CLI entry point (form_scanner/main.py). catalog_client module (httpx DOI API calls, SQLite TTL cache management, state-specific adapter classes for TX/CA/FL/NY DOI API response schemas). form_library_reader module (CSV and YAML form metadata parsers producing list of PolicyForm dataclass). mismatch_detector module (compares PolicyForm editions against CatalogForm entries using mandatory_as_of and supersedes_edition_date; returns MismatchResult with severity ENUM). report_renderer module (Rich table with severity color-coding; JSON and CSV serializers). rate_limiter module (SQLite rate_log table tracking DOI API requests per hour). config module (Pydantic Settings reading from config.yaml and FORM_SCANNER_DOI_API_KEY env var). SQLite database at ~/.local/share/form-scanner/cache.db.
target_output.workflows: Single-State Compliance Scan: Compliance officer runs 'form-scanner scan state --state TX --library carrier_forms.csv'. CLI loads config (env var FORM_SCANNER_DOI_API_KEY preferred, then config.yaml). Checks SQLite cache for TX catalog: if fetched_at + ttl_seconds > NOW(), uses cached data; otherwise fetches from DOI TX API (1-3 requests, rate limiter checks). form_library_reader parses carrier_forms.csv to list of PolicyForm(form_number, edition_date, state_code). mismatch_detector iterates: for each PolicyForm, looks up matching CatalogForm; if not found -> critical (form absent); if found and mandatory_as_of <= today and in_force < supersedes_edition -> critical; if mandatory_as_of > today -> warning; if optional newer edition -> info; if edition matches -> clean. report_renderer prints Rich table grouped by severity (red critical rows, yellow warning, blue info), summary counts, and writes report.json. Exits 1 if any critical, 2 if warning-only, 0 if info/clean. CI Gate: Policy administration CI pipeline runs 'form-scanner scan batch --states TX,CA,FL --library release_forms.csv --output compliance_report.json' using FORM_SCANNER_DOI_API_KEY GitHub Actions secret. GitHub Actions step fails on exit code 1, blocking deployment. Exit code 2 produces a warning annotation but does not block.
target_output.data_model: SQLite tables at ~/.local/share/form-scanner/cache.db. state_catalogs: state_code CHAR(2) PK, fetched_at TIMESTAMP, ttl_seconds INT DEFAULT 86400. catalog_forms: id INTEGER PK, state_code CHAR(2), form_number VARCHAR(20), edition_date DATE, form_title VARCHAR(200), mandatory_as_of DATE nullable, supersedes_edition_date DATE nullable, optional_since_date DATE nullable. rate_log: id INTEGER PK, api_key_hash VARCHAR(64), requested_at TIMESTAMP. scan_history: id INTEGER PK, scanned_at TIMESTAMP, state_code CHAR(2), library_file VARCHAR, critical_count INT, warning_count INT, info_count INT. Python dataclasses: PolicyForm(form_number: str, edition_date: date, state_code: str, form_title: str). CatalogForm(form_number: str, edition_date: date, mandatory_as_of: date | None, supersedes_edition_date: date | None). MismatchResult(form_number: str, in_force_edition: date, catalog_edition: date, severity: Severity, reason: str).
target_output.api_design: CLI command API. 'form-scanner scan state --state <STATE> --library <CSV_PATH> [--output <JSON_PATH>] [--format json|csv|table]': exit codes 0 clean, 1 critical mismatch, 2 warning only, 3 tool error. 'form-scanner scan batch --states <COMMA_LIST> --library <CSV_PATH> [--output-dir <DIR>]': runs state scans sequentially with rate limiter; aggregate exit code is worst across all states. 'form-scanner catalog update --state <STATE> [--force]': fetches DOI catalog even if TTL not expired when --force used. 'form-scanner catalog status [--state <STATE>]': tabulates state, last_fetched, ttl_remaining_hours for all cached states or specific state. DOI API integration: GET https://doi.{state}.gov/api/forms?type=endorsement&line={lob} via state-specific adapter (response schema varies; adapter normalizes to CatalogForm list).
target_output.security_and_compliance: API key precedence: FORM_SCANNER_DOI_API_KEY environment variable checked first; if unset, reads from ~/.config/form-scanner/config.yaml. Config init command creates config file with os.chmod(path, 0o600). On scan startup, tool checks config file permissions and warns if wider than 0600. No form library data included in structured logs (log only form_number, not edition details or form titles). SQLite cache stores only DOI catalog data (public information); no carrier proprietary form content stored. Rate limiter prevents accidental key throttling during batch scans by sleeping when within 10 requests of the 100/hour limit.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Package: form-scanner Python package built with python-build, published to internal PyPI mirror on tag push via GitHub Actions + twine. Install on workstations: 'pipx install form-scanner' or 'pip install form-scanner==1.2.0'. CI install: 'pip install form-scanner==1.2.0' in pipeline setup step pinned to a specific version. Upgrade: pipx upgrade form-scanner or pip install --upgrade form-scanner. No daemon, no server. SQLite database auto-created on first run with schema migration via a version table. Config initialized by 'form-scanner config init' with guided prompts for DOI API key.
target_output.observability: Structured JSON logs to stderr using Python logging with a JSONFormatter. Log fields: timestamp, level, command, state_code, action, duration_ms. --verbose flag enables DEBUG level including httpx request URLs (API key redacted from URL params). Scan summary always printed to stdout: 'Scanned 47 forms against TX DOI catalog: 2 critical, 1 warning, 3 info, 41 clean.' Scan history stored in scan_history SQLite table; 'form-scanner catalog status' shows recent scan summary. Exit codes: 0 = all clean or info only, 1 = one or more critical mismatches, 2 = warnings only no critical, 3 = tool error (API unavailable, missing config, invalid input).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost; runs on developer workstations and CI runners. DOI API calls are rate-limited public endpoints; 24-hour SQLite cache means typically 0-3 DOI API calls per scan day for a single state. Multi-state batch scan for 10 states: up to 30 DOI API calls, rate limiter ensures these stay within 100/hour. Package size approximately 2MB including dependencies. SQLite cache file is kilobytes of catalog data per state.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (delivered): single-state scan with DOI catalog fetch, SQLite cache, basic mismatch detection, terminal output, exit code for CI. Phase 2 current (this release): three-tier severity classification (critical/warning/info) with mandatory_as_of and supersedes fields, color-coded Rich terminal table, exit code 1/2/0 severity-mapped, env var API key for CI. Phase 3 planned: multi-state batch scan command with rate limiter, JSON and CSV output formats, catalog status command showing TTL remaining. Phase 4 roadmap: YAML-based per-state scan configuration for line-of-business scoping, scan history dashboard command, integration with policy admin CI gate as a pre-deploy hook.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with strict mypy type checking (mypy --strict). ruff for linting and formatting in CI. All Pydantic models for external data (DOI API responses, config file). Pytest with parameterized tests for mismatch_detector covering all severity boundary cases (mandatory_as_of = today, mandatory_as_of = tomorrow, no mandatory_as_of). State-specific DOI adapter tests use httpx respx mock to avoid real API calls in CI. 80% coverage gate enforced in GitHub Actions. No API key or form library data in any test fixture. Semantic versioning; changelog entry required in release PR.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: DOI API schemas change without notice when states update their portals. Mitigation: state-specific adapter classes isolate schema changes; adapter tests against recorded response fixtures catch breaking changes before release. Risk: mandatory_as_of date is not present in all state DOI API responses. Mitigation: when mandatory_as_of is absent, mismatch is classified as info (not critical); compliance documentation notes that some states require manual verification. Tradeoff: SQLite over in-memory cache - adds schema migration complexity but enables offline scan support (contract requirement). Risk: compliance officer scans stale cache after a DOI emergency mandate. Mitigation: catalog status command shows TTL remaining; documentation instructs users to run 'catalog update' before any issuance-gating scan.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Python over Go - team expertise and rich httpx + Rich library ecosystem outweigh Go's binary distribution advantage given pipx availability. Confirmed SQLite over flat file cache - relational schema supports TTL TTL queries and rate_log without custom parsing. Confirmed three-tier severity (critical/warning/info) over binary (error/ok) to match how compliance officers prioritize remediation work.

metadata.notes: Policy form compliance scanner CLI; round 2 adds three-tier mismatch severity classification resolving ISS-001 non-actionable flat error output.

profile.domain: Surplus Lines Tax Filing Automation Tool

project_goal.value: Automated surplus lines tax filing tool for a wholesale insurance broker, calculating and remitting state surplus lines taxes and stamping fees on non-admitted policies, generating state-specific affidavits, and submitting electronically to state stamping offices
project_goal.rationale: Replaces manual spreadsheet-based tax calculation and paper affidavit filing, reducing compliance risk and staff hours during quarterly filing windows

target_users.value: Surplus lines compliance staff at the wholesale broker and state stamping office portal APIs as integration targets
target_users.rationale: Internal compliance team as operators; state stamping office APIs as external integration partners

project_class.rationale: Backend batch automation with external integrations; no end-user UI beyond a compliance dashboard for monitoring filings

capabilities.rationale: Tax calculation engine, state portal integrations, ACH remittance, batch processing, and financial reconciliation all required

complexity_level.rationale: 50-state tax rate schedules, multi-authority payment routing, and state portal API integration are complex but well-bounded

risk_level.rationale: Incorrect tax calculations or missed filing deadlines cause regulatory fines and license revocation risk; ACH payment errors cause financial loss

data_sensitivity.rationale: Processes policy premium amounts, tax remittance transactions, and ACH payment credentials

external_exposure.rationale: Tool integrates with state stamping office portal APIs (SLIP, SLTX, FSLSO, etc.) as external partner systems

access_model.value: Internal staff access via custom JWT authentication to the compliance dashboard API; state portal credentials stored in AWS Secrets Manager per state; ACH origination via Nacha-compliant ACH gateway with origination credentials in Secrets Manager
access_model.rationale: Isolates state credentials and ACH origination credentials from application code

feature_scope.value: Policy ingestion from broker management system, per-state surplus lines tax calculation using current tax rate schedules, stamping fee calculation, state-specific affidavit document generation, electronic submission to state stamping office portals, ACH remittance batch to state tax authorities, reconciliation report matching payments to filings, filing deadline calendar with alerts, tax rate schedule management
feature_scope.rationale: Full compliance automation workflow identified with surplus lines compliance team

mvp_scope.value: Policy ingestion, tax and stamping fee calculation for top 5 premium states (TX, CA, FL, NY, IL), affidavit generation, manual download for portal submission, reconciliation report
mvp_scope.rationale: Top 5 states cover approximately 70% of the broker's surplus lines premium volume

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 for all state portal API calls, mTLS for stamping office partners requiring it (SLTX), AWS Secrets Manager for state credentials and ACH origination keys, all financial transactions logged in immutable audit trail, field-level encryption for ACH bank account details
security_baseline.rationale: State portal partner APIs require mTLS in several states; ACH origination requires PCI-aligned credential controls

frontend_stack.value: N/A - backend automation; compliance dashboard is a minimal read-only status interface using React served from the same FastAPI application
frontend_stack.rationale: No rich UI required; compliance staff need a filing status dashboard, not an interactive application

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Celery for task orchestration
backend_stack.rationale: Celery provides reliable task scheduling and retry for the batch filing workflows; Python has strong PDF generation and ACH formatting libraries

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for policy, filing, and payment records; Redis 7 as Celery broker
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for durable financial records with ACID guarantees; Redis for Celery task queue

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1 behind an ALB; Celery workers as separate Fargate tasks
hosting_target.rationale: Fargate for managed containers; us-east-1 for proximity to east-coast stamping office APIs

privacy_retention_policy.value: Policy and filing records retained 7 years per state surplus lines statute; ACH transaction records retained 7 years; affidavit PDFs retained in S3 with 7-year lifecycle rule
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: State surplus lines statutes require 7-year record retention for regulatory examination

future_scope.value: Automated portal submission for all 50 states, real-time NRRA home-state allocation for multi-state risks, direct integration with all major surplus lines stamping offices, insured notification letter generation
future_scope.rationale: Full 50-state automation after MVP validation of top-5 state accuracy

constraints.value: Monthly ACH remittance batches must clear by state statutory deadlines (typically the 30th of the month following the quarter); tax rate schedule updates must take effect on the correct effective date without retroactive recalculation of prior filings
constraints.rationale: Missing statutory ACH deadlines triggers automatic late payment penalties; retroactive tax recalculation would require amended filings

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM for Celery task monitoring, filing submission success/failure rates, ACH batch status; PagerDuty alert if ACH batch fails to submit by T-2 days before statutory deadline; structured JSON logs for all state portal API calls
observability_baseline.rationale: Filing deadline misses are regulatory events; early warning on ACH batch failures is critical

execution_preference.value: Biweekly sprints with compliance team sign-off on tax calculation accuracy against known test cases before each release
execution_preference.rationale: Tax calculation correctness is the core product quality gate

compliance_context.value: State surplus lines tax statutes (50-state), NRRA home-state tax allocation rules, NAIC Surplus Lines Insurance Model Act, ACH Nacha operating rules for payment origination
compliance_context.rationale: Surplus lines tax filing is a heavily state-regulated activity; ACH origination requires Nacha compliance

project.goal: Automate surplus lines tax calculation, affidavit generation, portal filing, and ACH remittance
project.target_users: Surplus lines compliance staff
project.timeline: Before Q3 filing window

backend.framework: FastAPI + Celery

security.auth: custom JWT for compliance dashboard

constraints.retroactive: no retroactive recalculation on rate schedule updates

reasoner_reviews.product: The scope is well-bounded around the highest-volume states for MVP. The affidavit generation requirement is critical because most stamping offices only accept filings with a properly formatted state-specific affidavit, and format requirements vary materially by state. A template system with per-state affidavit templates is essential.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Celery with PostgreSQL and Redis is the right stack for this problem. The tax calculation engine must be designed so that tax rate schedules are versioned with effective dates and the calculation always looks up the rate in effect on the policy effective date, not the filing date. This is crucial for retroactive filing corrections and amendment workflows.
reasoner_reviews.security: mTLS for SLTX (Texas surplus lines) is a hard requirement and needs a dedicated mTLS client certificate managed in Secrets Manager with automated rotation reminder. The ACH origination credentials must never be in application code or environment variables; only Secrets Manager with fine-grained IAM. Field-level encryption for ACH bank routing and account numbers using AWS KMS is required before storing in PostgreSQL.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The Nacha ACH deadline constraint requires a T-2 PagerDuty alert but also a dead-letter queue for failed ACH batches, since a failed ACH submission at T-0 has no recovery path within the statutory window. The Celery task for ACH submission must have idempotent design with an idempotency key per batch to prevent double-submission on retry.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with three Celery workers: filing-worker (policy ingestion, tax calculation, affidavit generation, portal submission), payment-worker (ACH batch assembly, submission, reconciliation), notification-worker (deadline alerts, compliance team notifications). Tax engine as a separate Python module with versioned rate schedule lookups.
backend.api_patterns: State portal integrations via state-specific adapter classes implementing a common SurplusLinesPortalAdapter interface with methods: submit_filing(affidavit_pdf, policy_data) -> SubmissionReceipt, check_status(filing_id) -> FilingStatus. Adapters handle portal-specific auth (API key, mTLS cert, session cookie) and response parsing.
backend.business_modules: tax_calculator module: given policy_effective_date and state_code, looks up tax_rate_schedules WHERE state_code = ? AND effective_from <= policy_effective_date ORDER BY effective_from DESC LIMIT 1; applies surplus_lines_tax_rate and stamping_fee_rate to gross_premium. affidavit_builder module: selects Jinja2 template by state_code and line_of_business, populates with policy and calculation data, renders to PDF via weasyprint. ach_assembler module: groups outstanding non_admitted_policies with status=tax_calculated by state_code and payment_authority, builds Nacha ACH file, submits via ACH gateway API.
backend.failure_handling: Portal submission failures go to a filing_submissions retry queue (max 3 retries, exponential backoff). After 3 failures, status set to manual_review and compliance team alerted. ACH submission uses idempotency key (batch_id + state_code + remittance_period); gateway rejects duplicate key if already processed. Dead-letter queue for ACH batches that exhaust retries.

security.auth_design: Compliance dashboard: custom JWT issued by FastAPI /auth/login endpoint, signed with HS256 key from Secrets Manager, 8-hour expiry. No public registration; accounts managed by IT admin.
security.secrets_management: One Secrets Manager secret per state containing portal_api_key, portal_username, portal_password, mtls_cert_arn (where required). ACH origination credentials in a separate Secrets Manager secret with access restricted to payment-worker IAM task role. All secrets fetched at task startup, never logged.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every filing_submission and remittance_transaction record is append-only with created_at and no update allowed (use status state machine). Separate audit_events table for all state portal API calls, ACH batch submissions, and underwriter overrides. CloudTrail for all Secrets Manager access.
security.privacy_controls: ACH bank_account_number and routing_number encrypted with AWS KMS data key before INSERT into payment_accounts table. Decrypted only in payment-worker memory during ACH file assembly.

data.entities: non_admitted_policies (id UUID PK, policy_number VARCHAR, insured_name VARCHAR, line_of_business VARCHAR, gross_premium DECIMAL(12,2), policy_effective_date DATE, policy_expiration_date DATE, home_state CHAR(2), states_of_risk TEXT[], imported_at TIMESTAMP, status ENUM ingested/tax_calculated/affidavit_generated/submitted/stamped). surplus_lines_taxes (id UUID PK, policy_id FK, state_code CHAR(2), gross_premium_allocated DECIMAL(12,2), tax_rate DECIMAL(6,5), tax_amount DECIMAL(10,2), stamping_fee_amount DECIMAL(10,2), total_due DECIMAL(10,2), rate_schedule_id FK, calculated_at TIMESTAMP). tax_rate_schedules (id UUID PK, state_code CHAR(2), surplus_lines_tax_rate DECIMAL(6,5), stamping_fee_rate DECIMAL(6,5), effective_from DATE, effective_to DATE nullable, authority VARCHAR). affidavits (id UUID PK, policy_id FK, state_code CHAR(2), template_version VARCHAR, s3_key VARCHAR, generated_at TIMESTAMP). filing_submissions (id UUID PK, affidavit_id FK, portal_adapter VARCHAR, submission_status ENUM pending/submitted/accepted/rejected/manual_review, portal_confirmation_number VARCHAR, submitted_at TIMESTAMP, accepted_at TIMESTAMP). remittance_transactions (id UUID PK, state_code CHAR(2), remittance_period VARCHAR, total_amount DECIMAL(12,2), policy_count INT, ach_batch_id VARCHAR, submission_status ENUM pending/submitted/cleared/failed, submitted_at TIMESTAMP, cleared_at TIMESTAMP, idempotency_key VARCHAR UNIQUE). payment_accounts (id UUID PK, state_code CHAR(2), payment_authority VARCHAR, routing_number_encrypted BYTEA, account_number_encrypted BYTEA, account_type ENUM checking/savings, kms_key_id VARCHAR)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with append-only pattern for all financial records. S3 bucket for affidavit PDFs with 7-year lifecycle rule (transition to Glacier after 2 years). Separate S3 bucket for ACH batch files with versioning enabled.
data.retention_and_deletion: PostgreSQL financial records: 7-year retention with archival to S3 Glacier-compatible cold storage after 5 years. Celery task result retention: 24 hours in Redis. Affidavit PDFs: S3 lifecycle transitions to S3 Glacier at 2 years, deletion at 7 years.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster with three task definitions: api-task (FastAPI + compliance dashboard), filing-worker-task (Celery filing worker, 2 tasks), payment-worker-task (Celery payment worker, 1 task). ALB routes HTTPS traffic to api-task. Internal service communication via VPC-internal ALB. Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ, ElastiCache Redis.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: on PR run pytest (90% coverage gate on tax_calculator and ach_assembler), mypy strict, ruff. On main merge: Docker build, push ECR, ECS rolling deployment to staging. On tag: production deployment with ECS blue/green via CodeDeploy with 10-minute bake period.
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS CodeDeploy blue/green enables instant rollback to previous task definition revision. Tax rate schedule updates are INSERT-only with effective_from date; rollback is a new INSERT with corrected rates.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a surplus lines tax automation tool with several critical design constraints: tax rate schedules must be versioned with effective dates so calculations always use the rate in effect on the policy effective date (not filing date); ACH remittance batches need idempotency keys to prevent double-payment on retry; mTLS is required for Texas SLTX portal; and all financial records must be append-only for 7-year retention. The state portal adapter pattern isolates the 50-state API complexity from the core calculation engine. Choosing Celery over a simpler scheduler because the filing and payment workers need reliable retry with dead-letter handling for missed ACH deadlines.
target_output.title: Surplus Lines Tax Filing Automation Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: Backend automation tool for a wholesale surplus lines broker to calculate state surplus lines taxes and stamping fees on non-admitted policies, generate state-specific affidavits, submit to state stamping office portals, and remit ACH payments to state tax authorities by statutory deadlines. Tax rate schedules are versioned with effective dates ensuring policy-date-accurate calculations. ACH batches use Nacha idempotency keys preventing double-submission on retry. mTLS client certificates handle SLTX and other portal requirements. All financial records are append-only with 7-year S3 retention for regulatory examination.
target_output.architecture_overview: FastAPI application hosted on ECS Fargate with three specialized Celery workers: filing-worker (policy ingestion, tax calculation, affidavit PDF generation, portal submission), payment-worker (ACH batch assembly and Nacha file submission), and notification-worker (deadline alerts, compliance team notifications). State portal integrations are abstracted behind a SurplusLinesPortalAdapter interface with per-state implementations handling API key, mTLS cert, or session-cookie authentication as required by each state's stamping office. Tax rate schedule lookups are versioned by effective_from date, ensuring retroactive filing amendments always recalculate at the historically correct rate. PostgreSQL 16 stores all financial records in append-only fashion. S3 holds affidavit PDFs and ACH batch files with 7-year lifecycle. Secrets Manager holds all state portal credentials and ACH origination keys.
target_output.technology_stack: Python 3.12. FastAPI 0.111 for API and compliance dashboard. Celery 5.4 with Redis 7 broker. PostgreSQL 16 on Aurora Multi-AZ. Jinja2 + WeasyPrint for affidavit PDF generation from per-state templates. Nacha library for ACH NACHA file formatting. httpx with tenacity retry for state portal API calls. mTLS via Python ssl module with client certificate from Secrets Manager for SLTX. AWS ECS Fargate, ALB, S3, Secrets Manager, KMS, CloudTrail. Datadog APM. GitHub Actions CI with CodeDeploy blue/green.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Policy Ingestion: Celery task polls broker management system API for new non-admitted policies, creates non_admitted_policies records, triggers tax calculation. Tax Calculation: tax_calculator module retrieves versioned rate schedule by (state_code, policy_effective_date), computes surplus_lines_tax_amount and stamping_fee_amount per state risk allocation, writes surplus_lines_taxes records. Affidavit Generation: affidavit_builder selects per-state Jinja2 template, populates with policy and tax data, renders PDF via WeasyPrint, uploads to S3, writes affidavits record. Portal Submission: state-specific adapter submits affidavit PDF to stamping office portal, captures confirmation number, updates filing_submissions status. ACH Remittance Batch: monthly Celery task aggregates accepted filings by state, assembles Nacha ACH file per payment authority, submits to ACH gateway with idempotency key, updates remittance_transactions. Reconciliation Report: queries filing_submissions and remittance_transactions for the period, produces CSV reconciliation matching ACH payment amounts to individual policy filings. Filing Deadline Calendar: notification-worker evaluates statutory deadlines per state, sends PagerDuty alert at T-2 days for pending ACH batches.
target_output.system_components: FastAPI api-task (compliance dashboard, policy ingest webhook endpoint, filing status API). filing-worker Celery task (tax_calculator, affidavit_builder, portal adapter dispatcher). payment-worker Celery task (ach_assembler, Nacha file builder, ACH gateway client, reconciliation_generator). notification-worker Celery beat task (deadline calendar evaluation, PagerDuty client). State portal adapters: SLIPAdapter (Surplus Lines Information Portal), SLTXAdapter (Texas with mTLS), FLSOAdapter (Florida), NYSLSAAdapter (New York), ILSLSAdapter (Illinois). tax_calculator module with versioned rate_schedule lookup. affidavit_builder with per-state Jinja2 templates in templates/{state_code}/affidavit.html. Secrets Manager secret resolver (fetched at task startup per state). Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ. ElastiCache Redis 7. S3 buckets: affidavit-pdfs (7-year lifecycle), ach-batch-files (versioned). KMS key for ACH account field encryption.
target_output.workflows: Policy Tax Calculation and Affidavit Generation: Celery beat task polls broker management system API every 15 minutes for policies with imported_at > last_checked_at. For each new policy: (1) NRRA home-state rule applied to allocate premium across states of risk; (2) for each state, tax_calculator looks up tax_rate_schedules WHERE state_code = {state} AND effective_from <= policy_effective_date ORDER BY effective_from DESC LIMIT 1, computes surplus_lines_tax_amount = gross_premium_allocated * surplus_lines_tax_rate, stamping_fee_amount = gross_premium_allocated * stamping_fee_rate; (3) affidavit_builder selects Jinja2 template for (state_code, line_of_business), populates affidavit fields, renders PDF via WeasyPrint, uploads to S3 under affidavits/{state}/{year}/{policy_number}.pdf; (4) policy status updated to affidavit_generated; (5) portal submission enqueued for state. Monthly ACH Remittance: Cloud Scheduler triggers payment-worker on the 25th of each month. Worker queries surplus_lines_taxes JOIN filing_submissions WHERE accepted_at IS NOT NULL AND remittance_period = current_quarter GROUP BY state_code, payment_authority. For each group, ach_assembler builds a Nacha ACH credit entry to the state tax authority routing/account (decrypted from payment_accounts via KMS). Nacha file uploaded to S3 ach-batch-files/{period}/{state}/. ACH gateway API called with idempotency key = batch_id + state_code + period. On success, remittance_transactions status updated to submitted. Notification-worker sends T-2 PagerDuty alert for any state with ACH batch not yet in submitted status.
target_output.data_model: non_admitted_policies (id UUID PK, policy_number VARCHAR UNIQUE, insured_name VARCHAR, line_of_business VARCHAR(50), gross_premium DECIMAL(12,2), policy_effective_date DATE, policy_expiration_date DATE, home_state CHAR(2), states_of_risk TEXT[], imported_at TIMESTAMP, status ENUM ingested/tax_calculated/affidavit_generated/submitted/stamped). surplus_lines_taxes (id UUID PK, policy_id FK, state_code CHAR(2), gross_premium_allocated DECIMAL(12,2), tax_rate DECIMAL(6,5), tax_amount DECIMAL(10,2), stamping_fee_rate DECIMAL(6,5), stamping_fee_amount DECIMAL(10,2), total_due DECIMAL(10,2), rate_schedule_id FK, calculated_at TIMESTAMP). tax_rate_schedules (id UUID PK, state_code CHAR(2), surplus_lines_tax_rate DECIMAL(6,5), stamping_fee_rate DECIMAL(6,5), effective_from DATE, effective_to DATE nullable, authority VARCHAR, source_citation VARCHAR). affidavits (id UUID PK, policy_id FK, state_code CHAR(2), template_version VARCHAR(10), s3_key VARCHAR, generated_at TIMESTAMP). filing_submissions (id UUID PK, affidavit_id FK, portal_adapter VARCHAR(20), submission_status ENUM pending/submitted/accepted/rejected/manual_review, portal_confirmation_number VARCHAR, submitted_at TIMESTAMP, accepted_at TIMESTAMP, failure_reason TEXT). remittance_transactions (id UUID PK, state_code CHAR(2), payment_authority VARCHAR, remittance_period VARCHAR(7), policy_count INT, total_amount DECIMAL(12,2), ach_batch_id VARCHAR, nacha_file_s3_key VARCHAR, submission_status ENUM pending/submitted/cleared/failed, submitted_at TIMESTAMP, cleared_at TIMESTAMP, idempotency_key VARCHAR UNIQUE). payment_accounts (id UUID PK, state_code CHAR(2), payment_authority VARCHAR, routing_number_enc BYTEA, account_number_enc BYTEA, account_type ENUM checking/savings, kms_key_id VARCHAR). audit_events (id UUID PK, event_type VARCHAR, actor VARCHAR, resource_type VARCHAR, resource_id UUID, state_code CHAR(2), outcome VARCHAR, details JSONB, occurred_at TIMESTAMP).
target_output.api_design: Internal FastAPI REST API. POST /api/v1/policies/ingest (webhook from broker management system, body: non-admitted policy JSON) -> 202 Accepted with policy_id. GET /api/v1/policies/{id}/taxes -> tax calculation breakdown per state with rate schedule IDs. GET /api/v1/filings?state={state}&status={status}&period={period} -> paginated filing_submissions list. GET /api/v1/filings/{id} -> filing detail with affidavit S3 presigned URL. GET /api/v1/remittances?state={state}&period={period} -> remittance_transactions for period. POST /api/v1/remittances/{id}/retry -> manually trigger retry for failed ACH batch. GET /api/v1/rate-schedules?state={state}&effective_date={date} -> applicable tax rates. POST /api/v1/rate-schedules -> insert new rate schedule with effective_from date. All endpoints require JWT Bearer auth. State portal adapters use external APIs: SLIP portal REST API, SLTX SOAP API with mTLS client cert, FLSO REST API, NYSLI REST API. ACH gateway: Nacha-formatted file upload to ACH gateway POST /ach/submit with idempotency key header.
target_output.security_and_compliance: mTLS for SLTX: SLTXAdapter loads client certificate and private key from Secrets Manager secret 'sltx-mtls-cert' at worker startup, configures ssl.SSLContext for all SLTX SOAP API calls. Certificate rotation: 90-day reminder alert via SNS 60 days before expiry. ACH credential isolation: payment-worker ECS task role has Secrets Manager GetSecretValue permission scoped to 'ach-origination-credentials' only; other task roles have no ACH secret access. KMS encryption: ACH routing_number_enc and account_number_enc fields encrypted with AWS KMS data key via GenerateDataKey before INSERT; decrypted in payment-worker memory only during ACH file assembly. Append-only financial records: no UPDATE or DELETE on surplus_lines_taxes, filing_submissions, or remittance_transactions; status changes are new rows with supersedes_id FK. 7-year retention: S3 lifecycle rule on affidavit-pdfs bucket transitions to S3 Glacier Instant Retrieval at 2 years; Object Lock (compliance mode) prevents deletion before 7 years. NRRA compliance: home-state tax allocation logic follows 2011 NRRA rules; only home state receives surplus lines tax for multi-state risks. Nacha compliance: ACH files formatted to Nacha CCD+ format; idempotency key prevents duplicate origination entries on retry.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster: api-task (1 task, 0.5 vCPU / 1GB), filing-worker-task (2 tasks, 1 vCPU / 2GB each), payment-worker-task (1 task, 0.5 vCPU / 1GB). ALB with HTTPS listener and WAF (AWS managed rules). Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ db.r6g.large; ElastiCache Redis 7 cache.t3.medium. GitHub Actions CI: pytest (90% coverage gate on tax_calculator and ach_assembler modules), mypy --strict, ruff, Hadolint for Dockerfiles. On main merge: Docker build -> ECR push -> ECS staging deployment. On version tag: ECS blue/green via AWS CodeDeploy with 10-minute observation window; automatic rollback on alarms. Celery beat scheduler on filing-worker schedules: policy ingest (every 15 min), monthly ACH batch (cron 0 6 25 * *), deadline alerts (daily 8 AM). Secrets Manager automatic rotation: JWT signing key every 30 days; state portal credentials annually with compliance team review.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM trace on every Celery task execution: task name, state_code, policy_id, duration, status. Custom Datadog metrics: surplus_lines.filings.submitted_count (tagged by state, line_of_business), surplus_lines.ach.batch_status (gauge per state: 0=pending, 1=submitted, 2=cleared, -1=failed), surplus_lines.tax_calculator.calc_duration_ms (histogram). Datadog dashboards: daily filing submission rate by state, ACH remittance status by state and period, portal adapter error rate, calculation throughput. PagerDuty alerts: (1) ACH batch for any state not in submitted status at T-2 days before statutory deadline; (2) portal adapter consecutive failure rate > 3 for same state; (3) Celery payment-worker task failure after 3 retries (DLQ message). CloudTrail for all Secrets Manager GetSecretValue calls. Structured JSON logs: all portal API calls logged with portal_adapter, state_code, http_status, duration_ms, filing_id (never logs policy premium values).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate: ~$80/month for api + 3 workers at current task sizes. Aurora db.r6g.large Multi-AZ: ~$240/month. ElastiCache cache.t3.medium: ~$30/month. S3 affidavit storage: ~$5/month growing at ~200MB/month for typical filing volume. ACH gateway: transaction-based per-batch fee (~$0.30/batch/state = ~$18/month for 5 states). Total infrastructure: ~$380/month. Scaling: filing-worker scales to 4 tasks during quarterly filing windows (Celery concurrency adjustment via ECS service update). Tax calculator is CPU-bound; vertical scaling of filing-worker task to 2 vCPU handles 10x volume increase.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Policy ingestion webhook, tax calculation engine with versioned rate schedules for TX/CA/FL/NY/IL, affidavit PDF generation for 5 states using Jinja2 templates, affidavit download (no portal submission yet), reconciliation report, compliance dashboard with filing status table, GitHub Actions CI with coverage gate. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Automated portal submission for SLIP and FLSO adapters, SLTX mTLS adapter for Texas, ACH remittance batch with Nacha file generation and ACH gateway integration, idempotency key enforcement, T-2 PagerDuty deadline alert. Phase 3 (4 weeks): NY and IL portal adapters, monthly ACH reconciliation report, KMS field-level encryption for payment_accounts, 7-year S3 Object Lock lifecycle. Phase 4 roadmap: remaining 45-state portal adapters, NRRA multi-state risk allocation engine, insured adverse action notification letters.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy --strict. Pydantic v2 for all API request/response models and Celery task input/output schemas. pytest with 90% coverage gate on tax_calculator (parameterized tests for all 5 states with known-correct calculations validated by compliance team) and ach_assembler (Nacha file format tests against Nacha spec). State portal adapters tested with httpx respx mock; SLTX SOAP adapter tested with mTLS fixture. No financial amounts (premium, tax, ACH) in any log message - use IDs only. Append-only database pattern enforced via application layer: no UPDATE or DELETE statements in ORM layer for financial tables. All rate schedule changes deployed as INSERT-only via a dedicated rate schedule management API with compliance team approval workflow.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: ACH gateway rejects Nacha file format due to state-specific origination requirements that differ from standard CCD+ format. Mitigation: end-to-end ACH test with sandbox credentials for each state before production launch; Nacha library allows custom batch header fields. Risk: State DOI updates tax rates without advance notice causing an underpayment filing. Mitigation: tax rate schedule management dashboard alerts compliance team when a rate_schedule has effective_from approaching within 30 days; compliance team subscribes to state bulletin RSS feeds. Risk: SLTX mTLS certificate expiry causes filing submission outage for Texas. Mitigation: SNS alert 60 days before certificate expiry with renewal SOP; secondary certificate staged and ready to swap. Tradeoff: append-only financial records over mutable status updates adds schema complexity (supersedes_id FK, status state machine) but is required for immutable audit trail. Tradeoff: Celery over AWS Step Functions - Celery gives the team direct retry and dead-letter control and avoids Step Functions Lambda cold-start costs during infrequent batch runs.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed NRRA home-state-only tax for multi-state risks rather than attempting to pro-rate across all states of risk - NRRA 2011 clearly assigns surplus lines tax to the home state only, which simplifies the calculation engine significantly. Confirmed Nacha CCD+ format for all state ACH payments rather than seeking state-specific ACH file formats. Confirmed S3 Object Lock (compliance mode) over application-layer retention enforcement for 7-year records to meet regulatory examination requirements.

metadata.notes: Surplus lines tax filing automation with versioned rate schedules, mTLS SLTX portal adapter, Nacha ACH idempotency, and 7-year append-only financial record retention.
