target_output.thinking_summary: Revising the bordereaux validator CLI to resolve Excel format support absence and unsafe YAML config loading. BordereauxParser gains an Excel branch using Polars scan_excel with calamine engine and a --sheet-name flag; file format is auto-detected from extension. ConfigLoader is updated to use yaml.safe_load throughout and validate the loaded config dict against a bundled JSON Schema using the jsonschema library, providing clear error messages for malformed YAML configs.

fix_report.action_taken: Added Excel parsing branch to BordereauxParser: auto-detects .xlsx and .xls extensions, calls pl.read_excel with engine='calamine' (faster than openpyxl for large sheets), added --sheet-name CLI flag defaulting to first sheet. Updated functional_feature_map, system_components, and workflows to reflect Excel support.
fix_report.expected_outcome: MGAs submitting bordereaux in Excel format can be validated without manual conversion to CSV.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced all yaml.load calls with yaml.safe_load in ConfigLoader. Added JSON Schema validation of the loaded config dict using jsonschema.validate with a bundled schemas/mga-config-schema.json. Config-check command now validates both YAML syntax and schema conformance. Updated security_and_compliance and development_guidelines sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Adversarial YAML config files cannot execute arbitrary Python code; malformed configs produce clear schema validation error messages with field paths.

target_output.title: MGA Bordereaux Validator CLI Architecture v2
target_output.executive_summary: Python Typer CLI tool for data engineers to validate MGA bordereaux files (CSV and Excel) against per-MGA YAML validation configurations before ETL pipeline ingestion. Uses Polars lazy evaluation for sub-60-second processing of 500K-row files, a registry-based rule engine for extensible business rules, and JSON and CSV report output for CI integration. Safe YAML loading with JSON Schema config validation prevents malformed or adversarial config execution.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-process Python CLI application with no network dependencies or persistent database. BordereauxParser auto-detects input file format (CSV via pl.scan_csv lazy scan, Excel via pl.read_excel with calamine engine) and returns a Polars LazyFrame for rule evaluation. ConfigLoader reads per-MGA YAML configs using yaml.safe_load, validates against a bundled JSON Schema, and produces a typed ValidationConfig dataclass. RuleEngine iterates the config rule list, dispatches each rule to its registered RuleClass, collects FieldError and ReconciliationResult objects. ReportWriter serializes to JSON and optionally CSV in the caller-specified --output-dir. All components are pure Python functions with no global state, enabling pytest unit testing without file I/O mocking.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Python 3.12. CLI: Typer 0.12 with Rich for colored terminal output and progress bars on large files. Dataframe: Polars 0.20 with lazy scan for CSV and calamine engine for Excel. Config parsing: PyYAML safe_load, jsonschema 4.x for config schema validation. Report serialization: stdlib json and csv. Distribution: PyPI package via hatch build, PyInstaller single-binary via GitHub Actions release workflow. CI: GitHub Actions with pytest, ruff lint, mypy type checking.
target_output.functional_feature_map: validate command: parse bordereaux file (CSV or Excel), load and validate MGA YAML config, run rule engine checks (field nullability, data type conformance, allowed value lists, premium sum reconciliation, date range plausibility), write JSON and optional CSV report to --output-dir, print summary table to stdout, exit 0/1/2. profile command: generate per-field statistics (null rate, dtype, min, max, cardinality) without rule evaluation, write profile JSON. config-check command: validate YAML syntax and JSON Schema conformance, print schema errors with field paths. list-rules command: print all registered RuleClass names with parameter documentation.
target_output.system_components: Typer CLI entry point with four commands. BordereauxParser: pl.scan_csv for .csv files, pl.read_excel(engine='calamine') for .xlsx/.xls, sheet auto-detection with --sheet-name override. ConfigLoader: yaml.safe_load, jsonschema.validate against schemas/mga-config-schema.json, returns ValidationConfig dataclass. RuleEngine: dict-based rule registry mapping YAML rule names to RuleClass callables; built-in rules: NullabilityRule, DtypeRule, AllowedValuesRule, PremiumReconciliationRule, DateRangeRule. ReportWriter: writes validation_report_{mga_id}_{timestamp}.json and optional .csv to --output-dir with path traversal prevention. PyInstaller binary wraps all components into a single executable for CI distribution.
target_output.workflows: CSV Bordereaux Validation: Data engineer runs 'bxval validate --file monthly_bx.csv --config configs/mga-alpha.yaml --output-dir ./reports'. ConfigLoader reads mga-alpha.yaml with safe_load, validates against bundled schema, raises ConfigError with JSON Schema field path on violation. BordereauxParser calls pl.scan_csv lazily, applies schema inference. RuleEngine evaluates 8 configured rules against the LazyFrame, collecting FieldError objects with row_index, field_name, rule_name, expected, actual. ReportWriter writes JSON report and prints summary: '45,321 rows validated. FAIL: 12 errors in premium_amount (dtype), 3 nullability violations in policy_number.' Exit code 1. Excel Bordereaux Validation: Data engineer receives an MGA Excel file, runs 'bxval validate --file quarterly_bx.xlsx --config configs/mga-beta.yaml --sheet-name Bordereaux'. BordereauxParser detects .xlsx extension, calls pl.read_excel(source, engine='calamine', sheet_name='Bordereaux'). Same rule evaluation and report pipeline as CSV path. For large Excel files (>100K rows), progress bar via Rich displays sheet loading progress.
target_output.data_model: YAML config structure per MGA (versioned in Git): mga_id STRING, schema_version STRING, fields LIST of (name, nullable BOOL, dtype ENUM string/integer/float/date/boolean, allowed_values OPTIONAL LIST), reconciliation OBJECT (premium_total_field, row_count_field, tolerance_pct FLOAT), business_rules LIST of (rule ENUM DateRangeRule/..., params OBJECT). JSON report output schema: mga_id, file_path, file_format ENUM csv/excel, sheet_name OPTIONAL, processed_at ISO8601, row_count INT, pass BOOL, error_count INT, warning_count INT, field_errors LIST of (row_index, field_name, rule_name, expected, actual), reconciliation_result OBJECT (expected_total, actual_total, delta, passed BOOL), rule_results LIST of (rule_name, passed, violation_count).
target_output.api_design: CLI command interface (no HTTP API). bxval validate --file PATH --config PATH --output-dir PATH [--format json|csv|both] [--sheet-name NAME] [--log-level DEBUG|INFO|WARNING]. bxval profile --file PATH [--sheet-name NAME] --output-dir PATH. bxval config-check --config PATH. bxval list-rules. Exit codes: 0 all rules passed, 1 one or more validation failures, 2 tool error (file not found, config parse error, Polars parse failure). Structured JSON log lines to stdout at INFO level: timestamp, level, command, mga_id, row_count, duration_ms.
target_output.security_and_compliance: YAML config loading uses yaml.safe_load exclusively; yaml.full_load and yaml.load without Loader are prohibited by ruff custom rule in CI. Loaded config dict validated against bundled schemas/mga-config-schema.json using jsonschema.validate before any field is accessed. --output-dir validated against path traversal: resolved path must be under the current working directory or an explicit allowlist prefix; tool errors on traversal attempt. Input CSV and Excel cell content is never evaluated or executed; Polars treats all string cells as literal values. No secrets, credentials, or network calls in the tool. Bordereaux files are read-only; no mutation of input files.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: ruff lint, mypy strict type checking, pytest with 85% coverage gate (all RuleClass implementations, ConfigLoader schema validation, BordereauxParser format detection, path traversal prevention). Build stage: hatch build for PyPI sdist and wheel; PyInstaller build for single-binary artifacts for Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64. Artifacts attached to GitHub Release on version tag. PyPI publish via trusted publisher (OIDC, no long-lived tokens). Developers install via pip install bxval or download binary from Releases. CI pipelines in the ETL repo invoke the binary directly without Python environment setup.
target_output.observability: Structured JSON log lines to stdout with fields: timestamp, level, command, mga_id, file_path, row_count, duration_ms, error_count. Log level controlled by --log-level flag. Validation report JSON written to --output-dir for downstream CI artifact archiving. CI pipeline reads exit code for pass/fail gate; parses JSON report for error_count in optional dashboard step. No remote telemetry; the tool is fully offline.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost; runs on local workstations and CI runner compute. Polars calamine Excel engine processes 100K-row Excel files in under 15 seconds on CI runner hardware. CSV lazy scan handles 500K rows in under 30 seconds. PyInstaller binary is approximately 35MB including Python runtime; acceptable for CI artifact download. No per-use cost; distributed as open internal package.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (2 weeks): Typer CLI scaffold, BordereauxParser for CSV and Excel (calamine), ConfigLoader with safe_load and JSON Schema validation, NullabilityRule, DtypeRule, PremiumReconciliationRule, JSON report output, GitHub Actions CI with pytest and binary build. Phase 2 (1 week): AllowedValuesRule, DateRangeRule, CSV report output format, Rich progress bar for large file loading, config-check and list-rules commands, PyPI publish workflow. Phase 3 (2 weeks): HTML report output for business user review, direct S3 input source (boto3 for bordereaux from carrier SFTP drop S3 bucket), schema drift detection comparing config schema_version between reporting periods.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with full type annotations. Ruff for lint and style (line length 100, isort). Mypy strict mode. All RuleClass implementations must be pure functions receiving a Polars LazyFrame and ValidationConfig, returning a list of FieldError dataclasses; no side effects. yaml.safe_load is the only permitted YAML loading function; ruff noqa comments on any yaml.load call are blocked by CODEOWNERS. pytest fixtures provide small synthetic bordereaux CSV and Excel files covering null, type, and reconciliation failure cases. All CLI flags must have a --help docstring. PyInstaller spec file versioned in repo; binary reproducibility verified by checksumming artifacts in CI.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Polars calamine Excel engine memory usage on very large Excel files (>500K rows with many columns). Mitigation: add row limit check before read_excel; recommend CSV conversion for files exceeding 200K rows. Risk: MGA schema drift between reporting periods silently passing validation if schema_version is not incremented. Mitigation: config-check warns when file row structure does not match config field list count. Tradeoff: Polars over pandas chosen for 3-5x faster large CSV scan at the cost of a less familiar API for data engineers; mitigated by comprehensive usage examples in README. Tradeoff: PyInstaller binary over pip install chosen for CI runner ease of use at the cost of a larger artifact size.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Calamine engine selected over openpyxl for Excel parsing due to 4x faster large sheet loading in benchmarks. jsonschema library selected over pydantic for YAML config validation to avoid introducing a heavy dependency for a simple schema check. --output-dir defaults to current working directory when not specified, consistent with standard CLI tool conventions.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision adding Excel format support and safe YAML loading to MGA bordereaux validator CLI for insurance data engineers.

project.goal: Educate cooperative managers and drive enrollment inquiries for parametric drought and rainfall insurance
project.target_users: Agricultural cooperative managers and crop insurance agents
project.timeline: 4 weeks to launch before spring planting season

frontend.content: Markdown with frontmatter for product pages
frontend.calculator: Client-side Alpine.js for trigger threshold calculator interactivity

devops.environments: branch preview for PRs, production on main merge

open_questions.calculator_lib: Alpine.js vs Astro Islands with React for trigger calculator

reasoner_reviews.product: The interactive trigger threshold calculator is the highest-value conversion feature on the site. Cooperative managers need to see concrete payout estimates for their specific county and crop type before they will engage with the sales team. Product pages organized by crop type (corn, soybeans, wheat, cotton, almonds) rather than by coverage type match how agricultural decision-makers think about their risk. The contact inquiry form routing to the sales CRM is a day-1 requirement.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro 4 with static export is the correct choice for a zero-backend public site with a 90+ Lighthouse performance target. The trigger calculator requires client-side interactivity; Alpine.js as an Astro Island is the lightest dependency for a simple input-output calculator without a full React component tree. Netlify Forms handles the inquiry form without any backend infrastructure. Markdown content with YAML frontmatter is editable by non-technical staff via GitHub web editor.
reasoner_reviews.security: Content Security Policy headers should be set in netlify.toml to restrict script sources to self and the Plausible Analytics domain only. The contact inquiry form must include a honeypot field to filter bot submissions before they reach the sales CRM. No cookies are set by Plausible Analytics, so no cookie consent banner is required.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Astro static build completes in under 30 seconds for a site of this page count, well within the 90-second Netlify budget. Lighthouse 90+ is achievable with Astro zero-JS pages and Tailwind CSS purge. Markdown content editing via GitHub web editor requires no local tooling for marketing staff.

backend.service_design: No server-side runtime. Netlify Forms with reCAPTCHA v2 and honeypot field for contact inquiry form spam prevention. Form submissions forwarded to HubSpot CRM via Netlify Forms webhook integration.
backend.api_patterns: No API. Static data files: crop-types.json, trigger-tables.json (county-level trigger thresholds and historical payout rates by peril) embedded at build time as Astro content collections.
backend.failure_handling: Netlify Forms submission errors display inline form error message. Netlify deploy failure triggers Slack notification. No runtime error handling required for static content.

security.auth_design: No authentication. All pages are publicly accessible.
security.abuse_prevention: Netlify Forms honeypot field filters automated bot submissions. Plausible Analytics is cookieless; no tracking consent banner required. CSP header restricts scripts to self and cdn.plausible.io.
security.privacy_controls: No personal data stored on site. Contact form submissions processed by Netlify and forwarded to CRM; Netlify Forms data retention limited to 30 days then auto-deleted.

data.entities: product_pages, trigger_explanations, payout_calculators, cooperative_testimonials, enrollment_faqs, crop_types
data.storage_design: All content in Git repository as Markdown files with YAML frontmatter. Trigger threshold data (county FIPS, crop type, drought trigger percentile, rainfall trigger mm, historical payout rates) stored as JSON data files in src/data/ consumed by Astro content collections at build time.
data.schema_notes: Product page frontmatter: title, crop_type, peril (drought|rainfall|both), trigger_description, payout_mechanism, regions, seo_description. Trigger table JSON: array of county_fips, state, crop_type, drought_trigger_percentile, rainfall_excess_mm, historical_payout_pct.

devops.deployment_topology: Netlify CDN with global edge nodes. Atomic deploys on every git push to main. Branch deploy previews for pull requests with unique preview URLs.
devops.ci_cd_design: Netlify build: npm install, astro build (static export), deploy to CDN. Build triggered on push to main and pull requests. Deploy preview URL posted to pull request as comment. Slack webhook notification on build success or failure.
devops.observability_stack: Plausible Analytics snippet in Astro layout for pageview and custom goal tracking (calculator use, inquiry form submission). Netlify Analytics for CDN bandwidth and top pages. Lighthouse CI in GitHub Actions on pull requests checking performance score >= 90.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 static site with React islands for interactive threshold calculator; product pages organized by crop type and climate region
frontend.pages_and_flows: Home, Product Pages by Crop/Region, Interactive Trigger Threshold Explainer, Farmer FAQ, Agent Locator, Application CTA
frontend.ui_modules: CropRegionProductCard, TriggerThresholdCalculator (React island), FarmerFAQAccordion, AgentLocatorMap, ApplicationCTA
frontend.performance_notes: Trigger threshold calculator lazy-hydrated on scroll into view; static product pages cached at CDN edge; map tiles loaded from CDN
frontend.accessibility_notes: Calculator inputs must have descriptive labels; map component needs keyboard navigation and text-based fallback for screen readers

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a static Astro 4 informational site for a parametric weather insurance startup targeting agricultural cooperatives. Key decisions: Alpine.js Astro Island for the trigger threshold calculator to minimize JavaScript payload, Netlify Forms with honeypot for inquiry routing, trigger threshold data as static JSON in content collections so county-level payout scenarios render without any API calls, and Plausible Analytics for cookieless visitor tracking that does not require a consent banner.
target_output.title: Parametric Weather Insurance Public Website Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: Static Astro 4 website with Tailwind CSS hosted on Netlify CDN for a parametric weather insurance startup educating agricultural cooperative managers about drought and excess-rainfall coverage triggers, payout mechanisms, and enrollment processes. Features an interactive trigger threshold calculator as an Alpine.js Astro Island, product pages organized by crop type and climate region, and a Netlify Forms inquiry routing to the sales CRM. Zero-JS pages by default for 90+ Lighthouse performance on rural cellular connections.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static Astro 4 site with no server-side runtime or database. Content authors manage product pages and FAQs via Markdown files with YAML frontmatter committed to GitHub; any push to main triggers a Netlify atomic build and CDN deploy in under 90 seconds. County-level drought and rainfall trigger threshold data is stored as static JSON in src/data/ and consumed by Astro content collections at build time, enabling the trigger calculator to operate entirely client-side with no API calls. Alpine.js is loaded only on the calculator page as an Astro client:load Island, keeping all other pages zero-JavaScript. Netlify Forms with honeypot spam prevention handles contact inquiries and forwards submissions to HubSpot CRM via webhook. Plausible Analytics provides cookieless visitor tracking without a GDPR consent banner.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4 with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 3, Alpine.js 3 (client-load Island for calculator), Astro content collections for Markdown pages. Content: Markdown with YAML frontmatter, JSON data files for trigger tables and crop type metadata. Hosting: Netlify CDN with atomic deploys, Netlify Forms for inquiry handling. Analytics: Plausible Analytics (cookieless). CI/CD: Netlify CI on git push, GitHub Actions Lighthouse CI on pull requests. Build: Astro static export, output to dist/ directory.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Product Pages by Crop Type: dedicated pages for corn, soybeans, wheat, cotton, and almonds explaining the peril coverage (drought, excess rainfall, or dual-trigger), county eligibility map, and historical payout frequency. Trigger Threshold Calculator: interactive Alpine.js Island where a cooperative manager selects their county, crop type, and coverage period, then sees estimated annual payout probability and example payout scenarios based on embedded trigger table data. Cooperative Testimonials: static testimonial cards from named cooperative managers with region and crop context. Enrollment FAQ: accordion-style Q&A covering trigger definition, payout timeline, premium payment, and claims process for parametric products. Contact Inquiry Form: Netlify Form with name, email, cooperative name, state, crop types of interest, and honeypot field; submissions forwarded to HubSpot CRM sales pipeline.
target_output.system_components: Astro 4 build engine generating static HTML, CSS, and minimal JavaScript to dist/. Netlify CDN distributing built assets globally via edge nodes. Netlify Forms for contact inquiry submission processing with HubSpot webhook forwarding. Alpine.js loaded as Astro Island on /calculator page only. Astro content collections consuming Markdown product pages and JSON trigger table data files. Netlify build environment running npm install and astro build. GitHub Actions workflow running Lighthouse CI against branch deploy preview URL on pull requests. Plausible Analytics script embedded in Astro BaseLayout component.
target_output.workflows: Content Update and Deploy: A marketing team member opens the GitHub web editor, edits a product page Markdown file (e.g. src/content/products/corn.md), updates frontmatter trigger_description or adds a new testimonial, and commits directly to a feature branch. A pull request triggers Netlify branch deploy preview and GitHub Actions Lighthouse CI; if performance score is 90+ the PR is approved and merged to main. Netlify detects the main push, runs astro build in under 90 seconds, and deploys atomically to the CDN, replacing the previous build with no downtime. Trigger Calculator Interaction: A cooperative manager for an Iowa corn operation visits the /calculator page. Alpine.js initializes with the county trigger table loaded from an embedded JSON data attribute. The manager selects Linn County, Iowa, corn, April-August coverage period. Alpine.js filters the trigger table and renders: drought trigger at SPI-2.0 (below 10th percentile), estimated annual payout probability 18%, example payout $45/acre at maximum trigger breach. The manager submits the contact inquiry form; Netlify Forms processes the submission and fires a webhook to HubSpot creating a new contact in the agricultural cooperatives sales pipeline.
target_output.data_model: Content stored as Markdown with YAML frontmatter in src/content/products/{crop}.md: title, crop_type (corn|soybeans|wheat|cotton|almonds), peril (drought|excess_rainfall|dual), trigger_description, payout_mechanism, coverage_months, eligible_states LIST, seo_description. JSON trigger table at src/data/trigger-tables.json: array of objects with county_fips CHAR(5), county_name, state_abbreviation, crop_type, peril, trigger_threshold_description, drought_spi_threshold FLOAT, rainfall_excess_mm INT, historical_trigger_pct FLOAT, example_payout_per_acre INT. FAQ content at src/content/faqs/{slug}.md: question, answer, category ENUM triggers/payouts/enrollment/premium. Testimonials at src/content/testimonials/{slug}.md: cooperative_name, manager_name, state, crop_type, quote.
target_output.api_design: No HTTP API. Client-side calculator data interface: Alpine.js x-data object initialized with triggerTables (embedded as JSON in a script data attribute from Astro build-time injection), exposing filterByCountyAndCrop(countyFips, cropType, peril) returning matching trigger table row with computed payout probability display values. Netlify Forms submission endpoint: POST /.netlify/forms/inquiry-form (handled by Netlify Forms platform, not application code). Plausible Analytics custom events fired via JavaScript: plausible('Calculator Use', {props: {crop_type, state}}) and plausible('Inquiry Form Submit').
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Netlify CDN for all requests; HTTP redirected to HTTPS. Content Security Policy header configured in netlify.toml: default-src 'self', script-src 'self' 'cdn.plausible.io', style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' (required for Tailwind). Netlify Forms honeypot field (hidden input with bot-trap name) reduces spam submissions before CRM forwarding. No cookies set by Plausible Analytics; no GDPR consent banner required. No personal data stored on site origin; contact form submissions retained by Netlify Forms for maximum 30 days then auto-deleted per Netlify data policy. X-Frame-Options DENY and X-Content-Type-Options nosniff headers set via netlify.toml.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Netlify build configuration in netlify.toml: build command 'npm run build', publish directory 'dist', Node 20 build environment. Every commit to main triggers production atomic deploy. Pull requests trigger branch deploy previews with unique subdomain URLs posted as GitHub PR comments. GitHub Actions Lighthouse CI workflow runs on pull request: fetches branch preview URL, runs Lighthouse with performance, accessibility, and best-practices checks, fails PR if performance score drops below 90 or accessibility below 90. Netlify Slack integration sends build success and failure notifications to the #marketing-deploys channel. Rollback: Netlify deploy history allows one-click instant rollback to any previous atomic deploy.
target_output.observability: Plausible Analytics dashboard tracking: pageviews by crop type product page, calculator page sessions, calculator interactions (custom goal), inquiry form submissions (custom goal), referral sources (farm bureau websites, email campaigns). Netlify Analytics (server-side, no JS required): CDN requests per page, bandwidth, top referrers, 404 error pages. Lighthouse CI performance score tracked in pull requests to detect regressions before deploy. Netlify deploy notifications to Slack for build time, deploy status, and first-byte time after deploy.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated under $50/month: Netlify Starter plan (free for initial traffic), Plausible Analytics $9/month for under 10K monthly pageviews, domain registration ~$15/year. Netlify CDN absorbs traffic spikes from agricultural media coverage or spring planting season outreach emails without any origin server involvement. Upgrade to Netlify Pro at $19/month if build minutes exceed 300 per month or team collaboration features are needed. Calculator data is embedded statically; no API costs regardless of visitor volume.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (2 weeks): Astro project scaffolding with Tailwind CSS, product pages for corn, soybeans, and wheat with Markdown content structure, trigger threshold calculator Alpine.js Island with initial 3-state county data, Netlify Forms inquiry form with HubSpot webhook, Plausible Analytics integration, netlify.toml with CSP headers, Lighthouse CI GitHub Actions workflow. Phase 2 (1 week): Cotton and almond product pages, remaining state trigger table data for all eligible counties, cooperative testimonials section, enrollment FAQ accordion, Netlify branch preview workflow for marketing review. Phase 3 (future): Spanish-language product pages via Astro i18n routing, downloadable PDF policy wording linked from product pages, embedded USGS weather station map for trigger zone visualization.
target_output.development_guidelines: Astro content collections with Zod schema validation for Markdown frontmatter; build fails if a content author omits a required frontmatter field. Tailwind CSS JIT purge enabled; no custom CSS files. Alpine.js Islands used only where client-side interactivity is unavoidable (calculator); all other components are Astro server-rendered. Trigger table JSON must pass a JSON Schema validation script in CI before deploy to prevent malformed county data from breaking the calculator. All images must be in Astro Image component with width, height, and alt attributes to maintain Lighthouse accessibility score. Pull requests require Lighthouse performance score 90+ gate before merge. Markdown content editable via GitHub web editor without local development environment.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Trigger table JSON grows large as coverage expands to all US counties, slowing Alpine.js initialization on the calculator page. Mitigation: split trigger table into per-state JSON files loaded lazily by Alpine.js on state selection. Risk: Netlify Forms spam volume overwhelming HubSpot CRM with bot submissions. Mitigation: honeypot field plus Netlify Forms Akismet spam filtering; monitor HubSpot lead quality weekly. Tradeoff: Alpine.js over React for calculator to minimize JavaScript bundle size; Alpine.js is sufficient for a county-select and result-display interaction pattern without a component tree. Tradeoff: flat-file Markdown content over a headless CMS (Contentful, Sanity) chosen for simplicity and zero additional cost; acceptable for a team of 2-3 content editors using GitHub web editor.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Alpine.js selected over React Island for trigger calculator to keep the JavaScript payload under 15KB; React with dependencies would be 80KB+ for equivalent functionality. Netlify Forms selected over a custom serverless function for inquiry handling to eliminate backend infrastructure entirely. Plausible Analytics selected over Google Analytics for cookieless tracking that does not require a GDPR consent banner, improving page load performance and simplifying legal compliance.

project.goal: Interactive insurance market data publication replacing PDF-only annual reports
project.target_users: Industry analysts, actuaries, association members
project.timeline: Q2 annual publication deadline

frontend.visualization: D3.js v7 for SVG charts and geographic maps
frontend.state: Svelte stores for filter state, URL-encoded for shareability

data.format: Pre-built JSON from NAIC CSV processing
data.entities: market_share_data, combined_ratio_series, catastrophe_loss_events, state_statistics, line_of_business_segments, carrier_groups, reporting_years
data.build_tool: Node.js data pipeline in GitHub Actions

constraints.deadline: Q2 aligned with NAIC data release

open_questions.csv_export: Determine scope of data available for CSV download

reasoner_reviews.product: Replacing PDF reports with interactive web visualizations is high-value for association members. The state-level market share data and catastrophe loss heatmaps are the primary draws. Shareable deep links are critical for analysts embedding specific views in research reports.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: SvelteKit static export with D3.js is the right fit: no server needed, pre-built JSON keeps the CDN cache hit rate near 100%, and D3.js gives full control over chart aesthetics. The main complexity is the data build pipeline that transforms NAIC CSVs into optimized JSON partitioned by state and line of business to keep individual page payloads under 500KB.
reasoner_reviews.security: No auth surface and no PII means minimal security concerns. Cloudflare provides DDoS mitigation and HTTPS termination. CSP headers should block inline scripts to prevent any XSS risk from third-party embeds. No cookies beyond Cloudflare Analytics.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Cloudflare Pages free tier handles the expected traffic comfortably. The main operational risk is the annual data pipeline failing close to the publication deadline due to NAIC CSV format changes. A pre-release data validation step in CI is essential.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 1 plan did not adequately address the data build pipeline architecture or the risk of NAIC source format changes breaking the build. The observability section was thin with no mention of build failure alerting. These gaps are addressed in this revision.

backend.service_design: Build-time Node.js data pipeline: parse NAIC CSVs, validate schema, normalize carrier groups, compute aggregates, output partitioned JSON files organized by state_code/lob/year
backend.api_patterns: No runtime API; static JSON files at /data/market-share/{state}/{lob}.json, /data/cat-losses/{year}.json, /data/combined-ratios/{lob}.json served directly from Cloudflare CDN
backend.failure_handling: GitHub Actions pipeline fails hard on NAIC schema validation errors, posting Slack alert to data engineering channel; a data fixture set enables local chart development without real NAIC data

security.auth_design: No authentication required; all content is public
security.secrets_management: No secrets in production; Cloudflare API token for Pages deploy stored in GitHub Actions secrets
security.abuse_prevention: Cloudflare rate limiting at edge for excessive data file downloads; CSP header blocking eval and inline scripts
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Cloudflare Analytics aggregate page view counts; no individual user tracking, no cookies, no fingerprinting

previous_plan.title: Insurance Market Data Interactive Publication Site
previous_plan.executive_summary: A static SvelteKit site with D3.js visualizations published to Cloudflare Pages, serving pre-built JSON market data to industry analysts without any server-side runtime.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: SvelteKit static export compiled to HTML/JS/CSS with pre-built JSON data files. Hosted on Cloudflare Pages CDN edge. D3.js renders SVG charts from JSON payloads. No backend, no database.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a round 2 revision addressing two concrete gaps from the round 1 audit: the data build pipeline was unspecified, and build failure alerting was missing. The architecture is fundamentally sound (SvelteKit static export, D3.js, Cloudflare Pages) so this revision deepens the data pipeline specification and adds operational alerting. The site complexity is moderate despite being static because the D3.js multi-dimensional filtering and URL-encoded state management require careful frontend architecture. No compliance context needed as data sensitivity is none.

fix_report.action_taken: Added detailed data build pipeline specification: Node.js script ingests NAIC CSV exports, validates column schema with a manifest file, normalizes carrier groups by NAIC group code, computes state/lob/year aggregates, and outputs partitioned JSON files with a manifest index; CI fails hard on schema validation errors
fix_report.expected_outcome: Auditor can trace the full data flow from NAIC CSV source to browser chart without ambiguity

fix_report.action_taken: Added GitHub Actions Slack webhook notification on pipeline failure and a separate build health badge in the repository README; observability section now covers both runtime chart analytics and build pipeline health
fix_report.expected_outcome: Build failures during annual publication sprint are detected within minutes rather than discovered manually

target_output.title: Insurance Market Data Interactive Publication - Revised Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A SvelteKit static site with D3.js interactive charts published to Cloudflare Pages, transforming annual NAIC market data releases into explorable visualizations covering personal and commercial lines market share, combined ratio trends, and catastrophe loss events across all 50 US states. A Node.js build pipeline transforms NAIC CSV exports into partitioned JSON data files during CI; the resulting static build serves all chart data from Cloudflare edge without any server-side runtime.
target_output.architecture_overview: Pure static architecture: SvelteKit compiles to pre-rendered HTML pages with client-side D3.js chart hydration. During each GitHub Actions build, a Node.js data pipeline ingests NAIC CSV exports, validates the column schema against a versioned manifest, normalizes carrier groups by NAIC group code, and outputs JSON files partitioned by state code, line of business, and reporting year into the /static/data directory. The compiled output is deployed to Cloudflare Pages, which serves all assets including data JSON files from 270+ edge PoPs globally. No origin server is involved at request time. Svelte stores manage multi-dimensional filter state and serialize it into URL query parameters, enabling shareable deep links to specific state/lob/year views.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: SvelteKit 2 with TypeScript, static adapter, D3.js v7 for SVG charts and geographic county-level maps, Tailwind CSS for layout. Data Pipeline: Node.js 20 scripts run in CI to process NAIC CSVs into JSON partitions. Testing: Vitest for pipeline unit tests and chart data transformation logic, Playwright for visual regression on key chart pages. Build/Deploy: GitHub Actions, Cloudflare Pages via wrangler CLI. Analytics: Cloudflare Web Analytics (privacy-first, no cookies).
target_output.functional_feature_map: Market Share Explorer: select state, line of business, and year range to render a ranked bar chart of carrier groups by direct written premium with market share percentages. Combined Ratio Trends: line chart comparing up to 5 lines of business over 20 years with hover-detail tooltips showing loss ratio and expense ratio breakdowns. Catastrophe Loss Map: US choropleth map shading states by insured catastrophe loss for the selected reporting year, with event-level detail panel. State Profile Pages: pre-rendered per-state pages aggregating all three data views for a given state, optimized for search indexing. CSV Data Export: download filtered dataset as a CSV file generated client-side from the loaded JSON partition. Deep Link Sharing: URL-encoded filter state allows direct linking to any specific chart configuration.
target_output.system_components: GitHub Actions CI pipeline: data build job (Node.js NAIC processor) and site build job (SvelteKit static adapter). Cloudflare Pages CDN serving /static/data JSON partitions and compiled SvelteKit output. D3.js chart modules as Svelte components wrapping SVG rendering logic. Svelte store layer managing filter state with URL serialization. Pre-rendered state profile pages generated by SvelteKit's file-based routing. Cloudflare Web Analytics script injected at build time.
target_output.workflows: Annual Data Publication: NAIC releases annual market data CSVs in April; a data engineer commits the new CSV files to the data/ directory and opens a PR; the GitHub Actions pipeline runs the Node.js processor which validates that all expected columns and NAIC group codes are present against the versioned schema manifest, halting on any schema mismatch and posting a Slack alert; after validation the processor computes state/lob aggregates and writes new JSON partitions; the PR preview deployment lets stakeholders review charts before merge; merge to main triggers the production deploy and Cloudflare cache purge. Interactive Filtering: a user on the market share page selects California from the state dropdown and Commercial Auto from the line-of-business selector; the Svelte store updates, serializing the selection to the URL query string; D3.js re-renders the carrier ranking bar chart by fetching /data/market-share/CA/commercial-auto.json from the Cloudflare edge cache; the user copies the URL and shares it with a colleague who lands on the exact same filtered view.
target_output.data_model: market_share_data (carrier_group_id STRING, carrier_group_name STRING, naic_group_code STRING, state_code CHAR(2), lob_segment STRING, direct_written_premium_usd BIGINT, market_share_pct DECIMAL, reporting_year INT) partitioned into JSON files at /data/market-share/{state_code}/{lob_slug}.json. combined_ratio_series (lob_segment STRING, state_code CHAR(2), reporting_year INT, combined_ratio DECIMAL, loss_ratio DECIMAL, expense_ratio DECIMAL, dividend_ratio DECIMAL) at /data/combined-ratios/{lob_slug}/{state_code}.json. catastrophe_loss_events (event_id STRING, event_name STRING, peril_type ENUM wind/flood/wildfire/earthquake/other, states_affected ARRAY, industry_insured_loss_usd BIGINT, reporting_year INT, quarter INT) at /data/cat-losses/{year}.json. state_statistics (state_code CHAR(2), reporting_year INT, total_market_premium_usd BIGINT, admitted_carrier_count INT, nonadmitted_carrier_count INT) at /data/states/{state_code}/summary.json. carrier_groups (group_id STRING, group_name STRING, naic_group_code STRING, domicile_state CHAR(2)) at /data/reference/carrier-groups.json.
target_output.api_design: No runtime API endpoints. Static file access patterns (all served from Cloudflare edge): GET /data/market-share/{state}/{lob}.json returns carrier ranking array for state+lob combination; GET /data/combined-ratios/{lob}/{state}.json returns annual ratio time series; GET /data/cat-losses/{year}.json returns catastrophe event array for the year; GET /data/states/{state}/summary.json returns state-level aggregate totals; GET /data/reference/carrier-groups.json returns the full carrier group reference table loaded once on app init. A data manifest at /data/manifest.json lists all available state/lob/year combinations and the build timestamp, allowing the client to detect stale cached data.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Cloudflare with TLS 1.3; HTTP to HTTPS redirect at edge. Content Security Policy header: default-src 'self', script-src 'self', style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' (Tailwind), img-src 'self' data:, connect-src 'self'. No cookies set; Cloudflare Web Analytics uses no cookies and collects no IP addresses. X-Frame-Options: DENY to prevent clickjacking. No user data collected, no forms with personal data. Cloudflare DDoS protection active at edge for traffic spike events following major catastrophes.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions workflow with two sequential jobs: (1) data-build: runs Node.js NAIC processor, validates CSV schema against src/data/schema-manifest.json, generates JSON partitions into static/data/, fails and posts Slack webhook alert if validation errors detected; (2) site-build: runs svelte-check, vitest, SvelteKit build with static adapter, wrangler pages deploy to Cloudflare Pages. PR branches trigger preview deployments automatically. Merge to main triggers production deploy. Cloudflare Pages retains the last 25 deployments; instant rollback via wrangler pages deployment rollback or the Cloudflare dashboard. No infrastructure to manage; zero VMs, zero containers, zero databases.
target_output.observability: Cloudflare Web Analytics: page views by URL, top referring domains, geographic distribution of visitors, browser breakdown. No individual user tracking, no session IDs. Chart interaction events tracked via custom analytics beacon (chart_filter_applied, csv_export_downloaded, deep_link_page_loaded) logged to Cloudflare Analytics Engine. GitHub Actions build health: Slack webhook notification on any pipeline failure; build duration tracked via Actions summary; schema validation error log artifact uploaded on failure for data engineering triage. Annual traffic spike monitoring: Cloudflare dashboard traffic volume during catastrophe event weeks compared to baseline.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloudflare Pages free tier covers unlimited bandwidth and 500 builds/month; expected cost is $0-20/month for the Pages Pro plan if build minutes exceed free limits. No servers to scale; Cloudflare edge absorbs any traffic spike automatically. Annual NAIC data file sizes total approximately 15MB of raw JSON after partitioning; Cloudflare CDN serves all data from edge cache after the first request per PoP. CSV export is generated client-side from already-loaded JSON, eliminating any server cost for data downloads.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks, Q1-Q2): Node.js NAIC CSV processor with schema validation, market share explorer and combined ratio trend charts for top 10 lines of business, SvelteKit static site with URL-encoded filter state, Cloudflare Pages deploy pipeline with Slack alerting, all 50 states covered. Phase 2 (3 weeks post-MVP): catastrophe loss choropleth map with peril filter, pre-rendered per-state profile pages for SEO, CSV data export. Phase 3 (4 weeks): carrier group drilldown pages, embeddable chart iFrame widgets for member websites, data manifest versioning for cache-busting on mid-year updates.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode throughout SvelteKit and data pipeline scripts. Vitest unit tests required for all data pipeline transformation functions (NAIC parser, aggregation logic, partition file naming). Playwright visual regression tests for the three primary chart pages using fixture JSON data. Schema manifest file at src/data/schema-manifest.json is the contract between NAIC data and the pipeline; any column rename requires a manifest PR reviewed by the data engineering lead. D3.js chart components must render correctly at 320px mobile width and 1440px desktop width. Deep link URL format is versioned (v1) to allow future parameter changes without breaking existing shared links.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: NAIC changes CSV column names or file structure between annual releases, breaking the pipeline days before publication deadline. Mitigation: schema-manifest validation fails loudly in CI with a diff of expected vs actual columns; data engineering team has 2-week buffer built into the Q2 publication schedule. Risk: Cloudflare Pages deployment limits (500 builds/month on free tier) exceeded during active development sprints. Mitigation: upgrade to Pro plan at $25/month if exceeded; cost well within budget. Tradeoff: pre-partitioned JSON files mean updating a single carrier's data requires a full rebuild and redeploy rather than an API PATCH. Accepted because the annual publication model means data updates are infrequent and a full redeploy takes under 3 minutes. Risk: D3.js SVG charts are not accessible to screen readers out of the box. Mitigation: add aria-label attributes and an accessible data table toggle for each chart view in Phase 1.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed SvelteKit static adapter over server adapter because no login, no session, and no server-side personalization is required. Selected Cloudflare Web Analytics over Google Analytics for privacy-first tracking with no cookie banner requirement. CSV export will be client-side only using the already-loaded JSON partition, not a server-generated file. NAIC data fixture set for local development will be a 10-state, 5-lob, 5-year subset committed to the repository to enable chart development without the full 15MB dataset.

metadata.notes: Static data viz site for insurance market statistics; revision addresses data pipeline spec gap and adds build alerting.

project_goal.value: Consolidate risk register maintenance, incident tracking, insurance program documentation, and renewal benchmarking across 40+ operating entities of a large industrial conglomerate into a single captive insurance management platform
project_goal.rationale: Fragmented Excel-based risk registers across subsidiaries create blind spots for group risk management

target_users.value: Group risk managers at holding company, subsidiary risk coordinators, captive insurance finance team, and executive leadership
target_users.rationale: Four distinct user personas with different data access scopes identified in discovery workshops

project_class.rationale: Requires rich UI for data entry and dashboarding alongside a complex backend with batch aggregation jobs

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, integrations, batch_jobs, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: Full platform with dashboards, broker data integrations, renewal batch jobs, and entity admin

complexity_level.rationale: 40+ entity hierarchy with row-level security, multi-currency financials, and cross-entity aggregation at scale

risk_level.rationale: Handles confidential captive financial data, SOX-relevant internal controls documentation, and GDPR-scoped EU entity data

data_sensitivity.rationale: Captive financials, risk register contents, and incident details are confidential corporate information

external_exposure.rationale: Internal enterprise application; all users authenticate via Azure AD SSO

access_model.value: Hierarchical RBAC with entity-scoped row-level security; holding company roles view all entities, subsidiary roles see only their own entity
access_model.rationale: Multi-entity architecture requires strict data segregation between subsidiaries

feature_scope.value: Risk register with heat map visualization, incident logging with investigation workflow, insurance program schedule with coverage line tracking, renewal benchmarking against broker market data feeds, captive financial summaries, cross-entity consolidated dashboards, admin panel for entity and user management
feature_scope.rationale: Core ERM platform features defined in business requirements document

mvp_scope.value: Risk register with entity-scoped RBAC, incident logging, insurance program schedule, holding company consolidated view
mvp_scope.rationale: Covers the highest-priority manual process replacements for Phase 1 pilot deployment

security_baseline.value: Azure AD SSO with MFA, TLS 1.3 in transit, AKS pod-to-pod mTLS via Istio, row-level security in PostgreSQL, audit logging for all record mutations
security_baseline.rationale: Enterprise security standard for confidential data platform

frontend_stack.value: Angular 17 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Enterprise Angular standardized across the conglomerate internal tooling teams

backend_stack.value: Java 21 with Spring Boot
backend_stack.rationale: Enterprise Java standard; Spring Boot used across existing internal systems

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security policies
data_platform.rationale: Relational structure suits hierarchical entity data; RLS enforces entity-scoped access at database layer

hosting_target.value: Azure Kubernetes Service on Azure commercial cloud
hosting_target.rationale: Conglomerate standard Kubernetes platform; existing AKS cluster infrastructure

privacy_retention_policy.value: Risk register and incident records retained 10 years; EU entity data processed under GDPR Article 6(1)(b); right-to-erasure workflow for personal data in incident records
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Long corporate record retention combined with GDPR obligations for EU subsidiaries

future_scope.value: Integration with third-party catastrophe modeling platforms, real-time broker market pricing API feeds, automated renewal submission generation
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 and 3 roadmap from business case

constraints.value: Must integrate with existing Azure AD tenant; deployment to existing AKS cluster within approved Azure regions; initial rollout to 5 pilot entities in 6 months
constraints.rationale: Enterprise infrastructure constraints and pilot timeline

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM, structured JSON logging with correlation IDs, Azure Monitor for AKS cluster metrics, PagerDuty alerts for API error rate and batch job failures
observability_baseline.rationale: Enterprise monitoring stack aligned with existing Datadog contract

execution_preference.value: Agile sprints with 2-week iterations, quarterly steering committee reviews
execution_preference.rationale: Enterprise project governance model

compliance_context.value: SOC 2 Type II, Sarbanes-Oxley internal controls documentation requirements, GDPR for EU entity personal data in incident records, captive regulatory requirements across domicile jurisdictions
compliance_context.rationale: Confidential financial data with SOX obligations and EU entity GDPR exposure

project.goal: Unified ERM platform replacing per-entity Excel risk registers and manual insurance renewal processes
project.target_users: Group risk managers, subsidiary risk coordinators, captive finance team, executive leadership
project.timeline: 6-month pilot rollout to 5 entities, full rollout by end of year

frontend.state: NgRx for complex state across entity hierarchy
frontend.styling: Angular Material with custom corporate design tokens
frontend.charts: Highcharts Enterprise for risk heat maps and financial trend charts

backend.framework: Spring Boot 3.2 with Spring Data JPA
backend.async: Spring Batch for renewal aggregation and financial roll-up jobs

security.auth: Azure AD SSO with SAML 2.0
security.mfa: Enforced via Azure AD Conditional Access
security.rls: PostgreSQL row-level security policies keyed on entity_id
security.transport: TLS 1.3, AKS pod mTLS via Istio

data.cache: Redis 7 for session state and dashboard query cache

constraints.azure_region: West Europe and East US 2 only

open_questions.broker_feed_format: Confirm format of broker market benchmarking data feed for renewal module

reasoner_reviews.product: The consolidation of 40+ entity risk registers is the primary business driver. The hierarchical view from holding company down to subsidiary is the key UX challenge. MVP scope correctly prioritizes risk register and incident logging, which are the most manual pain points today.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The entity hierarchy with PostgreSQL row-level security is the most architecturally complex aspect. RLS policies keyed on entity_id with a current_entity_ids session variable set at JDBC connection checkout is the right approach; application-layer filtering alone is insufficient for defense-in-depth. The renewal batch job must handle multi-currency premium normalization across 40+ entities with different functional currencies using ECB exchange rates.
reasoner_reviews.security: Azure AD SSO with Conditional Access MFA is appropriate for an enterprise platform handling confidential captive financials. RLS must be enforced at the database layer to prevent privilege escalation bugs. Audit logging must capture entity_id, user_id, action, and before/after values for all risk register and incident mutations to support SOX control evidence requirements.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 6-month pilot timeline is achievable for the MVP scope if Angular scaffolding and RLS policy framework are established in sprint 1. Full 40+ entity rollout will require an entity onboarding automation script rather than manual admin configuration for each subsidiary.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The architecture draft incorrectly references React in the technology_stack and system_components sections despite the contract specifying Angular 17. The data_model also omits the captive_financials and risk_owners entities that appear in the key_entities manifest. These are intentional flaws for training data purposes.

backend.service_design: Domain modules: RiskRegisterModule (risk entries, heat map scoring), IncidentModule (FNOL, investigation tasks, closure workflow), InsuranceProgramModule (coverage schedules, policy document attachments), RenewalModule (benchmarking, Spring Batch aggregation), EntityModule (hierarchy management, user-entity assignments), AdminModule (user provisioning, audit log viewer)
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.1; all data endpoints scoped by entity_id path prefix /api/v1/entities/{entityId}/; Spring Security enforces entity access via JWT entity_ids claim before RLS
backend.business_modules: RenewalBenchmarkingService: compares submitted renewal terms against broker feed data by line of business and industry sector with multi-currency normalization using ECB daily rates
backend.background_jobs: Spring Batch: nightly renewal aggregation job pulling exposure data from all active entities, computing consolidated program summaries; monthly captive financial roll-up job; GDPR erasure job processing pseudonymization requests for EU entity personal data

frontend.app_structure: Angular 17 with lazy-loaded feature modules per domain: risk-register, incidents, insurance-programs, renewal, captive-financials, admin
frontend.pages_and_flows: Entity Selector (holding company users see all entities, subsidiary coordinators see own entity only), Risk Heat Map (5x5 likelihood-severity matrix), Incident Filing Form with investigation task assignment, Insurance Program Schedule with coverage line grid, Renewal Benchmarking Dashboard, Group Consolidated Dashboard, Admin Panel for entity and user management
frontend.state_management: NgRx store with entity context as root state slice; all data selectors automatically scope to active entity context; router serialization of entity selection for bookmarkable URLs

security.auth_design: SAML 2.0 federation with Azure AD; Spring Security SAML processor issues application JWT with entity_ids claim resolved from user-entity assignment table at login; PostgreSQL SET LOCAL app.entity_ids called at JDBC connection checkout via DataSourceProxyListener
security.authorization_model: Roles: GROUP_RISK_MANAGER (all entities read/write), ENTITY_COORDINATOR (own entity read/write), CAPTIVE_FINANCE (financial modules read-only), EXECUTIVE (all entities read-only); role checked in Spring Security before RLS evaluated
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_events table: user_id, entity_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, old_values JSONB, new_values JSONB, ip_address, timestamp; append-only policy; forwarded to Datadog Log Management; 7-year retention for SOX evidence
security.secrets_management: Azure Key Vault for DB credentials, JWT signing keys, broker API keys; mounted as AKS pod secrets via CSI driver with Key Vault Provider

data.entities: operating_entities (id, entity_name, parent_entity_id, country_code, functional_currency, entity_type ENUM subsidiary/holding/captive), risk_registers (id, entity_id, risk_title, category, likelihood_score 1-5, severity_score 1-5, inherent_risk_rating, residual_risk_rating, risk_owner_id, review_date, status), incidents (id, entity_id, incident_date, incident_type, description, financial_impact_usd, investigation_status, root_cause, closure_date), insurance_programs (id, entity_id, program_year, insurer_name, line_of_business, limit_usd, deductible_usd, premium_usd, policy_number, expiry_date), renewal_benchmarks (id, entity_id, program_id, market_premium_usd, proposed_premium_usd, deviation_pct), captive_financials (id, entity_id, fiscal_year, gross_written_premium, net_earned_premium, incurred_losses, combined_ratio), risk_owners (id, entity_id, user_id, display_name, department, active)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with RLS policies; Redis 7 for heat map aggregation cache and session tokens; Azure Blob Storage for insurance policy document attachments
data.retention_and_deletion: 10-year retention on risk register and incident records; GDPR erasure workflow pseudonymizes personal data in EU entity incident records without removing audit trail rows

devops.deployment_topology: AKS cluster with dedicated namespace per environment; 3-replica API Deployment, 2-replica NGINX frontend Deployment, Spring Batch workers as Kubernetes CronJobs and on-demand Jobs
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps: Maven build with JUnit/Mockito 80% line coverage gate, SpotBugs static analysis, Flyway migration dry-run on staging DB, Docker build push to ACR, Helm chart deploy to AKS target namespace with readiness probe rollback
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM Spring Boot auto-instrumentation, Logback structured JSON logs, custom metrics: risk_register.entry_count by entity, incident.open_duration histogram, renewal_batch.duration_seconds, rls_violation_count
devops.rollback_strategy: Helm rollback to previous chart version triggered automatically on failed readiness probe within 5 minutes; Flyway migration rollback scripts required in each migration PR

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a round 1 first_pass for a high-scale enterprise ERM platform with a 40+ entity hierarchy and PostgreSQL row-level security. The plan is intentionally flawed for training purposes: the technology_stack and system_components sections reference React 18 instead of the contracted Angular 17, and the data_model omits captive_financials and risk_owners entities that appear in the manifest key_entities list. These simulate a realistic draft error where the author copied from a prior React project without updating the frontend references.
target_output.title: Enterprise Captive Insurance ERM Platform Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A high-scale enterprise fullstack application consolidating risk register management, incident tracking, insurance program scheduling, and renewal benchmarking across 40+ industrial conglomerate operating entities. Built with Angular 17 and Spring Boot on Azure Kubernetes Service, with PostgreSQL 16 row-level security policies enforcing entity-scoped data segregation. Handles multi-currency captive financial data with SOX audit trail requirements and GDPR obligations for European subsidiaries.
target_output.architecture_overview: Multi-tier enterprise architecture with an Angular 17 SPA served from AKS-hosted NGINX communicating to a Spring Boot REST API via Azure Application Gateway with WAF. The API connects to PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security policies restricting each query to the authenticated user's permitted entity_ids, set at JDBC connection checkout via a Spring Data JPA interceptor. Redis 7 caches risk heat map aggregation results and session tokens. Spring Batch workers run as Kubernetes CronJobs for nightly renewal aggregation and monthly captive financial roll-ups. Azure AD provides SSO with MFA enforced through Conditional Access. All RLS evaluations and data mutations are audited in an append-only audit_events table forwarded to Datadog Log Management for SOX evidence retention.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18 with TypeScript, Redux Toolkit, Highcharts Enterprise for heat maps and trend charts. Backend: Java 21 with Spring Boot 3.2, Spring Data JPA, Spring Batch, Spring Security SAML. Database: PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security, Flyway migrations. Cache: Redis 7. Infrastructure: Azure Kubernetes Service, Azure Application Gateway WAF, Azure Container Registry, Azure Key Vault CSI driver. CI/CD: Azure DevOps pipelines. Monitoring: Datadog APM, Azure Monitor, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Risk Register module for entity-scoped risk entry with 5x5 likelihood/severity matrix, computed inherent and residual risk ratings, and heat map visualization. Incident Management module with FNOL filing, investigation task assignment, root cause analysis, and closure workflow. Insurance Program Schedule tracking coverage lines, limits, deductibles, premiums, and policy document attachments per entity and program year. Renewal Benchmarking module comparing proposed renewal terms against broker market data by line of business and industry class, with multi-currency normalization. Group Consolidated Dashboard for holding company users showing aggregated risk counts by rating tier, open incidents, and upcoming renewal deadlines across all entities. Admin Panel for entity hierarchy management, user provisioning, and role-entity assignment.
target_output.system_components: Azure Application Gateway WAF routing HTTPS traffic to AKS ingress controller. React 18 frontend pods served by NGINX in the frontend Kubernetes namespace. Spring Boot API pods in the backend namespace with Istio sidecar for pod-to-pod mTLS. Spring Batch CronJob pods for nightly renewal aggregation and monthly financial roll-up workloads. PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server with RLS enabled. Redis 7 on Azure Cache for Redis. Azure Blob Storage for policy document attachments organized by entity_id and program_year. Azure Key Vault with CSI driver mounting DB credentials and JWT keys as AKS pod volumes. Azure AD for SAML 2.0 identity federation.
target_output.workflows: Risk Register Entry: A subsidiary risk coordinator logs in via Azure AD SSO, navigates to their entity risk register, creates a new risk entry with likelihood score 4 and severity score 5 generating a critical inherent rating, assigns a risk owner from the entity user list, and sets a quarterly review date; the PostgreSQL RLS policy ensures no cross-entity data leakage in the query. The holding company group risk manager opens the Group Consolidated Dashboard, applies a filter for critical residual rating, and sees a ranked entity breakdown allowing drill-down into each subsidiary's high-severity risks. Renewal Aggregation: At T-120 days before policy expiry, the Spring Batch renewal aggregation CronJob queries insurance_programs expiring within 120 days across all entities, normalizes premiums to USD using the ECB daily exchange rate API, fetches current broker benchmark data for each line of business and industry sector, computes deviation_pct for each program, and writes renewal_benchmark records; the following morning each entity's renewal dashboard displays side-by-side proposed versus market terms with deviation flagging.
target_output.data_model: operating_entities (id UUID PK, entity_name VARCHAR, parent_entity_id UUID FK nullable, country_code CHAR(2), functional_currency CHAR(3), entity_type ENUM subsidiary/holding/captive, is_active BOOLEAN). risk_registers (id UUID PK, entity_id UUID FK, risk_title VARCHAR, category ENUM operational/financial/strategic/compliance/reputational, likelihood_score SMALLINT 1-5, severity_score SMALLINT 1-5, inherent_risk_rating ENUM low/medium/high/critical, residual_risk_rating ENUM low/medium/high/critical, risk_owner_id UUID FK, review_date DATE, status ENUM active/closed/accepted). incidents (id UUID PK, entity_id UUID FK, incident_date DATE, incident_type VARCHAR, description TEXT, financial_impact_usd DECIMAL, investigation_status ENUM open/in_progress/closed, root_cause TEXT, closure_date DATE). insurance_programs (id UUID PK, entity_id UUID FK, program_year INT, insurer_name VARCHAR, line_of_business VARCHAR, limit_usd DECIMAL, deductible_usd DECIMAL, premium_usd DECIMAL, policy_number VARCHAR, expiry_date DATE). renewal_benchmarks (id UUID PK, entity_id UUID FK, program_id UUID FK, market_premium_usd DECIMAL, proposed_premium_usd DECIMAL, deviation_pct DECIMAL, benchmark_date DATE). All tables have PostgreSQL RLS policies enforcing entity_id in ANY(current_setting('app.entity_ids')::uuid[]).
target_output.api_design: RESTful API at /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.1 spec. Entity-scoped: GET /api/v1/entities/{entityId}/risks?rating=critical (paginated risk list), POST /api/v1/entities/{entityId}/risks (create risk entry), PATCH /api/v1/entities/{entityId}/risks/{riskId} (update risk), GET /api/v1/entities/{entityId}/incidents?status=open (incident list), POST /api/v1/entities/{entityId}/incidents (file FNOL), GET /api/v1/entities/{entityId}/programs (coverage schedule), GET /api/v1/entities/{entityId}/renewals/benchmarks (renewal comparison). Group-scoped: GET /api/v1/group/heatmap (consolidated risk matrix, GROUP_RISK_MANAGER role required), GET /api/v1/group/renewals/calendar (upcoming renewals across all entities). Admin: POST /api/v1/admin/entities, POST /api/v1/admin/users/{userId}/entity-roles. All endpoints return HTTP 403 if JWT entity_ids claim does not include the requested entityId.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD SAML 2.0 SSO with MFA enforced via Conditional Access policy. Application JWT includes entity_ids claim populated from user_entity_assignments table at login. PostgreSQL RLS: SET LOCAL app.entity_ids called at JDBC connection checkout via DataSourceProxyListener; policy CREATE POLICY entity_isolation ON risk_registers USING (entity_id = ANY(current_setting('app.entity_ids')::uuid[])). Istio mutual TLS for all AKS pod-to-pod traffic. Azure Application Gateway WAF in prevention mode (OWASP 3.2 ruleset). Audit log: audit_events table with old_values/new_values JSONB, append-only, 7-year retention forwarded to Datadog for SOX control evidence. GDPR: pseudonymization workflow for personal data in EU entity incident records on erasure request without deleting audit rows. Azure Key Vault secrets rotated on 90-day schedule. SOC 2 Type II controls mapped to access, availability, and confidentiality criteria.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure DevOps multi-stage pipeline: (1) build stage: Maven compile, JUnit/Mockito with 80% line coverage gate, SpotBugs security scan, Docker build and push to ACR; (2) staging deploy: Flyway migration dry-run against staging DB, Helm upgrade to staging AKS namespace, Playwright E2E smoke tests against staging; (3) production deploy: gated manual approval, Helm upgrade to production AKS namespace with readiness probe health check. Environments: dev (single replica, shared AKS namespace, dev Azure AD app registration), staging (2-replica, mirrors production, used for pilot entity UAT and penetration testing), production (3-replica API, 2-replica NGINX frontend, West Europe AKS, multi-AZ PostgreSQL Flexible Server). Spring Batch Jobs deployed as Kubernetes CronJobs at 02:00 UTC. Rollback: automated Helm rollback on failed readiness probe within 5 minutes; Flyway migration PRs require rollback scripts.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM with Spring Boot Micrometer auto-instrumentation tracing all API requests including PostgreSQL query spans. Logback structured JSON logs with X-Correlation-ID header propagated to Datadog Log Management. Custom Datadog metrics: risk_register.entry_count tagged by entity_id and rating tier, incident.open_duration_hours histogram, renewal_batch.job_duration_seconds, rls_policy.violation_count (zero expected), audit_event.write_rate. PagerDuty P1 escalation on: API error rate above 2%, renewal batch job failure, RLS violation detected, PostgreSQL replication lag above 30 seconds. Azure Monitor for AKS node CPU/memory and pod restart counts. Datadog dashboards: per-entity risk summary heat map, incident trend by entity, renewal pipeline status, Spring Batch job execution history.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: AKS Standard tier with 3 Standard_D4s_v3 nodes estimated at $1,800/month. Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server GP_Standard_D4s_v3 with 100GB storage estimated at $400/month. Azure Cache for Redis C2 instance estimated at $150/month. Azure Application Gateway WAF_v2 estimated at $250/month. Datadog APM Pro 5 hosts estimated at $300/month. Total estimated $3,000-3,500/month at 5-entity pilot scaling to $5,500/month at full 40-entity rollout. HPA scales API pods on 70% CPU target. Redis caches risk heat map aggregation results with 5-minute TTL to prevent N+1 query fan-out across 40+ entities at the group dashboard.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Pilot (6 months, 5 entities): Risk register module with PostgreSQL RLS framework and Angular 17 entity context, incident logging with investigation task workflow, insurance program schedule with document upload to Azure Blob, holding company consolidated dashboard, Azure AD SSO via SAML, Azure DevOps CI/CD pipeline, Datadog APM baseline. Phase 2 (3 months): Renewal benchmarking with Spring Batch aggregation job, multi-currency normalization via ECB API, broker data feed integration, captive financial roll-up batch job, GDPR erasure workflow for EU entities. Phase 3 (3 months): Full 40+ entity rollout with entity onboarding automation, advanced group analytics with drill-through, SOX audit evidence export reports, admin panel self-service entity hierarchy management.
target_output.development_guidelines: Java 21 records and sealed classes for domain model value types. Spring Boot 3.2 virtual threads enabled for improved JDBC connection throughput. 80% line coverage enforced in Azure DevOps via JaCoCo; PostgreSQL RLS integration tests validate entity isolation for every new API endpoint using dual-entity test fixtures. Flyway migration PRs require both forward migration and rollback script. Angular 17 standalone components with NgRx signal store. Highcharts Enterprise license for risk heat map and financial trend charts. All RLS policy changes require security engineer review before merge. ADR documents required for architectural decisions affecting the entity hierarchy model or RLS design.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: PostgreSQL RLS policy misconfiguration could expose one subsidiary entity's confidential risk data to a different entity's coordinator. Mitigation: dedicated integration test suite runs cross-entity data isolation checks for every API endpoint on every PR; RLS policy changes require security engineer approval. Risk: Spring Batch renewal aggregation job runtime grows with entity count; 40+ entities with multi-currency normalization may approach a 2-hour maintenance window. Mitigation: partition the batch job by entity using Spring Batch partitioned steps with 8 parallel partition threads. Risk: Azure AD SAML token size grows large as entity_ids claim expands to 40+ UUID values. Mitigation: store only the user role in the SAML assertion and resolve entity_ids from the assignment table at JWT issuance. Tradeoff: PostgreSQL RLS over application-layer filtering increases correctness confidence but requires disciplined connection management via the JPA interceptor; accepted for defense-in-depth compliance requirements.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Angular 17 as frontend per enterprise standard. PostgreSQL RLS chosen over application-layer filtering for defense-in-depth. Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server over self-managed PostgreSQL for managed HA and patching. Spring Batch over Kafka for renewal aggregation because workloads are nightly batch, not continuous streaming. Highcharts Enterprise license confirmed for heat map visualization. GDPR erasure approach confirmed as pseudonymization rather than hard delete to preserve SOX audit trail integrity.
