profile.domain: Embedded Professional Liability Insurance API Distribution

project_goal.value: Provide a REST API enabling SaaS platform partners to quote, bind, and manage short-term professional liability coverage for their end-user freelancers and consultants at the point of project engagement, with sub-500ms quote latency
project_goal.rationale: Embedded insurance distribution via API is the core go-to-market model

target_users.value: SaaS platform engineering teams integrating the API on behalf of their freelancer and consultant end users
target_users.rationale: B2B2C model; direct consumers are partner developers, indirect are freelancers

project_class.rationale: Headless API with no frontend; all UX rendered within partner platforms

capabilities.value: backend, data, auth, integrations, payments, public_api, analytics
capabilities.rationale: API platform with partner auth, Stripe payments, webhook delivery, and usage analytics

complexity_level.rationale: Idempotent quote-bind flow, real-time rating engine, webhook delivery guarantees, and PCI payment integration

risk_level.rationale: Handles financial transactions, policy binding decisions, and partner API credentials for production insurance operations

data_sensitivity.rationale: Processes premium payments, stores bound policy financial records, and transmits coverage amounts

external_exposure.rationale: API accessible only to registered and credentialed SaaS platform partners, not general public

access_model.value: API key authentication for partner accounts; each partner scoped to their own policy and payment data; no direct freelancer authentication to the API
access_model.rationale: B2B partner model; partners manage their own user authentication

feature_scope.value: Coverage quote with real-time rating, policy binding with payment collection, certificate of insurance generation, policy cancellation and pro-rata refund, webhook subscriptions for lifecycle events, partner sandbox environment, usage analytics dashboard for partners
feature_scope.rationale: Core embedded insurance product lifecycle features

mvp_scope.value: Quote, bind, and certificate generation endpoints; Stripe payment integration; webhook delivery for policy.bound and payment.completed events; partner sandbox with test mode
mvp_scope.rationale: Covers the complete engagement-time binding flow for first partner integrations

security_baseline.value: API key + HMAC request signing for partner authentication, TLS 1.3 in transit, mTLS for partner webhook delivery, PCI-DSS SAQ-A compliance via Stripe tokenization, idempotency keys on all mutating endpoints
security_baseline.rationale: Financial API security standards with PCI delegated to Stripe for cardholder data

frontend_stack.value: None; API-only service
frontend_stack.rationale: No frontend deployed; partner platforms render their own UX

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin
backend_stack.rationale: Go provides the sub-millisecond routing overhead needed for the 500ms quote SLA; Gin for structured middleware

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for transactional records; Redis 7 for idempotency key deduplication and rate limiting
data_platform.rationale: Relational integrity for policy financial records; Redis for fast sub-millisecond idempotency checks

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run with min-instances set to avoid cold start latency
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless containers with min-instance warm pool satisfy the 500ms SLA without over-provisioning

privacy_retention_policy.value: Bound policy records retained 7 years per state insurance records retention requirements; payment tokens stored as Stripe customer IDs, never raw card data; webhook delivery logs retained 90 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Insurance record-keeping requirements and PCI cardholder data scoping

future_scope.value: Multi-line expansion to general liability and cyber liability, direct freelancer-facing portal, partner usage-based pricing tiers
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 growth roadmap

constraints.value: Quote endpoint must return a bindable premium in under 500ms at p99; no cold starts on the critical path; PCI scope limited to SAQ-A via Stripe tokenization
constraints.rationale: Latency SLA critical for in-flow partner UX; PCI scope reduction required to avoid full QSA assessment

observability_baseline.value: Grafana dashboards with Prometheus metrics; GCP Cloud Logging for structured request logs; PagerDuty alerts for p99 latency breaches and payment failure spikes
observability_baseline.rationale: Startup observability stack aligned with existing GCP infrastructure

execution_preference.value: Continuous deployment with automated tests and canary releases via Cloud Run traffic splitting
execution_preference.rationale: High deployment frequency needed for rapid partner integration feedback

compliance_context.value: PCI-DSS SAQ-A via Stripe Elements tokenization; state insurance producer licensing requirements for API distribution; NAIC model surplus lines and admitted filing requirements for policy issuance
compliance_context.rationale: Financial data sensitivity and insurance regulatory distribution requirements

project.goal: Embedded professional liability API enabling SaaS partners to quote and bind coverage at point of freelancer project engagement
project.target_users: SaaS platform engineering teams; indirect: freelancers and consultants
project.timeline: First partner integration in Q3

backend.framework: Gin HTTP framework with custom middleware stack
backend.api: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.1 spec published at /openapi.json
backend.async: Background goroutines for webhook delivery with retry queue in Redis

security.auth: API key + HMAC-SHA256 request signature verification
security.partner_isolation: Partner account ID enforced on all DB queries
security.pci: Stripe tokenization; no raw card data ever reaches the API
security.idempotency: Idempotency-Key header required on POST /quotes and POST /policies; Redis 24h deduplication

data.cache: Redis 7 for idempotency keys and rate limit counters

open_questions.cancellation_proration: Confirm pro-rata refund calculation method for mid-term cancellations

reasoner_reviews.product: The embedded binding flow is the core product moment: a freelancer accepts a project and coverage is offered inline by the partner platform within the same UX. Quote latency under 500ms at p99 is non-negotiable for this experience. The certificate of insurance generation is equally critical because many freelancer platforms require a COI as a project prerequisite.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go with Gin on Cloud Run with min_instances=2 is the right combination for the 500ms SLA. The rating engine computation must be in-process (not a remote call) to avoid network latency on the critical path. Redis idempotency deduplication at the handler level prevents duplicate policy bindings if a partner retries on a network timeout. The webhook delivery subsystem needs an at-least-once guarantee with exponential backoff, separate from the synchronous API response path.
reasoner_reviews.security: HMAC-SHA256 request signing on all partner API calls prevents replay attacks and key leakage; the timestamp in the signed payload must be validated within a 5-minute window. mTLS for outbound webhook delivery ensures the partner endpoint identity is verified. Stripe tokenization keeps all cardholder data out of the API scope, achieving SAQ-A with no stored card data.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Round 1 plan did not address the cold start latency risk. Cloud Run min_instances=2 must be explicitly configured; the plan must also specify that the rating engine tables are loaded into memory at startup, not queried per request, to achieve the 500ms budget. The webhook delivery retry logic was also underspecified in round 1.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 1 missing: no cold start mitigation strategy, no webhook retry architecture, no idempotency key expiry policy. These were raised as issues ISS-001, ISS-002, and ISS-003 in the round 1 audit.

backend.service_design: Single Go binary with Gin router; domain packages: quotes (rating engine, premium calculation), policies (binding, lifecycle state machine), payments (Stripe integration, refund computation), webhooks (event fanout, delivery queue), partners (account management, API key rotation), certificates (COI PDF generation)
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.1; Idempotency-Key header required on POST /v1/quotes and POST /v1/policies; all responses include X-Request-ID for tracing; partner_id extracted from API key and injected into request context for all downstream DB queries
backend.failure_handling: Rating engine panics recovered at middleware level with structured error response; Stripe webhook retries handled with idempotency; PostgreSQL connection pool circuit breaker via pgxpool health monitoring; Redis unavailability falls back to in-memory idempotency store for single-instance requests
backend.scaling_notes: Rating engine rate tables loaded into sync.Map at startup; no per-request DB reads for base premium computation; PostgreSQL pool max_conns=20 per Cloud Run instance; min_instances=2 configured to keep warm pool available at all times

security.auth_design: API key issued per partner account stored as SHA-256 hash in partner_accounts table; HMAC-SHA256 request signature computed over method+path+timestamp+body-hash; 5-minute timestamp tolerance window prevents replay attacks
security.authorization_model: Partner account ID scope enforced at repository layer for all policy and payment queries; no cross-partner data access possible even with valid API key
security.secrets_management: GCP Secret Manager for PostgreSQL DSN, Redis URL, Stripe secret key, HMAC signing seed; mounted as environment variables at Cloud Run service deployment via Secret Manager bindings
security.abuse_prevention: Per-partner rate limiting: 100 quote requests/minute, 20 bind requests/minute enforced via Redis sliding window counters; IP-based rate limiting at GCP Cloud Armor WAF for unauthenticated request floods

data.entities: partner_accounts (id, name, api_key_hash, webhook_url, webhook_signing_secret, sandbox_mode, created_at, status), coverage_requests (id, partner_id, idempotency_key, freelancer_ref, project_type, coverage_limit_usd, start_date, end_date, created_at), bound_policies (id, partner_id, coverage_request_id, policy_number, premium_usd, stripe_payment_intent_id, status ENUM active/cancelled/expired, certificate_s3_key, bound_at, expires_at, cancelled_at), premium_transactions (id, policy_id, partner_id, amount_usd, transaction_type ENUM charge/refund, stripe_charge_id, processed_at), webhook_subscriptions (id, partner_id, event_type, endpoint_url, active), webhook_deliveries (id, subscription_id, policy_id, event_type, payload JSONB, attempt_count, last_attempt_at, status ENUM pending/delivered/failed), certificate_of_insurance (id, policy_id, generated_at, pdf_gcs_path, download_token)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL for transactional records with connection pooling via pgxpool; Redis 7 on Memorystore for idempotency key TTL store and rate limit counters; GCS bucket for COI PDF storage with signed URL generation
data.consistency_notes: Policy binding uses a PostgreSQL transaction spanning coverage_requests status update, bound_policies insert, and premium_transactions insert; Stripe payment intent confirmation is the final commit gate

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Run service in us-central1 with min_instances=2 and max_instances=50; Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 in us-central1 with private IP via VPC connector; Memorystore Redis in same VPC; GCS bucket for COI PDFs; Cloud Armor WAF in front of Cloud Run HTTPS endpoint
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build: go vet, go test -race with 85% coverage gate, Docker build, push to Artifact Registry, Cloud Run deploy with 5% canary traffic split, automated smoke tests against canary, promote to 100% or rollback
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus metrics exported from Go process via /metrics endpoint scraped by Cloud Monitoring; Grafana dashboards: quote_latency_p99 histogram, bind_success_rate, webhook_delivery_success_rate, stripe_payment_failure_rate; GCP Cloud Logging structured JSON logs with partner_id and policy_id correlation fields
devops.rollback_strategy: Cloud Run traffic splitting allows instant rollback to previous revision; canary at 5% traffic for 10 minutes before full promotion; automated rollback triggered if quote_latency_p99 exceeds 400ms during canary window

latest_recommendations: Set Cloud Run min_instances=2 and document rating engine in-memory table loading at startup to satisfy 500ms quote SLA
Specify webhook delivery retry: exponential backoff starting at 30 seconds, max 8 attempts, dead-letter queue for permanently failed deliveries
Define idempotency key TTL as 24 hours; return cached response for duplicate key within TTL; return 409 Conflict after TTL expiry with original operation outcome

Clean Go domain package structure

Idempotency key TTL not defined

previous_plan.title: Embedded Professional Liability Insurance API
previous_plan.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 Gin REST API on GCP Cloud Run enabling SaaS partners to quote and bind short-term professional liability coverage for freelancers at point of engagement.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Go Gin service on Cloud Run backed by PostgreSQL 16 and Redis 7. Stripe handles payment tokenization. Webhooks delivered on policy lifecycle events.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Build CI deploys to Cloud Run on merge to main. No cold start mitigation specified. PostgreSQL on Cloud SQL.
previous_plan.workflows: Partner calls POST /v1/quotes with project details; rating engine returns premium; partner calls POST /v1/policies to bind; Stripe charge collected; COI generated.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a round 2 revision for a partner-facing professional liability insurance API. Three issues were raised in round 1: cold start latency risk on Cloud Run, missing webhook retry architecture, and undefined idempotency key TTL. All three are addressed in this revision. The architecture is fundamentally correct (Go/Gin, Cloud Run, Stripe for PCI) so this revision adds operational depth without changing the core design. The plan must include compliance_context because data_sensitivity is financial and capabilities includes payments.

fix_report.action_taken: Added explicit Cloud Run min_instances=2 configuration in deployment_and_operations and cost_and_scaling sections; added rating engine in-memory table loading at startup in the architecture_overview and system_components sections, explaining that base premium rate tables are loaded into a sync.Map at process startup and refreshed on a 1-hour background tick, eliminating per-request DB reads on the quote hot path
fix_report.expected_outcome: p99 quote latency achievable under 500ms with in-process rating engine and no cold starts; auditor can trace the latency budget from API entry to response

fix_report.action_taken: Added full webhook delivery retry architecture in system_components and workflows: initial delivery attempt synchronous after policy event, failures enqueued to Redis webhook_retry list, background worker retries with exponential backoff (30s, 1m, 2m, 4m, 8m, 15m, 30m, 60m), permanently failed deliveries moved to dead-letter queue with partner email notification, mTLS verification on all outbound webhook requests
fix_report.expected_outcome: Partner webhook reliability meets at-least-once delivery guarantee with clear dead-letter escalation; auditor can evaluate retry behavior and failure visibility

target_output.title: Embedded Professional Liability Insurance API - Revised Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 Gin REST API on GCP Cloud Run enabling SaaS platform partners to quote, bind, and manage short-term professional liability coverage for freelancers at the point of project engagement. Rating engine tables are loaded into memory at startup to achieve sub-500ms quote latency at p99. Stripe tokenization limits PCI scope to SAQ-A. HMAC-SHA256 signed requests authenticate partners. Webhook events deliver policy lifecycle notifications with an 8-attempt exponential backoff retry queue backed by Redis, with dead-letter escalation for permanently failed deliveries.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-process Go 1.22 binary running on Cloud Run with min_instances=2 to eliminate cold start latency on the critical quote path. At process startup, the rating engine loads professional liability base rate tables, project type factors, and coverage limit multipliers from PostgreSQL into a sync.Map; a background goroutine refreshes the map every 60 minutes without blocking in-flight requests. Gin middleware stack: API key extraction and HMAC-SHA256 signature verification, partner account resolution, request ID injection, rate limit check via Redis sliding window, and structured logging. The binding flow executes a PostgreSQL transaction: mark coverage_request as binding, insert bound_policy, insert premium_transaction, confirm Stripe payment intent; rollback on any failure. Webhook delivery runs in a separate goroutine pool reading from a Redis retry queue with exponential backoff. Cloud Armor WAF sits in front of the Cloud Run HTTPS endpoint.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22, Gin v1 HTTP framework, pgx/v5 PostgreSQL driver with pgxpool connection pooling, go-redis v9, Stripe Go SDK v76. Infrastructure: GCP Cloud Run (min_instances=2), Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 via private VPC, Memorystore Redis 7 via private VPC, GCS for COI PDF storage, GCP Secret Manager, Cloud Armor WAF. Build/Deploy: Cloud Build, Artifact Registry, golang-migrate for schema migrations. Observability: Prometheus client_golang, Grafana, GCP Cloud Logging. PDF generation: chromedp headless Chrome for COI rendering from HTML templates.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Quote endpoint: accepts project_type, coverage_limit_usd, coverage_duration_days, freelancer_state; returns bindable premium with breakdown and 10-minute quote expiry. Bind endpoint: accepts quote_id, idempotency_key, Stripe payment_method_id; initiates Stripe payment intent, confirms on success, records bound_policy, triggers COI generation. Certificate endpoint: GET /v1/policies/{id}/certificate returns signed GCS URL for COI PDF download. Cancellation endpoint: PATCH /v1/policies/{id}/cancel computes pro-rata refund, initiates Stripe refund, updates policy status. Webhook subscriptions: partners register endpoint URLs for event types policy.bound, payment.completed, policy.cancelled, payment.refunded. Partner sandbox: identical endpoints with test mode flag; Stripe test mode keys; no real policy records created. Usage analytics: GET /v1/analytics/usage returns partner-scoped metrics on quote volume, bind conversion rate, and payment failure rate.
target_output.system_components: Cloud Armor WAF: OWASP Core Rule Set in prevention mode, IP-based rate limiting for unauthenticated requests. Cloud Run service (Go binary): Gin router, HMAC auth middleware, rating engine sync.Map (loaded at startup, 60-minute background refresh), quote handler, bind handler, certificate handler, cancellation handler, webhook fanout goroutine pool. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (private IP): transactional records for partner_accounts, coverage_requests, bound_policies, premium_transactions, webhook_deliveries. Memorystore Redis 7: idempotency key store (24h TTL), rate limit sliding window counters, webhook retry queue (sorted set by next_attempt_at), webhook dead-letter list. GCS bucket: COI PDFs with signed URL generation (1-hour expiry). Stripe API: payment intent creation, confirmation, and refund processing; all cardholder data handled server-side by Stripe, never touching the Go process. Cloud Build canary pipeline: 5% traffic split on new revision, automated p99 latency check, promote or rollback.
target_output.workflows: Quote Flow: SaaS partner sends POST /v1/quotes with Idempotency-Key, project_type ENUM consulting/software/design, coverage_limit_usd, freelancer_state; Gin middleware verifies HMAC-SHA256 signature over method+path+unix_timestamp+body_sha256; rating engine reads base_rate from sync.Map, applies project_type_factor and state_factor, computes annual_premium scaled to coverage_duration_days, returns quote JSON with premium_usd, quote_id, expires_at; total latency budget: 2ms auth + 1ms rating + 5ms DB insert + network = well under 500ms. Bind and Payment Flow: Partner sends POST /v1/policies with quote_id and Stripe payment_method_id; idempotency key checked against Redis (return cached response if duplicate); PostgreSQL transaction opens; Stripe payment intent created and confirmed; bound_policy record inserted with stripe_payment_intent_id; premium_transaction recorded; transaction committed; COI generation job enqueued to background goroutine; webhook events policy.bound and payment.completed enqueued to Redis webhook_retry queue for delivery. Webhook Delivery: Background worker polls Redis sorted set ordered by next_attempt_at; pops due deliveries; makes HTTPS POST to partner endpoint with mTLS verification; on 2xx marks delivery as delivered; on failure increments attempt_count and reschedules at next_attempt_at = now + 30s*2^(attempt_count-1) up to 8 attempts; after 8 failures moves to dead-letter list and sends partner notification email via SendGrid.
target_output.data_model: partner_accounts (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, api_key_hash CHAR(64), webhook_signing_secret VARCHAR, sandbox_mode BOOLEAN, status ENUM active/suspended, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). coverage_requests (id UUID PK, partner_id UUID FK, idempotency_key VARCHAR UNIQUE, freelancer_ref VARCHAR, project_type ENUM consulting/software/design/other, coverage_limit_usd DECIMAL, coverage_duration_days INT, freelancer_state CHAR(2), premium_usd DECIMAL, status ENUM pending/binding/bound/expired, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ). bound_policies (id UUID PK, partner_id UUID FK, coverage_request_id UUID FK UNIQUE, policy_number VARCHAR UNIQUE, premium_usd DECIMAL, stripe_payment_intent_id VARCHAR, status ENUM active/cancelled/expired, certificate_gcs_path VARCHAR, bound_at TIMESTAMPTZ, expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ, cancelled_at TIMESTAMPTZ). premium_transactions (id UUID PK, policy_id UUID FK, partner_id UUID FK, amount_usd DECIMAL, transaction_type ENUM charge/refund, stripe_charge_id VARCHAR, processed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). webhook_deliveries (id UUID PK, partner_id UUID FK, policy_id UUID FK, event_type VARCHAR, payload JSONB, attempt_count SMALLINT, next_attempt_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM pending/delivered/failed, last_error TEXT). certificate_of_insurance (id UUID PK, policy_id UUID FK UNIQUE, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, pdf_gcs_path VARCHAR, download_token VARCHAR).
target_output.api_design: All endpoints under /v1, OpenAPI 3.1 at /openapi.json. POST /v1/quotes (Idempotency-Key required): request {project_type, coverage_limit_usd, coverage_duration_days, freelancer_state}; response {quote_id, premium_usd, premium_breakdown, expires_at}. POST /v1/policies (Idempotency-Key required): request {quote_id, payment_method_id, freelancer_name, freelancer_email}; response {policy_id, policy_number, premium_usd, status, certificate_url}. GET /v1/policies/{id}: response {policy details, status, certificate_url}. GET /v1/policies/{id}/certificate: 302 redirect to signed GCS URL (1-hour expiry). PATCH /v1/policies/{id}/cancel: response {refund_amount_usd, effective_date}. POST /v1/webhooks (register subscription): request {event_type, endpoint_url}. GET /v1/analytics/usage?from=&to=: response {quote_count, bind_count, bind_conversion_rate, payment_failure_count}. Errors: RFC 7807 Problem Details with partner-visible error_code and detail fields. All responses include X-Request-ID.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Partner authentication: API key transmitted in X-Api-Key header; HMAC-SHA256 computed over HTTP-method + URL-path + unix-timestamp + SHA256(request-body), transmitted in X-Signature header; 5-minute timestamp window validated server-side to prevent replay attacks. PCI-DSS SAQ-A: Stripe.js tokenizes cardholder data client-side within partner platforms; only Stripe payment_method_id reaches the API; no PAN, CVV, or expiry stored anywhere. mTLS for outbound webhook delivery: partner registers webhook endpoint with a TLS certificate fingerprint; Go http.Client verifies fingerprint on every delivery attempt. Cloud Armor WAF blocks OWASP Top 10 patterns and rate-limits unauthenticated sources. PostgreSQL: partner_id scoping enforced at repository layer on all queries; no cross-partner data access. GCP Secret Manager for all credentials with 90-day rotation. NAIC producer licensing: API distribution model reviewed by legal per state; admitted filing compliance tracked in partner onboarding records.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Build pipeline: go vet, staticcheck, go test -race -coverprofile (85% coverage gate), Docker build, push to Artifact Registry, Cloud Run deploy with --no-traffic flag, run smoke test suite against new revision, update traffic to 5% canary, monitor quote_latency_p99 and error_rate for 10 minutes, promote to 100% if healthy or rollback to previous revision. Cloud Run service configuration: min_instances=2 (maintains warm pool eliminating cold starts), max_instances=50, concurrency=100, CPU-always-allocated=true, memory=512Mi. Cloud SQL: PostgreSQL 16 db-standard-4 instance, private IP via VPC connector, automated daily backups retained 30 days, point-in-time recovery enabled. Environments: sandbox (separate Cloud Run service, Stripe test mode, sandbox flag set on partner accounts, shared Cloud SQL with sandbox schema), production (dedicated project, private networking, Cloud Armor enabled). golang-migrate runs as Cloud Build step before deploy; no direct DB access from CI workers.
target_output.observability: Prometheus metrics exported from /metrics endpoint scraped by Cloud Monitoring: quote_request_duration_seconds histogram (p50, p95, p99 tracked), policy_bind_total counter by result (success/payment_failed/rating_error), webhook_delivery_attempts_total by status, stripe_api_call_duration_seconds histogram. Grafana dashboards: Quote SLA board (p99 latency vs 500ms threshold), Bind Funnel (quote to bind conversion), Webhook Health (delivery success rate and dead-letter queue depth), Payment Failure Rate. GCP Cloud Logging: structured JSON with partner_id, policy_id, quote_id, X-Request-ID on every log line for partner support queries. PagerDuty alerts: quote_latency_p99 above 400ms for 2 minutes, bind_error_rate above 5% for 5 minutes, webhook_dead_letter_depth above 10, Stripe API error rate above 1%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Run estimated $200-400/month for min_instances=2 with CPU-always-allocated covering baseline warm cost plus burst traffic. Cloud SQL db-standard-4 estimated $300/month. Memorystore Redis M1 estimated $80/month. Total infrastructure estimated $650-850/month at launch. Rating engine in-memory eliminates expensive DB reads on the quote path; each Cloud Run instance handles 100 concurrent quote requests without DB fan-out. Cloud Run scales to 50 instances under sustained load; PostgreSQL pgxpool max_conns=20 per instance means up to 1,000 total connections at max scale; use PgBouncer sidecar if connection pressure detected.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Quote and bind endpoints with in-memory rating engine, Stripe payment integration, COI PDF generation, HMAC partner auth, idempotency deduplication, webhook delivery for policy.bound and payment.completed with 8-attempt retry, partner sandbox environment, Cloud Build CI/CD pipeline with canary releases, Grafana SLA dashboards. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Policy cancellation with pro-rata Stripe refund, webhook subscriptions management endpoint, usage analytics endpoint, partner API key rotation UI, PagerDuty alert tuning based on production baseline. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Multi-line expansion to general liability, partner developer portal with sandbox self-serve registration, rate table versioning for state-specific filing updates.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go 1.22 with modules; go vet and staticcheck enforced in CI with zero warnings. Table-driven unit tests for rating engine covering all project_type and state combinations. Integration tests against Docker Compose (PostgreSQL + Redis) for bind flow and idempotency. 85% code coverage gate in Cloud Build. All PostgreSQL queries use parameterized pgx statements; no string interpolation in SQL. Idempotency-Key validation: UUID v4 format enforced at handler layer. Partner-facing error responses use RFC 7807 Problem Details with internal error_code enum for partner support. OpenAPI 3.1 spec generated from code annotations via swaggo/swag; spec accuracy enforced by contract tests in CI.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Stripe API latency spike pushes bind endpoint above acceptable p99 during payment confirmation. Mitigation: Stripe payment intent pre-created at quote time and confirmed at bind, reducing bind-time Stripe call to a single confirm request; Stripe p99 is typically under 200ms. Risk: rating engine sync.Map grows stale if a state rate change is filed and the 60-minute background refresh has not yet run. Mitigation: provide a POST /admin/rating-engine/reload endpoint callable by ops to force immediate refresh without redeployment; state rate changes are rare and typically have a 30-day effective date. Risk: Redis Memorystore unavailability blocks idempotency key checks, potentially allowing duplicate binds during a Redis outage. Mitigation: fall back to an in-memory LRU cache for idempotency deduplication within a single instance during Redis downtime; accept that cross-instance deduplication is degraded during the outage window. Tradeoff: Cloud Run over GKE accepted for operational simplicity at this scale; revisit if concurrent instance count consistently exceeds 20.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Pro-rata refund calculation confirmed as daily_premium * remaining_days, rounded to nearest cent, with a 30-day minimum earned premium. Cold start risk resolved by min_instances=2 with CPU-always-allocated. Idempotency key TTL set to 24 hours matching Stripe's idempotency window. mTLS webhook verification selected over HMAC webhook signatures to provide mutual authentication rather than one-way integrity check.

metadata.notes: Embedded professional liability API revision addressing cold start latency mitigation and webhook retry architecture gaps from round 1 audit.
